The Minster of Harmony ensures the pack is content. A happy pack does not question its Leader and obeys, ensuring harmony. She serves as the Leader’s head communicator, councilor, propaganda agent, and logistical expert. This role is almost always filled by a Ragabash or a Galliard. 

- **Minister of Peace** 

The Minster of Peace is the instrument of the Leader’s will within the pack. In this example of “thug politics,” the Minister of Peace is the Leader’s physical enforcer during times of battle, and she the one who quells any verbal dissent. She might be a powerful Ahroun or a terrifying Theurge, ready to burn any doubters with fire. 

## Theocratic Packs 

Garou live with one foot in the Material Realm and the other in the realm of spirits. Some werewolves have come to believe that traditional leadership has failed, and that the physical world has corrupted the spirit of the Garou. To find balance, werewolves must return to the spiritual realms and reconnect with Gaia. The wisdom of spirits and the mysteries of the universe are the key to defeating the Wyrm. Individuals who ascribe to these philosophies frequently come together to form Theocratic packs. 

Theocratic packs dedicate themselves to learning the spirits’ will and being their agents in the material world. They explore the Aetherial Realms seeking answers and hidden weaknesses in their enemies. Some dare to wander near the Abyss, eliminating spiritual strongholds of the Wyrm. 

Theurges are especially attracted to Theocratic packs, finding solace in serving the spirits and acting on their behalf in the physical realm. However, other auspices are also attracted to this style of organization. Any Garou who feels a kinship with the spirits and a yearning for a deeper purpose for their existence might feel drawn to a Theocratic pack. 

These packs are not considered heretical by the Garou Nation—yet. Many werewolves consider them crazy, as they have left the Material Realm for too long and are divorced from the real problems that face Gaia. Theocratic packs, especially after long exploratory missions, often spend time walking the physical world, seeking out experiences within it to refresh themselves. This practice keeps them from turning their perspective completely towards the spiritual world, so that on their next mission they do not vanish into the Umbra forever. 

## **Roles that Garou in Theocratic packs assume are as follows:** 

- **Voice of the Spirits** 

The Voice of the Spirits acts as the default Pack Leader, but she really is more akin to its most-respected teacher. The official duty of the Voice of the Spirits is to serve as the mouthpiece for messages from spirits. The Voice also interprets the spirits’ will when their orders are unclear or when the pack needs to glean additional details from the information. This position is almost always held by the highest-Rank Theurge in the pack by default. The Voice keeps the position for life until another Theurge successfully challenges her in a craftsman’s challenge, or the spirits themselves convince her to willingly step down. Theocratic packs believe the spirits will choose the rightful leader. 

- **Chief Augur** 

The second-in-command of the pack serves as an aide-de-camp for the Voice of the Spirits, but her real duties involve noting and recording all prophecies and commands from spirits. The Voice of the Spirits must live in the now, and so the Chief Augur must focus on the future and record the signs the spirits have chosen to place in the pack’s path. 

- **Keeper of the Mysteries** 

The Keeper of the Mysteries serves as a historian, seeing to the education and intellectual needs of her pack. She often helps her packmates commune with spirits and heals any rifts they might feel with Gaia. The Keeper of the Mysteries helps supervise raising any Cubs who live among the pack and listens to the pack’s Betas when they feel they cannot approach the Voice of the Spirits. 

## Sample Packs 

The following packs serve as examples to give players and Storytellers ideas about the kinds of packs players can create, or for Storytellers to use as NPCs in their chronicles. 

## The Unbroken Vanguard 

Mother Gaia’s ecosystem is on life support. The great Canadian North represents one of the largest unspoiled strongholds of the Wyld in North America, despite climate change and attempts by oil companies to drill or build a pipeline. This sacred land represents one of the last Pure Lands keeping the world alive. The Unbroken Vanguard defends this realm and the Garou strongholds there against incursions from the Wyrm, be they corporate fracking, 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Thunderwyrms, or marauding packs of Warwolves. Its members have sworn to defend this land with their lives. Their motto is: _Our oaths unbroken now and forever._ 

The Unbroken Vanguard is a thriving Generational pack composed of three generations of Garou who love the land deeply. Many of them have patrolled their woods and mountains since they were Cubs, and each would be content to die out in the wilds. They have risked life and limb together, and they have even stood beside each other as they married. They claimed territory near Whistler Mountain and set up homes to raise their families together. On the battlefield, they are fierce. Their word is their bond, and the etiquette of hearth and home are closest to their hearts as they follow the Lady Snow Queen. Just this winter, the fourth-generation Cub was born, the great-grandson of John McAllister. The cycle continues. 

- **Classification:** Motif 

- **Life Cycle:** Generational 

- **Organizational Structure:** Wyld Pack 

- **Spirit Totem:** Snow Queen 

- **Purpose:** To protect the frozen North from the Wyrm

## Forged in Steel 

Forged in Steel is a pack created for the sole purpose of defeating the Gary Hive of Indiana. Its members excel at adapting to any situation presented by the Gary Hive, the city of Gary, the Wyrm, or the Weaver. Each moon brings different tasks and missions in the fight against the Gary Hive and creates the need for adaptability. Their motto is simple: _Adapt above all else._ 

Their founder, Dire “Skin of Stone,” leads the fight against the Gary Hive, the largest scar on the face of Gaia, with Forged in Steel close behind him. The fight against the hive is tenuous and requires a master tactician and a pack as versatile as the hive they seek to destroy. Dire “Skin of Stone” understands that a traditional pack would not meet the needs of the nation in this task. His quick rise among the Get of Fenris and the nation gained him great favor among the nation’s remaining leadership, allowing Forged in Steel to become a beacon of light in the darkness of the Age of Apocalypse. 

Forged in Steel sought out Chimera to guide it in its cause and purpose, allowing the pack to be of clear mind in their fight. Always seeking enlightenment and forethought, each Prime Auspice takes leadership as a given moment demands. As the pack continues to grow, the roles of the Prime Auspices become more prevalent and needed. 

The pack includes notable werewolves from across the nation, spanning all tribes and factions. Any attempt the hive makes to lash out at the nation or the Wyld are quickly routed by Forged in Steel. Each member of the pack exemplifies her auspice and excels at her auspicial duties. 

- **Classification:** Destiny 

- **Life Cycle:** Wayfarers 

- **Organizational Structure:** Collective 

- **Spirit Totem:** Chimera 

- **Purpose:** Protect the nation from the Gary Hive

## Horizon 

Horizon pack was forged from two packs during the Battle of the Ural Mountains, when it protected the Sept of the Crescent Moon with its members’ very lives. These two forceful packs were opposed in philosophy in nearly every way—one with strong ties to the sun while the other was equally connected to the moon. Despite the seemingly opposed loyalties between the two packs, they shared a common bond of spilled blood and sacrifice. 

Originally the unified pack was democratic, seeking to reach unity among Garou everywhere and to ensure that all members of the nation focused on external threats. However hopeful they were in those first few years, times changed and the world shifted. Caerns fell and septs were slaughtered. The Garou of Horizon knew that they had to become more resolute if they were to enact a positive change in the nation and on the world. And so they restructured the pack into its current form. 

As it exists now, Horizon embodies nearly pure aggression and terrorizes any who get in its way. This behavior still satisfies the pack’s totem, the Eclipse, which is tempered with both a dedication to Helios’ uncompromising nature and Luna’s gift of Rage. Horizon’s members each hold enough sway over their respective tribes that if a werewolf wishes to deal with high-minded tribal matters, she will likely encounter a member of this pack. 

Horizon’s current home is the Sept of the Crescent Moon, where its leader rules as Lord. Its members travel wherever necessary to enact their vision for the Garou Nation and to protect Gaia from threats. 

- **Classification:** Motif 

- **Life Cycle:** Wayfarers 

- **Organizational Structure:** Authoritarian 

- **Spirit Totem:** Eclipse 

- **Purpose:** To lead the nation under a unified banner of Helios and Luna

- (Leader) Sabine “Silver Swift” LaCoix, Homid, Ahroun, Silver Fang, Elder 

- (Minister of Harmony) Chad “Broken Halo” Sterling, Homid, Galliard, Child of Gaia, Elder 

- (Minister of Peace) Feast of Ashes “Walks Helios’ Path,” Lupus, Ahroun, Get of Fenris, Athro

- Alex “Sasha” Roberts, “Shadow’s Mercy,” Homid, Philodox, Shadow Lord, Athro
