## Rites 

In the Age of Myth, when the worlds of spirit and material were just beginning separate from each other, the Garou wished to solidify their relationship with their spirit allies. Forging pacts of alliance and friendship, the spirits granted the shapeshifters the secrets of gifts. In return, the Changing Breeds developed _rites_ to call upon this revered partnership. Ceremonial in nature, the core of any ritual resides in invoking these ancient pacts, empowering the ritual to enact an effect. A vital part of Garou interactions with spirits, rites are just one of many ways the Garou honor those bonds made at the dawn of time. Belief, concentration, and spiritual invocation are the three core components required of any ritual. 

Each ritual cast affirms the bond Garou share with spirits and results in strengthening of the spiritual half of the Garou. These rituals come in many variations depending on the Garou performing it, as well as on the type of rite. 

The rituals employed by the Garou are broken down into five primary types: Caern Rites, Mystic Rites, Punishment Rites, Renown Rites, and Seasonal Rites. While there are other minor rites Garou often perform, such as thanking spirits of prey that were killed for food or howling in greeting to the sun or moon, these five primary ritual types are the most commonly taught and accepted. 

Rituals are empowered by the spiritual essence within a Garou, Fera, or kinfolk. They are handed down from a Master of the Rite to her students in an unbroken chain dating back to when the original spirit pacts were formed. While the actual details of the rituals can vary depending on the tribe, sept, or perceptions of the participants, the belief of the caster and those participating in the rite is a ritual’s most important component. In order to successfully cast a ritual, the caster must possess the Rites background with 1 dot corresponding to the type of rite she wishes to perform. Assisting participants need not have knowledge of how to cast a rite in order to be involved. For more information on the Rites background, see **Chapter Four: Character** 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
## **The Price of Failure** 

Failing to cast a Rite is seen as a sign that the caster is somehow out of alignment with the spirits. This isn’t considered formally scandalous enough to warrant the loss of Renown, but is quite embarrassing. Storytellers may assign 1 to 2 Seethe traits, depending on the number of Garou that witnessed the mystical blunder. Afterwards, the caster must wait 15 minutes before attempting to performing the ritual once again. 

A failure to complete a ritual is always considered a bad omen or warning from the spirits. However, if a Punishment Rite fails, then the intended target may use that time to argue for mercy or proclaim that the spirits have found her innocent. Some silver-tongued Garou have managed to escape harsh punishments via this defense. 

**
## **Creation, Step Eight: Assign Additional Backgrounds, Background Descriptions, Rites** , page 199. 

## **System** 

Performing a ritual requires that the caster possess the Rites background and has knowledge of the appropriate category of ritual. Each ritual takes at least five minutes to perform, though casters may increase the time if they wish to add large groups of participants or additional flourishes. While a rite can be enacted in combat, if the caster receives any damage or uses an action to target another combatant, the rite immediately fails. A caster must have a firm belief in the success of the ritual and the purpose behind it in order to properly empower a rite. 

Casting a rite requires a static challenge with a difficulty rating of 10. For each additional participant who has the Rites background and has chosen rites of the same category, the difficulty lowers by 1. Characters who do not meet this requirement may still participate in the rite, but do not lower the difficulty to cast it. In addition to the standard methods of retesting a static challenge, the caster may expend a trait of Wisdom Renown to retest the casting of a Rite, paying chiminage to the spirits to help her through the process. 

## Caern Rites 

_Caern rites_ are rituals that focus on the sacred nature of a caern itself. From the opening of Moon Bridges to creating a new caern by planting a shard seed, these rituals are vital to the successful growth and maintenance of a caern. 

## **System** 

Caern rites require a successful Mental attribute + Occult skill challenge to perform. 

## Rite of Badger’s Burrow 

This ritual allows you to partially merge your spirit with that of the sept totem in order to share in its knowledge of the land. The intimate bond a totem shares with its caern via the gnostic energy in the land gives the totem unmatched insight into the area. However, the mind of a spirit is alien to Garou, so only a limited number of questions can be answered before the ritual automatically ends to preserve the caster’s sanity. 

Performances of the Rite of Badger’s Burrow happen at the bawn. Through careful meditation and mystic chanting, you form a close attunement with the mind of the totem. Various cleansing rituals or gifts are best used in preparation for such a close bond with a powerful totem spirit. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action asking the sept totem to grace you with its attention. After succeeding in the required challenge, you may ask and receive an answer to one of the following questions: 

- Are there any sources of Wyrm Taint within the boundaries of the bawn? 

- Has any shapeshifter who hasn’t undergone the Rite of Acceptance crossed the boundaries of the sept territory in the last month? 

- Are there any spirits within the boundary of the bawn who feel negatively towards recognized members of the sept? 

For each additional point of Gnosis that the ritual caster spends during the ritual, you may ask and receive an answer to another question. This rite can only be cast once per game session. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Rite of Furrowed Earth 

This ritual is the final step in the long process of creating a shard caern. Upon finding an area receptive to the implantation of a shard seed, you must prepare the land for the mystical connection. Invocations and cleansing gifts purify the area, welcoming the seed and hastening its bond with those sworn to protect the new caern. 

Once the site has been chosen, it must be marked with items significant to the four elemental lords and ladies. When each element has been invoked and the spirits called to witness, the Rite of Furrowed Earth is performed, opening the land to receive the shard seed. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use four standard actions to beg the attention of the elemental powers. A successful challenge opens the land for the implantation of the shard seed and primes the gnostic energy in the land to bond with it. 

When completed, the Rite of Furrowed Earth connects the shard seed to the land’s ambient gnostic energy, forming a true shard caern. This rite stabilizes the umbral reflection of the area and begins to allow those bound to the sept to regain Gnosis while they are within the caern heart. Such is the powerful life energy flowing through this ritual that any participants find themselves fully healed at the completion of the rite. 

This ritual may also be cast in reverse, minimizing the damage done to a shard seed when it is removed from the earth to which it was attuned. While this ritual does not negate all the damage done to the seed, performing it lessens the impact. For more information on removing a shard seed, see **Chapter Twelve: Caerns** . 

## Rite of Opened Bridge 

With the onslaught of umbral storms destroying many of the permanent Moon Bridges that once connected caerns throughout the world, this ritual offers a small reminder of those once-myriad paths through the spirit world. 

This ritual requires access to a Pathstone, a mystical item connected to a caern and its totem spirit, which allows the caster to open the bridge between worlds. Using the Pathstone, you may invoke the aid of Luna and her servants, the Lunes, to once more open the door to the hidden pathways of the Umbra, creating a Moon Bridge. For more information on using Moon Bridges, see **Chapter Twelve: Caerns** . 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use 2 standard actions to invoke the aid of Luna and her servants. A successful challenge opens a Moon Bridge. This rite may be used in two ways. If performed at a prime caern, the caster may choose any shard caern to which that caern is linked and open a bridge to that location. If the ritual is performed at a shard caern, it opens a bridge to the prime caern where the caern’s shard seed was originally grown. Each traveler using the Moon Bridge must spend 1 point of Gnosis in order to power the transition. The caster may choose to pay this price for any number of travelers in addition to the initial cost of the ritual. 

With the return of umbral storms, travel through the Umbra has become dangerous and sometimes deadly. The use of a Moon Bridge provides safe and instantaneous travel between caerns. However, it must be used with care, as the unexpected opening of a Moon Bridge is often met with violent force. At the very least, the one who opened such a Moon Bridge could find herself investigated or punished for failing to warn the destination of incoming guests. 

## Rite of the Questing Stone 

Shard seeds are powerful and rare, which means they are highly coveted. The ability to move a shard seed puts them in danger of theft, and this ritual was developed to combat that threat. 

This rite requires some form of compass. This item can be an actual compass or something as simple as a rock tied to a string. When cast successfully, the compass pulls towards the direction of a specific shard seed. Depending on the material used, this guidance can be as simple as a needle pointing in the direction of the shard seed instead of north or a rock pulling in that direction when held by the string. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to prepare the compass. If you succeed in your challenge, this ritual allows you to feel pulled in the direction of the shard seed that belongs to your specific sept for the next hour. While in most cases this rite is used when a shard seed is stolen or goes missing, it can also be used by Garou in unfamiliar territory to find their way back to their home caern. 

No manner of mundane or supernatural obfuscation can impede the pull of the compass towards the shard seed. 

## Rite of the Shrouded Glen 

Sometimes, the best defense is never being seen in the first place. This ritual renders the umbral reflection of a shard 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

caern invisible both to the naked eye of any not bound to it, as well as to the ravaging damage of umbral storms. While potent, this layer of protection is draining, and must be constantly maintained in order to ensure that the caern itself does not become vulnerable at the worst possible time. For more information on shard caerns and their umbral reflections, see **Chapter Twelve: Caerns** . 

In order to perform this ritual successfully, the caster must walk the boundaries of the bawn in the Umbra, marking the territory to be encompassed. You can use methods as varied as scent marking to setting sentry spirits along the boundary, but you must erect some identification. 

kinfolk. When successfully cast, it bonds a kin-fetch to the child, a spirit of protection whose role is to watch over the child and alert the sept when it undergoes the First Change. If cast on a child destined to be kinfolk, it instead grants a minor blessing of health and protection that lasts for one year. While the presence of a kin-fetch is beneficial to a newly changed Garou, it is as subject to umbral storms and other predators as any other spirit. In the Age of Apocalypse, kin-fetch that fulfill their full term are far too rare. 

When the target child is born, you must anoint her with pure water mixed with herbs for health and vitality. While chanting invocations for endurance and stamina, you cast the ritual imploring the spirits to divine the child’s future. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use five standard actions to identify the caern’s territorial boundaries. When this ritual is successfully performed, the umbral reflection of the shard seed and its bawn are rendered invisible for the next month. This rite also serves to shelter the area from the effects of umbral storms. Vital to the defense and safety of a shard seed, failing to upkeep this ritual can subject the shard seed to the gnostic damage that umbral storms inflict, leading to damaging or even possibly destroying an unprotected shard seed. 

While under the effects of this ritual, the sept’s bawn is invisible in the Umbra. This protection also solidifies the attachment of the shard seed, rendering it immune to any attempt to perform the Rite of Furrowed Earth on the shard seed. It also penalizes any attempt to pierce the supernatural concealment through a wild card penalty equal to the number of dots you possess of the Occult skill. While this rite is in effect, spirits within the boundary of the bawn are also immune to any damage caused by umbral storms. 

## Mystic Rites 

_Mystic rites_ use formal invocations and movements to call for direct intervention by the spirits. These rituals are used for everything from identifying the difference between kinfolk or Garou at birth to bonding a pack to its totem spirit. Mystic rituals rely heavily on the caster’s ability to focus and concentrate, directing the often-capricious spirits to the required task. 

## **System** 

Mystic rites require a successful Mental attribute + Occult skill challenge to perform. 

## Baptism of Fire 

Cast during the birth of a child, this ritual calls on the spirits to discern whether the baby is destined to become Garou or 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to call a kin-fetch to attend you. If you succeed in the rite’s challenge, the spirit arrives and divines whether the child will remain kin or become Garou. 

Successfully performing this ritual on a child destined to change binds a rating 1 Stock NPC spirit known as a kinfetch to her. This spirit remains with the child until she undergoes her First Change. It then races to inform the rite’s caster of this event. If the one who originally cast the ritual is dead, the spirit instead goes to the closest Sept Leader and informs her. If cast on a kinfolk, this rite confers a minor blessing of health, helping ensure a healthy kin during their most vulnerable first year. 

## Rite of Accord 

Rites of Accord allow a caster to transfer her own gnostic energy to a spirit. The rite is most often used as chiminage to thank a spirit for its efforts on the behalf of a Garou. It can also be used to give Gnosis to a spirit to improve its attitude towards the caster before asking for a service. Alternatively, this ritual may be used to channel gnostic energy to a shard seed, helping prevent damage to the fragile item during transit or when under attack. 

An almost completely internal ritual, the most common elements of this rite’s enactment include chanting in the High Tongue to invoke the good will of the spirits or meditating to focus gnostic energy to be given to the target. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action gathering the attention of a spirit. Successfully casting this rite allows you to spend an additional Gnosis, which is directly transferred to the spirit target of the ritual. Doing so improves the spirit’s attitude towards you. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

This ritual may also be used to channel gnostic energy directly to a shard seed. When cast in this way, you can spend any number of additional points of Gnosis beyond the initial 1 point required to perform the rite, then transfer those points directly to the target shard seed. Any participants joining you in the ritual may do so as well. 

When cast on a shard seed, each point of Gnosis spent in this way prevents 2 levels of damage that would otherwise be dealt to the shard seed. This protection applies to physical damage as well as any gnostic damage suffered from umbral storms or while removing the shard seed from the earth to move it. 

## Rite of Awakening 

The Rite of Awakening calls spirits to reinforce the Gauntlet and restore an area of Broken Lands back to stability, ensuring the Veil remains protected. The rite is dangerous, as it calls the attention of the Wyrm and its minions with an outpouring of gnostic energy. 

Performing this rite requires cleansing an area within a Broken Land with gifts. The caster must spend the entirety of the rite chanting in the High Tongue to beseech spirits to repair the shredded Gauntlet. Any interruption before the completion of the ritual causes it to immediately fail, and the caster must begin again if she wishes to repair the Gauntlet. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to cleanse the target area. If you succeed in the ritual challenge, you must begin to chant for the next five minutes. During this time, any interruption causes the rite to fail. 

Beginning this ritual acts like a beacon to the Wyrm, and any creature within one mile that has Wyrm affinity immediately realizes this rite has begun, knows where the rite is happening, and will attempt to stop it. If you manage to succeed in spite of this possible interference, the Gauntlet in the area is restored and the Broken Land is repaired, returning the area to a periphery. 

## Rite of Contrition 

There comes a time when even the most prideful must apologize. This ritual provides a formal way to apologize to someone who the caster has wronged, be it Garou or spirit. The target of the rite must stay within three steps of the caster for its duration, or the rite automatically fails. If the recipient moves away, this is usually a sign that she does not accept the caster’s apology. Continuing to hold a grudge against someone who has successfully performed this rite is 

considered both dishonorable and a grave sign of disrespect. This rite cannot be cast if the one attempting it does not have the required Gnosis to sacrifice. 

When performing this rite while within three steps of her target, the caster must recount the affronts she has committed for which she is apologizing. This act is often accompanied by some gesture of respect or submission, such as a bowing, inclining her head, or even exposing her belly. Having done so, the caster can then perform the rest of the ritual. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions listing the offenses to be forgiven. Success on the Mystic rite challenge transfers the Gnosis you spent to enact it to the target of the ritual. For the next month, the caster automatically relents to any Staredown challenge instigated by the target of this ritual. 

Once this rite has been successfully cast, the target is compelled to honor the apology and cannot hold a grudge against the caster for the actions named within the ritual. Further affronts committed by the caster may be responded to normally. 

## Rite of the Totem 

The Rite of the Totem is the mystic ritual that completes the formation of a pack by summoning and bonding a totem spirit to its members. Some totems are eager to bond with Garou, while others may require a service or promise made by those who would go into battle with them. Once the ritual itself concludes, the pack is considered whole and complete, more than ready to face the challenges ahead of it. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions calling out in supplication to the requested totem spirit. Success on the Mystic rite challenge bonds all of the participants in the ritual to the willing totem spirit or severs their bond to it, depending on which aspect of the rite the caster invokes. 

If cast to dissolve a pack, this ritual offers a spirit the ability to tell a pack member what service it may require to allow her to amicably leave the pack and join another. It also allows Garou to petition a new spirit to be their totem. Performing any required task ensures the positive attitude of the departing spirit, while failure to do so is sure to earn its ire. 

## Punishment Rites 

Covering everything from breaches of the Litany to offenses against the laws of a sept and one another, Punishment rites 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

are a formal condemnation of wrongdoing. These rituals are often performed publicly both to punish the offender and to remind witnesses of the rite’s power. Punishment rites are sacred rituals, and can only be performed at the command of a Sept Leader, Councilor, or High King without a trial. Once a trial has been completed, if the target Garou is found guilty, the Truthcatcher who presided over the trial may then declare a Punishment rite is warranted. For more information on when a punishment rite is warranted, see **The Sept: The Litany and the Sept, Crime and Punishment** , page 528. 

## **System** 

Punishment rites require a successful Social attribute + Leadership skill challenge. 

## Branding 

No matter how often they are punished for the same or even different crimes, some Garou never quite seem to learn their lesson. They carry the moniker _Coward_ , _Dishonorable_ , or _Reckless_ without ever learning the lessons that these Punishment rites are meant to teach. As such, this ritual was developed so that all other Garou become aware of the stain a criminal carries. No longer can she merely wait until everyone has forgotten; she must go forth and truly impress the Garou Nation with her willingness to change. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to draw the moniker being assigned onto the skin of the target. A successful Punishment rite challenge ensures that the mark becomes permanently etched to the target’s body, just as the moniker, lesser or greater, becomes permanently attached to her reputation. Only a majority vote of the tribal Councilors or an edict of the High King can remove it. This rite adds the moniker _Branded_ to a connected moniker associated with the target’s punishment. For example, a Garou suffering under Voice of the Jackal and labeled as _Dishonorable_ also becomes known as _Branded_ , in addition to _Dishonorable_ . 

This ritual can also be used to etch a permanent positive moniker on a Garou, at the order of a Sept Leader or tribal Councilor with the authority to assign the moniker being made permanent. 

## Ostracism 

Nothing is quite so frightening to one who relies on the bonds of pack, tribe, and sept as the idea of losing those ties and being alone. As such, this rite serves as an effective punishment. When this ritual is cast, the one performing 

it specifies a time limit: one day, two weeks, or one month. For the stated duration, the target is spiritually marked, and other Garou and spirits treat her as if she did not exist. Due to the sacred nature of rituals, it takes extreme danger for someone to disregard the stigma attached to the target, but in some cases it does happen. Ignoring the effect of the rite frivolously, however, could reasonably bring punishment on both the Garou who interacted improperly with the original target of the rite, and again on the target herself. 

Before the ritual is cast, the target is marked with the glyph for “Outcast.” This marking can be made using any nonpermanent substance, as the ritual keeps the mark like new for the duration of the punishment. As the caster marks her target, she proclaims aloud the crimes her target committed to warrant Ostracism. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to mark the target with the “Outcast” glyph and declare the duration of the ritual. A successful Punishment rite challenge ensures that the mark will not fade until the stated duration ends. The ritual makes the mark easily visible to any Garou, Fera, and spirits that are able to see the target. 

For as long as the glyph remains, the target marked is treated as a Ronin Garou, with all the penalties associated with that social class. 

## Satire Rite 

Some transgressions are proof that a Garou is unworthy of the Rank she currently holds. This Punishment rite rectifies this mistake by stripping the target of her current Rank, lowering her to her previous level. This ritual is only cast after the target has undergone a proper trial, or at the direct order of a Sept Leader. 

Witnesses encircle the target while the caster leads the group in publicly shaming the target Garou for the crimes she committed or the failures she allowed to occur. The witnesses are encouraged to join in, reminding the target of the failings that brought her to this place. It is a mark of honor for the punished Garou to endure her shame stoically while being humiliated. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions heaping mockery and scorn on the target. Succeeding on the Punishment rite challenge instantly strips the target of her current Rank, reducing her to the next-lowest Rank. Targets of this ritual also receive the lesser moniker _Dishonorable_ . 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Once this ritual has been cast on a target, the crimes that she committed to earn the punishment are never spoken of again. Enduring the humiliation required as part of the rite is penance enough, and only the most dishonorable would dare bring up the actions after the punishment has been completed. 

For more information on when to use the Satire Rite, see **The Sept: The Litany and the Sept, Crime and Punishment** , page 528. 

## Stone of Scorn 

Representing a level of punishment between Ostracism and the Satire Rite is the Stone of Scorn. As a constant reminder of the shameful action that warranted this punishment, this rite requires the caster to hang a rock around the neck of her target. This stone is engraved with the glyph for “Honor,” “Wisdom,” or “Glory,” with deep claw marks disfiguring it. 

Choose a stone and affix it to a necklace or other harness worn around the neck. While reciting the crimes of the transgressor, the caster paints or etches into the stone the glyph for “Glory,” “Honor,” or “Wisdom,” depending on the target’s crime. Then, the caster mars the mark and places the necklace or other harness around the neck of her target. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions painting a stone with the glyph for “Honor,” “Wisdom,” or “Glory,” marring the stone and placing it around your target’s neck. If you succeed on the Punishment rite challenge, you bind the stone to your target for the next month. No matter how hard she tries, the stone cannot be removed until the duration expires. While the target suffering under this ritual is within the boundaries of the sept’s territory, the stone is always visible to any Garou or Fera, glowing faintly through any amount of covering. 

The target receives one of the following monikers as chosen by the caster of the rite: _Coward_ , _Dishonorable_ , or _Reckless_ . 

## The Hunt 

The Rite of the Hunt is the final punishment a Garou can receive. Those involved with the rite encircle the target and ritually anoint their bodies with glyphs, marking themselves as hunters. Being chosen as one of the hunters is a high honor, and witnesses defer to them until the rite is complete. Once the hunters are prepared, the target is marked with the glyph for “prey,” and her bonds are released. While making these marks, the caster names the crimes committed to call down the ritual. As the target flees, the hunters chase down and kill the target. Death in 

this manner is seen as a form of atonement, allowing the target to be mourned and buried with honor. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use five standard actions marking yourself and the other participants in the rite with glyphs that denote them as hunters or the prey. If you succeed on the Punishment rite challenge, this ritual grants the hunters an unfailing instinct for the location of their prey for the duration of rite. Their only desire becomes to hunt down and kill the prey. This ritual lasts until the prey is dead or one month has passed, whichever is shorter. The target receives the moniker _Hunted_ . 

When the prey target dies, those marked with the hunter glyphs receive 1 trait of Fleeting Honor Renown for their participation in this sacred duty. Any stain of dishonor on the prey is wiped clean, so she may be mourned and remembered with honor. The Garou who lands the killing blow on the prey receives the moniker _Hero_ . 

Only a Sept Leader or the High King can call for a Rite of the Hunt to be performed. For more details on this ritual and when it may be used, see **The Sept: The Litany and the Sept, Crime and Punishment,** page 528. 

## Voice of the Jackal 

This punishment is performed on those whose words cannot be trusted, ensuring that any who hear her voice are aware of the target’s dishonesty. For the next month, all who hear the target’s high-pitched yipping voice realize the target is not someone to be trusted. 

In order to perform this rite, the caster needs a whistle, a bell, or some other way to produce a high-pitched sound. The target is made to announce her crime while the caster creates high-pitched tones throughout her recitation. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to create a high-pitched chime or whining sound. If you succeed on the Punishment rite challenge, you render the target’s voice high-pitched and squeaky while in Homid form and accented with hyena-like barks and yips when attempting to communicate in her Lupus form. 

While under the effects of this ritual, the target receives a wild card penalty equal to the number of dots she possesses of the Rank background, applicable to any Leadership challenges in which she particpates. The target gains one of the following monikers as chosen by the caster of the rite: _Coward_ , _Dishonorable_ , or _Reckless_ . 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Renown Rites 

_Renown rites_ serve to give praise to those whose actions merit it, to celebrate memorable moments in life, and to bring participants together to enforce the sense of community that keeps a sept healthy. 

## **System** 

Renown rites require a successful Social attribute + Performance skill challenge. 

## Gathering for the Departed 

Death is a way of life for Gaia’s children. Mourning and celebrating the way a fallen werewolf lived allows Garou to deal with the constant stream of losing loved ones in a never-ending life of battle. This ritual grants Garou a chance to say goodbye while celebrating the victories that the deceased achieved in life. 

In order to perform this ritual, the caster must obtain either the body of the fallen Garou or an item significant to her. A cleansing is performed on the body or item, and the other participants and mourners gather. Once all are in attendance, the caster begins to speak of the deceased werewolf’s deeds, inviting others to participate as she casts the ritual. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to begin telling a story of the deceased. If you succeed on the Renown rite challenge, the rite negates a  number of points of Harano equal to the number of persons participating in the ritual by sharing a story of the deceased. Only points of Harano that were or would be gained as a result of the death of a packmate, septmate, or loved one can be removed through this ritual. 

## Rite of Acceptance 

Joining a sept is a momentous occasion, as a Garou bonds with both the totem spirit overseeing the caern as well as the area’s defenders. This ritual marks the formal acceptance of a Garou or Fera into the community of a sept. 

The individual casting this ritual brings the prospective member to the bawn and beseeches the attention of the sept totem. When the totem pays heed to the request, the target is ritually purified and formally presented to the totem for acceptance. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to bring yourself to the attention of the sept totem. If you 

succeed on the Renown rite challenge, this ritual formally bonds the target to the sept and its totem. When the ritual is complete, all members of the sept feel the effect of another Garou’s addition to their number, and those who hold a position in the sept are spiritually notified regarding identity of the newest member. This recognition lasts until death or the target successfully joins another sept. 

When the ritual is complete, the identity of the newly bonded Garou, including her name, deed name, tribe, breed, and auspice are delivered by spiritual messenger to any who hold a position within the sept. Other bonded members of the sept who do not hold a position are aware another has been added to their ranks, but they must learn her identity on their own. 

## Rite of Accomplishment 

A werewolf rising in Rank is a moment of joy and celebration for her sept as well as herself. This ritual informs the whole community of an individual’s achievement, so they may celebrate the elevation of one of their own. It is the final step required to formally acknowledge a Garou’s rise in station. 

A Garou who has completed her Rank challenge is circled by witnesses, who offer congratulations and short tales of her successes. While that happens, the caster uses sacred oils to anoint her with the glyph of the Rank to which she has risen. 

This ritual can also be used when granting a positive moniker in reward for service to the sept or the Garou Nation. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions anointing the target with oil. If you succeed on the Renown rite challenge, this ritual sends a spiritual message to the sept’s totem, which announces to every bonded member of the sept the news that one of its members has risen in Rank. 

The target of this rite immediately regains enough Gnosis to bring her to her maximum, and it refreshes any Innate or Abiding Renown she may have spent during the game session. When used to grant a moniker, if you succeed on the rite challenge, this ritual sends information to the sept totem or tribal totems, informing the sept or the Garou Nation of the granting of such an honor. 

## Rite of Passage 

When a Cub has learned her lessons and is ready to become a full member of the nation, she must undergo her Rite of Passage. This ritual is usually performed with other Cubs, after they have completed a mission showing their worthiness. It is the capstone of the Cubs’ transition into 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

full sept membership, and it formally acknowledges the Cubs’ success. Cubs who go through this rite together often form the basis of a new pack. 

A Den Parent gathers the Cubs who are ready to accept the mission and gives them their task. Those involved must go and work together to accomplish the goal that has been set forth for them. When they return, the Den Parent judges whether those who have participated have succeeded in demonstrating their suitability to be Cliath of the nation. 

In the presence of the Den Parent, the caster anoints the new Cliath in sacred oil with the glyph for their new Rank. If a Den Parent is not available, the Sept Leader may stand in for this ritual. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action anointing the new Cliath with oils. If you succeed in the Renown rite challenge, this ritual marks the completion of the task and formally inducts the participants into the Rank of Cliath. 

Performance of this ritual grants the participants the Renown, privileges, and responsibilities that come with the Rank of Cliath. It also notifies the local spirits that there are new members of the nation who may form a new pack or join an existing one. Successfully performing this ritual removes the _Youngling_ moniker from the new Cliath. 

## Rite of Wounding 

Performed when a warrior returns bearing the scars of battle, this ritual ensures the reminder of the wound is never lost. Serving both as a teaching moment and a symbol of surviving a life-threatening battle, battle scars are proudly borne as reminders of the never-ending war. 

The caster must combine cleansed and purified water with small pinch of silver dust. Then, she pours the liquid over the site of the battle scar. This act ensures that while the damage may heal, the scar will forever remain with the Garou who bears it. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use two standard actions to combine the ingredients and pour the liquid. If you succeed in the Renown rite challenge, this ritual ensures that while the wound may heal, its scar will persist. 

Completion of this ritual revitalizes a wounded werewolf after a draining battle, restoring her Gnosis pool to maximum. 

## Seasonal Rites 

_Seasonal rites_ are deeply rooted in the well-being of the sept and its members. They echo the turning of the earth and the changing of Gaia’s seasons, drawing power and returning it to her in a continuous cycle. 

Seasonal rituals can only be active during their associated time periods, with the exception of the Lunar Celebration rite, which can be performed at any time without canceling the others. You can also only have one of the four equinox/ solstice rituals active at a time. For example, casting the Rite of Reawakening while the Rite of the Winter Wind is still active immediately ends the effects of the Rite of the Winter Wind. The participation of five individuals bound to the sept through the Rite of Acceptance is required for the successful casting of Seasonal rites. 

Repeatedly casting the same Seasonal ritual has no additional effect; bonuses do not increase or stack. 

## **System** 

Seasonal rites require a successful Social attribute + Performance skill challenge. 

## Lunar Celebration 

Harano is a true danger to Garou and Fera alike, and there is not always time for moot activities to alleviate that distress. This ritual was created to allow a pack to mitigate worry until they can fully participate in a moot. Calling on Luna and praising the roles for which they were born reminds werewolves of their sacred duties, keeping the sorrow at bay. 

The caster and all participants gather together under the light of the moon. They each speak aloud their plea to Luna for relief from the sadness growing within them. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use five standard actions to invoke Luna in each of her phases. If you succeed on a Seasonal rite challenge, this ritual removes a point of Harano from each participant for the next month. 

If a participant in the Lunar Celebration rite participates in a moot before the month is over, her removed point of Harano does not return. If a werewolf fails to participate in a moot before the allotted time, the removed point of Harano returns, and the target gains another point of Harano for her failure. You can only be under the effect of one Lunar Celebration rite at a time. 

Chapter eleven: the Garou nation 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Rite of Reawakening 

The Rite of Reawakening calls the land surrounding the sept to life once again. Participants share their gnostic energy with the land and the spirits around them to ensure growth and strength for all. This ritual may be cast any time from the day after the Winter Solstice until the day before the Summer Solstice. 

The caster gathers seeds or some other symbol of growth. Digging a small hole in the earth, she must bury the symbol while intoning a prayer for the safety and fertility of the land. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions planting the symbol. If you succeed on a Seasonal rite challenge, this ritual infuses the land with gnostic energy, awakening it from the long winter slumber. 

For the next three months, the stock NPC rating of any spiritual defenders of the sept raises by 1, to a maximum of 5. 

## Rite of the Great Hunt 

Summer is a time to celebrate fertility and virility. The Rite of the Great Hunt ensures the strength and bounty of the coming harvest and of the sept and its members. This ritual may be cast at any time from the day after the Vernal Equinox until the day before the Autumnal Equinox. 

The caster and any other participants shift to their Lupus forms and hunt down a prey animal. While giving thanks for its sacrifice, they share the bounty of the life they have taken. Every piece of the animal must be used in some way, even if only taken for later use. After this task is completed, the caster intones the ritual. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use your standard action to invoke this ritual over the body of the slain. If you succeed on a Seasonal rite challenge, this ritual imbues strength and protection into the sept and its shard seed. 

For the next three months, if the shard seed would lose its strength due to being moved or from lack of attention, the loss is negated. For more information on shard seeds, see **Chapter Twelve: Caerns** . 

## Rite of the Long Vigil 

Autumn is the time of harvest in preparation for the winter to come. The Rite of the Long Vigil reminds its participants 

of the coming chill and ensures the sept is ready. This ritual may be cast at any time from the day after the Summer Solstice until the day before the Winter Solstice. 

The caster and each participant must bring some item of comfort or food to be stored at the sept. Each item is cleansed and ritually purified before being added to storage. Once each item has undergone this blessing, the caster invokes this ritual over the location where they have been placed. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use three standard actions blessing the items involved. If you succeed on a Seasonal rite challenge, this ritual renders the sept territory a bastion against blights, disease, and sickness. 

For the next three months, those bound to the sept are immune to disease, toxins, or poison while present on the sept’s territory. This rite does not prevent the targets from contracting these effects outside of their territory, nor does it negate any pre-existing effects. 

## Rite of the Winter Wind 

Traditionally, winter was a time of attrition and death, a struggle to endure the cold months until the spring returned. The Rite of the Winter Wind was created to gift resilience and endurance to the participants, helping them thrive during harsh times. This ritual may be cast at any time from the day after the Autumnal Equinox until the day before the Vernal Equinox. 

The caster and each participant must stand vigil outdoors on the sept’s territory from dusk until the following dawn. They cannot eat, drink, or sleep during this time, or receive any creature comforts beyond their own fur. When dawn breaks the horizon, the ritual is cast with an invocation to the four winds. 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and use four standard actions to name the four winds. If you succeed on a Seasonal rite challenge, this ritual grants the participants the fortitude to endure the harshest of climates. 

For the next three months, those bound to the sept are not subject to any negative effects due to climate, from the coldest arctic tundra to the hottest of deserts. This rite does not prevent damage from cold or fire attacks, but instead it mitigates only the effects of such environments. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Secrets Recovered 

“A giant. Seriously? I know you said this was going to be a challenge, but not only do you send me to fight a giant, but you can’t even be bothered to stop tinkering with that spirit phone long enough to help me?” Son-of-Annwyn grumbled, as he wrapped another bandage around his forearm, activating the talen that would enhance his natural healing abilities. Son-of-Annwyn, a Fianna Ahroun so interested in the spirits and rituals that he could be a Theurge, had an unparalled tactical mind and was a great asset to this quest. Luckily his sense of humor and down-to-earth nature balanced out the ferocity he brought to play. 

“You were taller than him; it looked like you had everything under control,” Peter commented laconically, tapping his finger on the tiny keyboard attached to his smartphone. “Besides, if I can get this mapping feature to work, just think about how much easier travelling in the Umbra will be in the future.” The Elder Glass Walker chuckled at his own joke as he rummaged in his omnipresent man-purse for some obscure tool. 

The rest of the team ignored the banter, as they continued to prepare for the rest of the mission. They’d made sure to bring plenty of supplies, as the cryptic explanation hadn’t specified how long the quest would take. Even with several powerful Garou participating, it was better to be overprepared than risk finding themselves without an essential item when they needed it most. The last pocket realm had been a lush jungle teeming with energy. This new realm was the polar opposite: a barren desert with burning sands, harsh winds, and little sign of life. 

Packing the last of the bandages back into her satchel, Vidya recited the lines once more for the group. “‘Betwixt and between, through four spirit gates. Challenges passing, your answers await.’ Not the most informative guide, but it’s been successful so far,” she smiled charmingly at the others. Vidya Armund, a Get of Fenris Galliard on loan from her own pack, had a knowledge of history, language, and culture unparalleled throughout the Garou Nation. As an ardent follower of Snow Queen, she could walk into any situation with grace and poise, no matter how unfamiliar or alien. 

“We’ve managed to complete the Air challenge with a leap of faith when Sierra spotted that gateway ripple; the Water portal when yours truly swam down to retrieve the key, while being chased by twisted piranha spirits who tried to bite her in the most uncomfortable places. Defeating that giant guardian earned passage through the stone doorway, which obviously accounts for the Earth challenge.” Vidya smiled at Son-of-Annwyn, who still looked annoyed about the fight with the mountain giant. 

Cait chimed in, musing over the quest she had set them all on, “The vision Stag gave me showed that we would need to overcome three stages in order to find the answers. I believe the first was Shapes the Path-rhya deciphering where to begin. Now that we are here, it’s passing through the different gates, just as Vidya-rhya and Sierra-rhya translated from the rocks. That means even if—no, when—we complete this part, we will still have one final challenge to overcome, to get the answers I need.” Cait, a Fianna Ragabash, was the youngest among the five, but her curiosity and fearlessness rivaled all the others. 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

After finding the Ragabash, they followed her beyond three more dunes, while the ambient heat climbed higher. A dull rumble filled the air, an unfamiliar sound almost like an avalanche, totally out of place in a desert. Reaching the last rise, they stood, stunned at the sight, while Cait cocked her eyebrow at Peter saucily, “Ever seen a waterfall of fire, not lava—FIRE—before?” She paused before adding a belated, “…rhya?” 

Peter chuckled. “Nope.” Shaking his head, he continued to walk down the side of the dune towards it. 

“Doorways of Fire. Again? Really?” The young Ragabash continued to gripe, as she followed Peter down the side of the dune. “How the hell are we going to deal with this? Please tell me you have a plan!” 

“Of course I have a plan,” his response was immediate and calm. 

“Well?  What’s the plan?” Cait asked, as the others shook their heads in silent laughter. “Improvise.” 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
_“Some say that the great caerns are a symbol of hope for a better tomorrow. Bah! The great caerns are a testament to our tenacity. Despite everything the Wyrm threw at us, the umbral storms, the Caern Plague, and the Siege of the Dark Brigade, the great caerns survived. We raged against their hate and filth! We endured! We won, though we drowned our enemies in our blood.”_ 

—Valka One-Eye, Jarl of the Sept of the Blood Fist 

A _caern_ is a place blessed by Gaia. It serves as part of the spirit realm’s circulatory system by helping maintain the world’s Gnosis. Shapeshifters cultivate these sacred locations, protecting them from those who would use their raw gnostic power for selfish and destructive purposes. During the Age of Man, Silver Fang Theurges raised the first caern deep in the Ural Mountains of Russia. Since then, caerns have become places to commune with Mother Gaia, outposts to rest from the eternal war against the Wyrm, and homes to defend against any and all threats. 

## Historically, caerns were rarely shared 

between tribes. Werewolves fiercely defended these places, often against other Garou, and their owners jealously hoarded their caern’s power. During the Age of Storms, when the Garou Nation unified against the Storm Eater, this taboo slowly began to change. While caerns often remained primarily controlled by one tribe, members of other tribes filtered in to support these places of power. Over time, the staunch tribal divisions over caerns faded, and the Garou Nation found solidarity in mixed-tribe caerns. As mankind continued to destroy the Wyld places and threaten the very existence of the Garou, caerns became more important than ever. 

Once, hundreds of caerns tempered and spread the flow of Gnosis, continually renewing the world. During the battles at the onset of the Age of Apocalypse, the Wyrm took advantage of how few shapeshifters defended each caern, and it sent huge waves of minions to topple them in succession through sheer force. As the survivors of these battles sought shelter with one another, the number of 

defenders in each caern grew. In the end, shapeshifters saved thirteen of the caerns; each of these became known as the _great caerns_ . With the widespread destruction of so many of these spiritual centers came another heart-stopping blow—the sacred rites that allowed for the creation of a new caern had ceased to function. Gaia’s children reeled at this loss, fearing that the Garou would be confined to those thirteen spaces left to them and that the remaining caerns would eventually be overrun. Then came what many called a miracle. An unlikely cooperation between Fera and Garou led to the discovery of _shard seeds_ , items capable of giving birth to an entirely new type of caern. 

The new caerns— _shard caerns_ —are vastly weaker than their predecessors. Compared to the mighty locales from which they are descended, shard caerns are fragile and 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

need constant protection. Now, shapeshifters guard the few remaining great caerns with zealous fanaticism, as they serve both as the remaining gnostic centers of the world and as the only known source of shard seeds. If the world is to survive, shard caerns must be protected long enough to blossom into great caerns, hopefully sparking a new age. 

## The Great Caerns 

_“My first memories involve the diaspora of the nation, after the great caerns were destroyed by the Siege of the Dark Brigade. I try to imagine a world filled with great caerns… the Garou of that time must have still felt hope that the world could be set right.”_ 

## —Verity Argyris, Black Fury, Historian 

Once, before the Age of Apocalypse, there were hundreds of caerns around the world, located anyplace with a wellspring of Gnosis. Werewolves curated and protected these places for Gaia. Though she might be far from home, a weary traveler would have few problems finding a friendly sept at which to lay her head while she traversed Gaia’s lands. These sacred places were the pinnacle of Garou society, offering shelter to weary travelers and kin alike. They served as staging points for missions against the Wyrm, and they stood as places of learning for those who sought to expand their minds. If Gaia was the soul of the Garou Nation, then her caerns were its heart. 

With the onset of the Age of Apocalypse came umbral storms and the Siege of the Dark Brigade, which decimated the Garou’s bastions and overwhelmed their steadfast defenders. When the dust had settled, the surviving werewolves found themselves lost. Many had lived their entire lives in the 

same sept. For some, the loss of their caerns was less painful, but the Silent Striders, who rarely called one singular place home for very long, knew how to cope with this _diaspora_ . Others found their grief gave way to despair. These Garou slipped deep into Harano as the enormity of so much loss in such a short time began to pile upon them. 

For a time, the nation was in chaos. Frantic survivors didn’t know where to turn. Rumors regarding which caerns had fallen and which had survived often conflicted, as conventional means of communication were compromised. The remaining great caerns were inundated with refugees, and while they tried to accommodate as many as possible, numerous Garou found themselves turned away at the bawn. Some werewolves returned to the locations of their now-defunct caerns with their septmates, struggling to maintain some semblance of normalcy in the face of the Age of Apocalypse. Others set out with their packs, roaming the earth in a desperate attempt to find a place to cling to: a place where they could set down roots and once again attempt to fulfill the duties proscribed by Gaia. 

The discovery of shard seeds gave birth to the first shard caern in Las Vegas, bringing a new hope to the Garou Nation. Wanderers and refugees, though tired and increasingly desperate, found themselves filled with new purpose. Dutifully, they made their way to the remaining great caerns to receive cultivated shard seeds. Like the seeds themselves, their hopes had been brought back from extinction, and they saw within them a promise that life as they once knew it might someday return. The Garou slowly began the process of reclaiming their destroyed caerns, replacing them with ones powered by the new shard seeds. Meanwhile, the great caerns continued to fortify themselves, their survival increasingly vital to Garou Nation. 

**
## **Lost Great Caerns** 

The opening battles of the Age of Apocalypse were a time of immense turmoil. Initial reports were frantic, as caerns worldwide came under simultaneous assault, leaving much of the world under the fog of war. Lists of casualties remain incomplete, with Garou in the thousands presumed dead. A number of caerns simply “went dark” in the chaos and still remain lost. 

Some believe that there are lost great caerns sleeping deep in the remaining places of the Wyld. These caerns may have escaped the Siege of the Dark Brigade, perhaps because their Moon Bridges were deliberately severed or because the Garou who tended them were isolated enough to somehow escape notice. Garou and Fera alike have begun scouring the corners of the world, seeking to claim the glory that would come from discovering a lost great caern. News that any caern managed to survive would be a boost to morale, as well as a tactical boon in securing territory against the Wyrm. 

**
Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## The Surviving Thirteen Great Caerns 

Thirteen great caerns survived the plagues and the Siege of the Dark Brigade. These last bastions of the nation’s strength have become symbols of hope to beleaguered Garou seeking 

any inspiration to keep fighting. Although they are few in number and often found in areas far from civilization, the great caerns are rabidly defended by all; the survival of the nation and of Gaia depends on the stability of these precious few places of power. 

**
## The Great Caerns of North America 

## Sept of Forgotten Waters 

Maurepas Swamp, Louisiana, United States 

**Caern Totem:** Big Papa, Totem of War 

## **Caern Rating:** 2 

**Sept Leader:** Benny “Ear to the Ground” Reed, Ahroun, Elder, Bone Gnawer Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of Forgotten Waters served as the battleground for a ruthless siege between the Garou Nation and the Dark Brigade. As other caerns across the world fell, this place became the only caern left standing in the continental United States. Garou from every tribe, along with many Fera, stood in defense of the caern. While there were terrible casualties among those protecting the sept, they were ultimately triumphant in turning back the tide of the Wyrm. Today, the sept remains open to all who enjoy Gaia’s blessing, both Garou and Fera, and it has become the base of operations for the Concordat of Stars. Many Fera breeds call Forgotten Waters home; this was one of the first caerns in the world at which Garou allowed their Fera companions to officially join the sept. 

Situated deep in the Louisiana swamp, the Sept of Forgotten Waters is not an easy place to find. The Warders and Guardians have used the natural defenses of the wetlands to hide their presence from those who would wish them harm. Travel to the caern’s site can be difficult, often necessitating the use of boats to travel through the area. The deep waters surrounding the protectorate are patrolled by the two Mokolé who call Forgotten Waters home, and the nearby towns are watched by Bone Gnawers. The caern itself is set at the center of the protectorate on a large, flat stretch of ground, peppered with ancient trees. Here, the sept structures can be found. The Sept of Forgotten Waters takes particular pride in its challenge ring, a massive muddy wallow. 

**
## **The Beast Courts and Dragon Nests** 

The Stargazer tribe has reported that, in spite of the Wyrm’s devastating assault, the Beast Courts were able to secure and retain an unknown number of their own caerns, known as **Dragon Nests** , in undisclosed locations throughout Asia. The Garou Nation has yet to bridge centuries of distrust to learn more, but there is hope that perhaps there are others fighting the good fight to restore Gaia. As they were party to the discovery of the shard seeds, it is expected that the Beast Courts are using similar methods to retake their former holdings. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of the Last Breath 

Caribou River Park Reserve, Manitoba, Canada 

**Caern Totem:** Ever Dreamer, Totem of Wisdom 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Joseph Wintersrage, Ahroun, Elder, Wendigo Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of the Last Breath is an ancient caern that traces its roots back to the time of the Pure Lands. For centuries, this sept has been a haven for the Wendigo, who carefully guarded the nearby herds and terrain against human expansion; they scarcely tolerated werewolves from other tribes. Although these ancient grudges have diminished in the Age of Apocalypse, this caern remains predominantly populated by Wendigo. 

Nestled in the wilds of Canada, the Sept of the Last Breath has stood unflinching in the face of storms, both natural and umbral. The lands of the protectorate are harsh and wild, and those who inhabit the sept are of a similar vein. As the caerns of the world were being overrun by minions of the Wyrm, this caern stood firm as the sept’s Theurges summoned powerful storms to sweep the area. When the Wyrm’s hordes were forced to take shelter, the sept’s warriors emerged, and they fought in close quarters despite the freezing conditions. Through their efforts, they dispatched the Wyrm, and the sept was able to serve as a staging point to aid in relief efforts. But the Wendigo who call the sept home protected their caern at great cost, and they are continuing to slowly rebuild their numbers. 

**
Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of the Weeping Daughter 

Grimbone Creek, 15 miles west of Lawson, Alaska, United States 

**Caern Totem:** The Weeping Daughter, Totem of Wisdom 

## **Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Thistlechar, Galliard, Elder, Red Talon Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of the Weeping Daughter was once an ancient caern, raised when the Three Brother tribes first came to the Pure Lands. During the Age of Sorrow, an unknown Ragabash awakened a Wyrm creature that destroyed the sept. Afterwards, the caern went into hibernation. During the Age of Storms, a pack of Red Talons, known as the Strong Backs, migrated to America and headed north, seeking to test themselves in a real frontier. They encountered swarm after swarm of Black Spiral Dancers and other Wyrm creatures before they cleansed the land and awoke the caern. 

For nearly two hundred years, the Sept of the Weeping Daughter remained isolated; its keepers restricted admittance to Red Talons and, out of respect, select Wendigo. But during the Siege of the Dark Brigade, the few local guardians could not withstand the Wyrm’s onslaught. In a move considered controversial at the time, the Sept Leader decreed the caern open to refugees escaping the cities and destruction, and asked only that any who were able to stand take part in the fighting. When the Wyrm arrived on the heels of the newcomers, the Sept Leader led the defenders with a powerful howl that bolstered their resolve and spurred the attackers into disorganized frenzy. Filled with powerful rage, the once-haggard refugees seized their pursuers while Thistlechar challenged the enemy commander to face her personally. After an epic struggle, she gained the upper hand, and violently tore out his throat. Lacking any semblance of structure, the Dark Brigade was outmatched and sought to flee. The Red Talons and Wendigo spent the next several days hunting them across the tundra. 

In recognition of the group effort that saved the great caern, it has since been decreed that any Garou may freely seek permission to enter it. However, the caern remains a stronghold for the Sanctum of Gaia, and its leaders are staunchly opposed to any notions of Weaver sympathy. Recently, a slew of Rokea have made the sept a port of call, suggesting that their relations with the Red Talons have warmed. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

The Great Caerns of South America 

## Sept of the Hollow Heart 

Near Tefé, Brazil 

**Caern Totem:** Great White Mouse, Totem of Cunning 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Harmony “Spins the Wheel” Mason, Galliard, Elder, Child of Gaia Councilor **Fera Leader:** Mercutio Tovani, Dusk Pryio, Elder, Bastet 

**Sept Description:** Although the Sept of the Hollow Heart is relatively new, none can deny that it is a well-defended caern. For years, this caern served as the base of Garou operations in the Amazon. Its inhabitants stood ready to take the fight to the Wyrm at a moment’s notice, which often involved engaging the miners and loggers through non-violent means. When Pentex’s elite strike teams set out for the caern, few—locally, or further afield—believed that they could win the day. But rather than engage the invaders directly, the sept called on its human and media connections to thwart an attempt to clear-cut a path to the caern. Afterwards, the defenders spent weeks confounding and exhausting the attackers who sought to make their way through the jungles, leaving them depleted and unable to fight. Deserted by many of its human allies, the forces of the Wyrm were eventually cornered and defeated. 

Located near the Tefé river in Brazil, the Sept of the Hollow Heart lies deep in the Amazon rainforest. It is difficult to locate and isolated from civilization, though the sept itself has much in the way of modern conveniences. With many of its structures built high in the trees, the sept is a marvel of non-invasive housing and sustainable living, all powered through renewable energy. The members of the sept have pioneered new techniques for fighting the Wyrm, and they routinely work with indigenous tribes and activists worldwide. Although the sept is always prepared for war, it has chosen a totem that revels in cunning. These Garou understand that brute force alone will not destroy the creatures who threaten the Garou Nation. The sept remains the main staging ground for actions against the Wyrm in South America. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of the Split Sun 

Sierra de las Minas, Guatemala 

**Caern Totem:** Quetzal, Totem of Respect 

**Caern Rating:** 3 

**Sept Leader:** Aylen “Ripper” Queupul, Theurge, Elder, Uktena Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of the Split Sun can track its history back to the days when the first Uktena migrated to South America. Founded to serve as a central staging point for fighting the vampires of Mesoamerica, the Garou of the Sept of the Split Sun have been known for their strength and tenacity in the overwhelming face of the Wyrm’s corruption since the sept was founded. When war came to the Pure Lands, Shadow Lords attempted to take this sept by force, in hopes of securing resources for their war against the Aztecs. The werewolves of the Split Sun, following their totem Quetzal, hid all traces of the caern and retreated deep into their protectorate, where the Shadow Lords could not find them. When the Dark Brigade came to attack the Sept of the Split Sun, the ancient magics endured, and the creatures of the Wyrm found themselves hopelessly lost in the jungle. Throughout the course of weeks, they were picked off by the caern’s defenders, led by the sept’s Ragabash and Ahroun. Although they were unable to damage the sept itself, the area was not left unmolested, as the Wyrm burned the jungle and killed dozens of locals in retribution. 

One of the largest remaining great caerns in the world, the Sept of the Split Sun sprawls over a massive protectorate deep in the mountains of Sierra de las Minas. Many of the sept’s structures are carefully hidden amongst the natural flora, and some parts of the caern have never been seen by those who are not sworn to the sept. It’s rumored that among the monuments in its hall of heroes are some dedicated to the Camazotz. The Sept of the Split Sun has opened its borders to most Garou; however, Shadow Lords are met with a wary eye, and find that they must work harder to gain the trust of the inhabitants of the sept. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

The Great Caerns of Asia 

## Sept of the Crescent Moon 

Ural Mountains, Russia 

**Caern Totem:** Falcon, Totem of Respect 

**Caern Rating:** 5 

**Sept Leader:** Sabine “Silver Swift” LaCoix, Ahroun, Elder, Silver Fang Councilor 

**Sept Description:** As caerns across the world fell to the Dark Brigade, Garou survivors and Fera reinforcements fled to the Sept of the Crescent Moon deep in the central Ural Mountains of Russia, hoping that, of all places, the world’s oldest caern would be able to endure. Here, they found a massive battle. The Wyrm attacked with an overwhelming number of its total forces, hoping to score a symbolic victory and demoralize the remaining defenders. Knowing this, the High King chose to lead the defense personally, bringing with him his legendary pack. 

The ensuing battle was pitiless and costly. Though valiant, the defenders were still outmatched. When all seemed lost, they received unexpected aid from an envoy of Fera who fought beside them without prejudice. Additional assistance came from a group of vampires from the Gangrel clan. Catching their enemies unawares, they seized upon the Wyrm’s forces from behind, dragging them away into the frozen night. Only through the combined might of the Fera and Garou—along with assistance from vampires—was a narrow victory made possible. 

However, many were quick to criticize the Silver Fangs for concentrating their efforts on protecting this single caern, traditionally one of their own, when so many others needed their assistance. The Shadow Lords argued that had this particular caern yielded, perhaps three or four on the brink of holding could have been saved. Though a great number of refugees were spared as part of the effort, the issue remains a contentious one. 

Pristine and savage, the Sept of the Crescent Moon is a beautiful Wyld sept. For many years, no man-made structures could be found on its bawn, although small housing clusters dotted its protectorate. This prohibition was briefly relaxed during the onset of the Age of Apocalypse, as several defensive structures were erected. As is custom, all official business of the sept takes place outdoors, regardless of the weather, often set near one of the waterfalls that highlight the landscape. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of Glass and Steel 

Galaxy Soho Building, Beijing, China 

**Caern Totem:** Glass and Steel, Totem of Cunning 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Anh Lai Far-Traveler, Ahroun, Elder, Stargazer Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of Glass and Steel is a relatively new sept, only formed in 2009 when ground broke on the Galaxy Soho building in Beijing. Many younger shapeshifters took to this new caern, looking to prove their worth. Although Beijing boasted several urban caerns prior to the Age of Apocalypse, only one was able to emerge from the flames unscathed. 

The unique design of the building positioned it at the heart of the city’s leylines, enabling it to draw an excess of Gnosis. For the past fifty years, Stargazer kinfolk worked carefully with urban planners to arrange this unique alignment, regulating the construction of nearby buildings to allow the lines of power to be maintained. When the Wyrm attacked, the Stargazers had fortune on their side, having recalled the sept’s warriors in preparation for what was deemed an inauspicious day in advance. While their attackers believed them easy prey, the Stargazers had careful preparation, the mortal populace, and for once, Weaver technology on their side. Through clarity and resolve, their caern endured, though others did not share in their fortune. 

The Galaxy Soho building in Beijing is a massive, sprawling creation of glass and steel, rising high into the sky. The top fifteen floors of the building are dedicated to the caern, along with the massive gardens in the atrium. The Stargazers are immensely proud of their new harmonious space, and they find that their attitude towards the Weaver has softened in the wake of their victory. Although the sept is open to any Garou or Fera, mostly members of the Beast Courts can be found here. All members enjoy excellent relations with the surrounding community, who treat their elder kinfolk with great care and reverence. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

The Great Caerns of Africa 

## Dark River Caern 

Near Lisala, Democratic Republic of the Congo 

**Caern Totem:** Elephant, Totem of Glory 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Fera Leader:** Amari the Thunder, Midnight Pryio, Elder, Simba Bastet 

**Notable Garou:** Samira “Doomspeaker” Tahan, Ragabash, Elder, Silent Strider Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Dark River Caern is the home of the Ahadi, and it serves as the heart of the war against the Wyrm in Africa. Founded years ago, Dark River acted as a staging point for the conflict against Black Tooth and offered refuge to those whose homes were destroyed by the mad Simba and his Endless Storm. After Black Tooth’s demise, the Dark River Caern continued to grow, as more shapeshifters learned of his death and came out of hiding. 

The attack on the Dark River Caern was swift and brutal, coming on the heels of the attack on the Sept of Last Stand. The Dark Brigade waited until warriors of the sept had left to assist their sister caern to the west before swarming the boundaries of the protectorate. However, this attack was all according to the defender’s plan. Once the Dark Brigade began to move, the warriors returned, joined by Silent Striders from a nearby caern who arrived in what seemed an impossibly short time. The creatures of the Wyrm had grossly underestimated the fortitude of kinfolk and young Garou, and they found themselves crushed between spears and claws. 

The Ahadi reacted immediately. The Fera were grateful to the Silent Striders, who had chosen to cede their caern to better defend the others. For their part, the Striders felt more eager to face their enemy on the road, and they immediately dispatched groups to engage and harry the forces in the field. Thanks to their efforts, the remaining caerns in Africa were evacuated of both shapeshifters and kinfolk alike, and large groups of warriors traveled to assist the Garou in protecting their remaining septs, their rapid movement confounding the Wyrm’s forces. 

The Dark River Caern is situated on the Congo, near Lisala. Traveling there is treacherous, if one doesn’t know the proper routes, and several of the sept’s structures—including the hall of heroes and the caern heart—are housed in caves only accessible through the water. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of the Wheel of Ptah 

Casablanca, Morocco 

**Caern Totem:** Ptah, Totem of Cunning 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Margrave Josef Konietzko, Ragabash, Elder, Shadow Lord Councilor 

**Sept Description:** The Sept of the Wheel of Ptah makes its home in Casablanca, in the ancient Mosque Anfa. Established by the Silent Striders, this caern has served as a place of rest for weary travelers since its founding. Prior to the Age of Apocalypse, the Sept of the Wheel of Ptah stood as a crossroads of sorts for Garou who wished to travel via Moon Bridge—the unique power of the caern allowed it to open a gateway to anywhere in the world, regardless of whether or not a pathstone existed on the other end. Many adventurous Garou used this ability to travel deep into the Umbra or to discover ancient lost caerns that lay dormant. A secluded courtyard protects the Fountain of a Thousand Grieving Battles. Historically, the water of this fountain would run blood red whenever a tragic battle occurred within the nation. The first sign that an attack on the caerns was imminent was the water in the fountain running black. 

As a Moon Bridge nexus, the Sept of the Wheel of Ptah was among the first to be attacked. The Wyrm had hoped to seize the caern and use it to travel between septs. Realizing this, the Sept Leader ordered the destruction of the centuries-old Pathstone—one of the first ever to be created—which was the sept’s only hope of retreat should they be overwhelmed. With its strategic value diminished, the Wyrm chose to reallocate a number of its forces elsewhere. Fortune then intervened, and the caern was able to hold with the assistance of Silent Striders and members of the Ahadi alike. Although the sept has yet to regain its ability to travel nearly anywhere via Moon Bridge, the kinfolk of the Sept of the Wheel of Ptah have worked to find acceptable alternatives, and the sept remains a travel hub for members of the Garou Nation traveling via mundane means. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of Last Stand 

Lagos, Nigeria 

**Caern Totem:** Menegehwo, Totem of Wisdom 

**Caern Rating:** 2 

**Fera Leader:** Kisasi, Dusk Pryio, Midnight Aspect, Elder, Ajaba, Leader of the Ahadi 

**Notable Garou:** Roger Daly, Theurge, Elder, Glass Walker Councilor 

**Sept Description:** As the Ahadi triumphed over Black Tooth, the Sept of Last Stand was founded in Lagos. At the time, it was the only caern of its kind—affiliated closely with the Ahadi and welcoming all shapeshifters who allied themselves with that cause. Even a handful of Rokea who helped to patrol the coasts found themselves welcome guests. The victory of the new sept was shortlived, however, as soon after its founding, the Wyrm rose up once again. As the vampires and allies of Black Tooth, since fallen to the Wyrm, swarmed the fledgling sept, the members of Last Stand were in dire straits. 

As the Silicon Valley of Africa, Lagos had found itself the object of Glass Walker attention. The tribe had been watching the area with great curiosity, observing how Weaver spirits would comingle with the local ecology. At the onset of the Apocalypse, Roger Daly and a number of Glass Walkers found themselves in the city on special assignment. When the Sept of Last Stand was the first on the continent to be attacked, they worked to convince Kisasi and the Ahadi that the war against the Wyrm would not be won by Garou or Fera alone. In order to combat the forces of darkness, they would have to fight together. With their combined prowess and the Weaver-backed might of the Glass Walkers, the Wyrm was repulsed, and the caern was saved. 

Situated on the coast of Nigeria, the Sept of Last Stand is on the edge of the city: a small urban caern. Several office buildings, a sprawling park, and a set of docks make up its bawn, with several additional areas slated for expansion. The Glass Walkers have chosen this area as the epicenter of their rebirth, seeking to demonstrate once and for all that Weaver technology can be beneficial to Gaia, even in places characterized by their rapid technological and economic growth.
