Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

The Great Caerns of Europe 

## Sept of Bygone Visions 

Ecube, Greece 

**Caern Totem:** Themis the Dream Weaver, Totem of Wisdom 

**Caern Rating:** 4 

**Sept Leader:** Alika Kalonimos, Philodox, Athro, Black Fury Councilor 

**Sept Description:** For many years, the Sept of Bygone Visions was a popular place for young Black Furies to train and go through their Rites of Passage. Here, young women were groomed in the proper behavior of a Black Fury, taught how to interact with other werewolves throughout the world, and how to remain triumphant in the face of a society that was heavily slanted towards men. When the Dark Brigade attacked, they hit the caern hard and fast in the middle of the night, without warning. Only a pack of Cubs on a regular patrol spotted the incoming danger, and their quick thinking is credited with saving the lives of hundreds. 

Throughout the course of days, as wave after wave of Wyrm creatures broke against the walls, the sept endured. At the onset of the crisis, their oracles told them that the caern would hold, but only after help came from their sisters in arms. When reports arrived telling of nearby septs falling, hope began to falter. Alika Kalonimos, the Sept Leader, decided she was through waiting for relief. With the hope that she was not too late, she reached out to an unlikely ally. When the battle cry of the Get of Fenris sounded behind the Wyrm’s lines, the other Black Furies were shocked. Fighting in the distance were the Valkyria of Freya, come to relieve their sisters. 

Among their number was Daghur, born the brother of Sept Leader Alika, but given away at birth. He had since joined the Valkyria, and having received word from his sister, he convinced the camp to come to the Black Furies’ aid. Under the fury of their combined assault, the forces of the Wyrm crumpled and fell, and were soon slaughtered entirely. A number of the surviving Furies were shocked to see a man fighting so valiantly among the Valkyria, and they were even more surprised to find he he had been born a Fury. His presence that day sparked the debate that eventually led the Furies’ to shift their tribal policies. 

The Sept of Bygone Visions is situated on the island of Ecube, also known as _Miria_ to the Black Furies. The sept itself is ancient, and the stone structures that dot its bawn have endured the test of time. On the nearby coast of Crete, the Black Furies founded a mainland villa as a safe haven for kinfolk, including both Black Fury kin and others alike. For centuries, this sept was exclusively open to Black Furies. Only after the start of the Age of Apocalypse did the sisters of Bygone Visions open their borders to others. The island remains a center of power for the Black Furies, and it is here that the Inner Calyx meets to discuss tribal business. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Sept of Blood Fist 

Black Forest, Germany 

**Caern Totem:** Boar, Totem of War 

**Caern Rating:** 3 

**Sept Leader:** Valka One-Eye, Philodox, Elder, Fenrir Councilor 

**Sept Description:** For centuries, legends have swirled about the ferocious werewolves that haunt the Black Forest of Germany, rumored to prey on lone travelers and livestock from surrounding villages. Like many stories, these myths have their foundation in reality. The Sept of the Blood Fist was one of the first caerns to be established in Europe, deep within the heart of the woods. Its territory includes several small villages that are scattered around the edge of its protectorate, where many Get of Fenris kin live and work. 

When news of the Dark Brigade came, Valka One-Eye gleefully awaited them with her forces well-prepared. For centuries, the Get of Fenris had awaited Ragnarok in anticipation that mighty Fenris would finally be free of his captivity. The sept exalted as they took to the field, eager to join the battle they’d anxiously expected; surely, Fenris was nearly free. Yet when the dust settled and the sept stood triumphant, there was neither sign nor portent of their totem. In the wake of their victory, the Sept of the Blood Fist was one of the first to offer aid and shelter to others, displaying a startling lack of the haughtiness characteristic of the tribe. 

Stark and wild, Blood Fist is a haven for Lupus and Metis Garou. With much of the wilderness in the area still untouched by man, it offers solitude not found in many places. After the decimation of many of the world’s septs, Blood Fist remains largely unmolested. The Get of Fenris leaders have opened their boundaries to their allies, even inviting Metis, who require the safety of a sept, to live on their grounds. Although the sept no longer strictly belongs to the Get of Fenris, it is still ruled by their traditions and laws. It is a harsh place to live, as the countryside struggles to support the refugee population, but none can deny that the Sept of Blood Fist is a boon to many who would otherwise be destitute.

## Sept of Silver Tara 

Hill of Tara, Ireland 

**Caern Totem:** Stag, Totem of Respect 

## **Caern Rating:** 5 

**Sept Leader:** Bron Mac Fionn, the Ard Righ, Galliard, Elder, Fianna Councilor 

**Sept Description:** Since the dawn of the Age of Legends, when the Fianna and the fae were bound with ties of love, loyalty, and honor, Silver Tara has been the stronghold of that tribe. Thanks to a unique glamour spell from an ancient pact with the fae, Silver Tara remained undetectable to any who would harm the Fianna. Thanks to this fortification, the Fianna held Ireland against invaders throughout their history. But prior to the onset of the Apocalypse, the Fianna were tricked into severing their pact with faerie, which lowered their defenses and allowed the Dark Brigade to initiate a siege on the caern. Caught without its fae allies, the Sept of Silver Tara endured thanks to the aid of the aid of the Corax, who came to help the ancient fortress in order to repay debts earned during the Age of Tragedy. 

As the other caerns began to fall, Bron Mac Fionn, the Ard Righ, feared his own fortifications wouldn’t last. With a small band, he escaped the blockade and went deep into the Umbra, seeking aid from the fae while his army held the line. He returned from the Arcadia Gateway with an artifact that restored the pact between the Sept of Silver Tara and its old allies. The resurgence of power restored the defenses and healed the caern, but at a cost. Bron had earned the ire of the fae Princess Mariana, who cursed him and all of the Fianna, opening their hearts to the sadness she endured. 

With the pact restored, the Sept of Silver Tara remains one of the most fortified positions in the world for the Garou Nation. Nestled in emerald hills and protected by ancient magics, it serves as a staging point to retake the British Isles. However, its politics are in a delicate state as Mac Fionn holds the reins of power by a thin thread. Many worry that the unrest stirred by the recent curse may lead to bloody conflict among the hot-blooded Fianna, or even worse——a crisis of leadership, leaving the caern vulnerable in the face of a second onslaught by the Wyrm. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Geography of a Caern 

All caerns—whether great caerns or shard caerns—are unique places of power, shaped by their gnostic energies and the personality of the septs that protect them. A sept uses the best available means to lay out its defenses, where and how they construct buildings on the bawn, and how they organize the space they inhabit. While each caern is unique, tradition dictates a that all septs share a few customary features. While these design elements can vary in appearance, the two most important locales are the caern heart and the bawn. In terms of scale, a shard caern is generally distinguished from a great caern by having a smaller caern heart and bawn. For more information on the areas of a caern and how they interact with sept positions, see **Chapter Eleven: The Garou Nation, The Sept, Workings of a Sept** , page 524. 

## Caern Heart 

The _caern heart_ is the center of the caern, a holy shrine to Gaia that reflects the spiritual nature of the caern’s totem spirit its primary aspect. Caerns of _war_ possess caern hearts that provoke feelings of rage, strength, and endurance, while a caern of _healing_ infuses its visitors with tranquil and calming feelings. Caerns of _respect_ kindle feelings of awe and reverence, while caerns of _wisdom_ inspire deep contemplation. Caerns of _cunning_ are often carefully concealed, while caerns of _glory_ stand as testaments to the deeds of the past. 

Each of the great caerns has a _caern stone_ , which serves as the physical focus for binding the totem spirit and also delineates the center of the caern heart. A shard caern, however, has a shard seed buried in the earth at the exact center of the caern. In each, the respective focal point is where the Gauntlet is weakest, even within the alreadyfaint bounds of the bawn, making its location incredibly obvious to anyone sensitive to such emanations. The focal point of a caern is its most heavily protected area, and some Warders go so far as to live as close to it as possible, ensuring constant levels of surveillance. 

## The Bawn 

The _bawn_ is the outer ring of a caern that surrounds the caern heart. Often the bawn is considered the outer wall, heavily patrolled by the Warder and Guardians to protect the caern heart from intruders. The size of the bawn depends on the rating of the caern, as the potency of the caern’s heart determines how far its power and influence can radiate. A caern totem may restrict the size of a bawn, pulling the area back from its normal limits to encompass 

a smaller area, one that its protectors can defend safely. Rural caerns can and do even sometimes encompass small towns in the wilderness, with kin and Garou living within its boundaries, while urban caern bawns may be smaller in order to protect the Veil, even if the rating of the caern would warrant a larger space. 

## The Waystation 

Typically situated on the periphery of the bawn, the _waystation_ is a formal area where travelers are housed. Depending on the sept’s facilities, this space may be a simple barn or a dry part of a cave in which to curl up, or it can be as elaborate as a deluxe suite in select city caerns. Visitors are often made to remain in this area before they secure permission to freely move throughout the sept. Additionally, should a caern have a functioning Pathstone, it will often be housed in the same complex at the waystation, to keep visitors in one place while their business can be determined. During the Age of Apocalypse, waystations were filled beyond capacity, with temporary housing hastily erected to accommodate refugees. As of late, this area is increasingly used as a storehouse to resupply the various itinerant packs that look to caerns for support. 

## The Challenge Mound 

When a member of a sept wishes to announce a challenge, she takes to the _challenge mound_ , making a bold declaration of her name, who she is challenging, and the nature of the challenge. What began as a place younger Garou found when seeking a higher elevation to address a crowd became a dedicated space in nearly every caern. Whether it’s a simple pile of dirt, an abandoned car, or an ornate stone dais, the challenge mound is easily recognizable and often avoided by foot traffic. Simply standing at the top is enough to attract attention, as most werewolves will stop what they’re doing in anticipation of seeing what is about to occur there. 

## The Hall of Heroes 

The _hall of heroes_ is a place memorializing those who gave their lives in service to Gaia in a heroic fashion. This area is typically removed from the caern heart, as it is often used for both mourning the dead and educating the young about sept history. Before the Apocalypse, pilgrimages to distant septs, honoring their legendary heroes, were very common. Following the events of the onset of the Age of Apocalypse, these halls have swelled with new additions worldwide, with some halls bearing a monument simply dedicated “to the fallen,” with the names and dates of battles noted. The monuments themselves take various forms, depending on the artistic skills of the sept’s members and on various cultural influences.

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Great Caern Rating 

The great caerns represent the last, best hope for the world, but even they have limitations. This section details the creation, attributes, and defenses of a great caern. 

## Generating a New Great Caern 

To create a great caern, the Storyteller must first assign it a rating between 1 and 5. This rating indicates the caern’s potency and helps determine the following information: 

- All great caerns are protected by a caern totem, whose rating equals the great caern’s rating + 10. For example, a great caern with a rating of 2 has a caern totem spirit with a rating of 12. For more information, see **Chapter Fourteen: Allies and Antagonists: Totems** , page 632. 

- The caern heart is the sacred center of the caern. So long as this place is intact, the caern continues to heal and cleanse itself of all taint. The caern heart possesses a number of health levels equal to 10 x the great caern’s rating. For more information about the caern heart, see **Chapter Eleven: The Garou Nation, The Sept, Workings of a Sept, Sept Geography** , page 526. 

- The totem spirit of a caern attracts other spirits to act as its guardians. A great caern has a number of guardian spirits equal to 10 x the great caern’s rating. These spirits are rating 3 Jaggling spirit Stock NPCs. 

## Great Caern Attributes 

A great caern has the following attributes: 

- The great caerns are Gaia’s spiritual life-support system, and Gnosis flows freely within their boundaries. A shapeshifter who meditates and rests uninterrupted for five minutes within one replenishes all of her Gnosis. 

- So long as the caern totem remains healthy, and the caern heart is untainted by the Wyrm, great caerns protect the local Penumbra from bane spirits. If the caern opens its Moon Bridge to receive visitors, this protection is temporarily weakened and bane spirits can enter the local Penumbra so long as the Moon Bridge is open for travel. 

- Great caerns must maintain greater numbers of shapeshifters bound to them in order to continue to thrive. In the Age of Apocalypse, for every increment of the caern’s rating, 20 Garou or Fera must be bound to that caern through the Rite of Acceptance in order to 

keep the caern totem and caern heart healthy. Failure to maintain this number results in the caern rapidly losing strength. If the population does not return to the minimum within a year, the caern’s rating drops by one increment. If a caern’s rating drops to 0 in this way, it becomes dormant until the necessary population is restored and the monthly sacrifice of Gnosis is once again made to the caern’s totem. 

- Great caerns can increase in power; however, before one can do so, its caern heart must remain undamaged for a number of decades equivalent to the caern’s current rating. For example, a rating 2 great caern must be unmolested for twenty years before it’s potency raises to rating 3. 

- If a sept can gather five times the usual required monthly sacrifice of Gnosis, the great caern immediately increases by one rating, and it remains at this rating for one month. This type of increase is particularly useful when a sept is aware that an attack is imminent. 

## Great Caern Defenses 

Some Garou who hold positions within the sept, such as the Warder, have the authority to expend Renown to issue a _call to arms_ . This ability activates the following defenses for the caern: 

- A spiritual klaxon alarm is triggered. All members of the sept who have completed the Rite of Acceptance immediately hear the alert, up to 50 miles away. (As a reminder, a Garou can only be under the effect of a single Rite of Acceptance at a time.) Garou who have ventured beyond the Penumbra can’t hear the alarm until they have returned closer to the Material Realm. 

- Any guardian spirits attached to the caern rise from slumber to defend the bawn under the command of the Sept Leader, Warder, or another designated leader of the sept. 

Once per game session, for each increment of rating a great caern possesses, the caern totem can extend one of the following effects to any single Garou pack that is actively defending the caern. When applied, this benefit extends to any member of the pack bound through the pack’s totem, and all pack members receive the same benefit. Anyone who can issue a call to arms can direct the caern totem to grant the following benefits in a time of crisis: 

- **Guardian’s Expertise:** For the next five minutes, the target receives a +3 bonus to one skill chosen by the caern totem. The chosen skill should reflect the nature of the totem in some way. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- **Battlefield Promotion:** The target receives a +1 bonus to her Rank background, used solely for determining the Gnosis costs of gifts. This benefit has no effect on a Garou’s actual Rank, or on any mechanic beyond Gnosis costs. 

- **Protector’s Blessing:** The target receives access to a general gift of level 1, 2, or 3. For the next five minutes, the target can use the gift as though she’d purchased it. The chosen gift should reflect the nature of the totem in some way. 

## Great Caern Bawn Size 

The rating of a great caern determines the average size of its bawn. This limit can vary slightly as determined by the needs and location of the sept. Below are the average sizes for a great caern’s bawn. 

||**Great Caern Rating **|**Great Caern Bawn**||
|---|---|---|---|
||1|200 acres or less||
||2|201 – 350 acres||
||3|351-800 acres||
||4|801 – 1000 acres||
||5|1001 – 1500 acres||

## Destroying A Great Caern 

Destroying a great caern is not an easy task. The Dark Brigade managed to trick the Garou Nation by using a mystical plague that caught many werewolves by surprise. These fortified places of power are still vulnerable to attack in the Material Realm. 

A great caern can be destroyed if its totem spirit discorporates. In addition, the caern heart must receive aggravated damage equivalent to its total health levels. This damage shatters the caern stone and destroys the mystical power contained within it. 

## Moon Bridges 

Long ago, during the Age of Man, the Elders of the nation realized that there was a need for safe and swift passage between caerns. A _Moon Bridge_ is a path through the Penumbra that connects two caerns. A werewolf who travels via a Moon Bridge experiences this travel as riding a moonbeam through the spirit world to her destination. 

As the tribes united under the banner of the Garou Nation, caerns began to look to one another for assistance in times of great need, frequently with little or no warning. Recognizing the need for speedy assistance, a quest led a pack of Elders deep into the Aetherial Realm. Here, they found a rare item—a _Pathstone_ —that allowed Garou to move quickly between caerns. Using these Pathstones, the Theurges of the nation created the first Moon Bridge. These pathways became the primary mode of long-distance travel for werewolves, who relied on them for their speed and relative safety. 

As umbral storms ripped through reality during the dawn of the Age of Apocalypse, the Moon Bridges shattered. Garou who were traveling at the time plummeted into dangerous parts of the Umbra, and many have not been heard from since. Work has begun to reestablish these connections; however, there are far fewer Moon Bridges than there were before the start of the Age of Apocalypse. 

A Moon Bridge now acts as a connection between two great caerns or between a great caern and one of its shard caerns, offering travelers a swift and relatively safe means of transportation. Within minutes, a traveler can cross vast distances, although traversing the Umbra, even through these secure pathways, can be dangerous. At times, corrupted spirits attempt to attack a Moon Bridge in hopes of wreaking havoc on Garou. The Moon Bridge passes through the Umbra and into the Aetherial Realm, before eventually reaching its destination. 

In order to open a Moon Bridge, you must be at a caern. Additionally, the caern must have a Pathstone dedicated to the sept totem. A sept that wishes to secure a Pathstone must undertake a Complex Quest of difficulty rating 3 (see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Quests** , page 390). Pathstones are ritually attuned to the caerns to which they belong, and they cease to function if taken beyond the borders of the bawn. 

Garou at a great caern can open Moon Bridges to any other great caern, or to a shard caern that was seeded from it. Shapeshifters who begin their journeys at shard caerns, however, can only open Moon Bridges to the great caern that provided the shard caern’s shard seed. Additionally, the way must be open at the destination. Any caern is capable of independently opening and closing its end of the Moon Bridge’s connection. This access is typically controlled by the Warder, who may appoint a Theurge in charge of overseeing the Pathstone itself. The act of opening the way to send out travelers also requires that its destination is also open to receiving travelers.

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
## **Risk Versus Reward: Opening a Moon Bridge** 

Opening a Moon Bridge is a dangerous proposition since the return of umbral storms. Creating the spiritual connection required for travel necessitates lowering the defenses of both the points of origin and destination. This channel renders caerns more vulnerable to banes and other offensive spirits. With so few remaining great caerns, it is key to communicate with one’s destination before lowering such barriers in order to avoid serious repercussions. As a result, travel between caerns via Moon Bridge is far more uncommon than it once was, at least without very real assurances that both locations will be safe from any spirits attempting to take advantage of the opening. 

**
Should the way be open at the desired destination, either through arrangement, happenstance, or an act of sabotage, then the Moon Bridge can transport up to five travelers at once. These individuals arrive at the location of the destination’s Pathstone, which typically is found at the target sept’s waystation. Very often, visitors are met by the Warder or one of her assistants, and some other quantity of able-bodied Garou. Barring prior arrangement, travelers are expected to introduce themselves and disclose the purpose of their visit before leaving the vicinity of the Pathstone. 

## Shard Caerns 

_“If the great caerns sustain the living heart of Gaia, then the shard caerns are her life-support. Pray that they are able to endure until we can heal her of the Wyrm’s infection.”_ 

## —Thaddeus “Wired Heart” Fitzgerald, Child of Gaia 

A shard caern is a spiritual splinter of one of the last thirteen great caerns of the world, birthed from a shard seed, planted in a periphery. However, this new breed of caern is a mere echo of its progenitor caern. Shard caerns are weaker and more vulnerable to attack and destruction, but they retain some of the powers and abilities of a great caern. These lonely outposts represent hope for the future, for it is believed that if they are properly nurtured and protected, shard caerns will blossom into great caerns in time. 

Shard caerns are the result of planting a shard seed, grown from the caern stone of a great caern, within a periphery. From then on, the sept’s care and nurture of its seed determines how strong and powerful it can become. The more Gnosis poured into the shard, and the better it is protecteds from external dangers like umbral storms, Wyrm corruption, or physical attacks on the seed itself, the more likely it is that the shard caern will eventually grow and mature into a great caern itself. 

Shard seeds were developed through collaboration between the Garou and the Fera. With the aid of the Gurahl, Garou Theurges cast rituals at the remaining great caerns, granting them the power to grow seeds from their caern stones. These seeds can be safely removed from the caern stone, then planted within the Gnosis-rich environment of a periphery. Once planted, they begin to feed on that energy, growing stronger over months, years, and decades. 

Embraced by the Concordat of the Stars as proof that a partnership between the Garou and the other Changing Breeds is vital to the future of them all, shard caerns are a light of hope to those who found nothing but despair after the loss of so many caerns and the heroes who defended them to the very end. Even the Sanctum of Gaia cannot deny that more caerns could lead to reinvigorating Gaia and the other spirits that suffered devastating blows during the destruction of so many sacred sites. 

Shard seeds are rare, and growing them takes time and great effort. Each seed represents the trust of a great caern’s defenders, given to those who would seek to establish a new sept. Some Garou have kept these priceless artifacts to themselves, using them as political leverage to determine which septs might receive their own shard caern. However, some tribes have sought a new direction, forging alliances with Fera by gifting them these seeds. The recent accord between the Red Talons and the Rokea was made possible through the gift of a shard seed, and the Uktena are working with a number of other Fera, as well. 

Although shard seeds, and the shard caerns they become, have offered a new hope to the Garou Nation, they do not come without their own dangers. The seeds themselves are mobile, and even once they are planted, they are not truly safe. As scarce as they are, it is easy to give credence to the rumors of precious seeds being ripped from the soil, stolen, and repurposed for others’ use. Umbral storms, too, are powerful enough to ravage unprotected shard caerns, 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

shredding the Gnosis they have managed to gather. In some cases, the storms are powerful enough to completely destroy a poorly protected shard seed. 

## Mechanics of a Shard Caern 

Shard caerns represent a slim chance to reignite Gaia’s spirit and perhaps start a new age for the world. They are weak and must be protected and nurtured, lest the Wyrm turn them to its own purposes. This section details the creation and attributes of a shard caern. 

## Generating a New Shard Caern 

To create a shard caern, the Storyteller must first assign it a shard caern rating between 1 and 5. Newly created shard caerns start at level 1, and the first generation of shard caerns, created years ago, are now reaching level 5. This rating indicates the potency of the caern and help determine the following derivative elements: 

- All shard caerns are protected by a caern totem, whose rating is calcuated by adding 5 to the shard caern’s rating. For example, a shard caern with a rating of 2 has a caern totem spirit with a rating of 7. For more information, see **Chapter Fourteen: Allies and Antagonists: Totems** , page 632. 

- The caern heart is the sacred center of the shard caern. So long as this place is intact, the shard caern continues to heal and cleanse itself of all taint. The caern heart possesses a number of health levels equal to 5 x its shard caern rating. For more information about the caern heart, see **Chapter Eleven: The Garou Nation, The Sept, Workings of a Sept, Sept Geography** , page 526. 

- The totem spirit of a caern attracts other spirits to act as guardians. A shard caern has a number of guardian spirits equal to 2 x its shard caern rating. These spirits are rating 2 Jaggling spirit Stock NPCs. 

## Shard Caern Attributes 

A shard caern has the following attributes: 

- Shard caerns are Gaia’s spiritual life-support system, and Gnosis flows freely within their boundaries. Once a shard seed is planted, the newly awakened shard caern is thereafter able to tap into that spiritual energy and share it with others. A shapeshifter who meditates and rests at a shard caern uninterrupted for fifteen minutes replenishes all of her Gnosis. 

- So long as the caern totem remains healthy and the caern heart stays pure, the local Penumbra is protected from bane spirits. If the caern opens its Moon Bridge to receive visitors, this protection is temporarily weakened and bane spirits can enter the local Penumbra so long as the Moon Bridge is open for travel. 

- All shard caerns begin at a shard caern rating of 1. Every year that the shard caern continues to grow and absorb Gnosis, its rating increases by one increment, to a maximum of 5. After a shard caern has reached rating 5, if it maintains this rating for a full decade, it becomes a great caern with a great caern rating of 1. 

- The Rite of Acceptance binds members of a sept to the shard caern. This ritual permits the shard caern to maintain a constant flow of Gnosis, which it uses to strengthen itself and grow. As the shard caern’s rating increases, the shard caern requires more shapeshifters to be bound to it through this rite. To remain healthy, a shard caern must have a minimum population of Garou or Fera equal to 10 x its shard caern rating. These shapeshifters must be bound to the shard caern with the Rite of Acceptance in order to keep its totem spirit and the seed itself healthy and strong. If six months pass without the minimum population present, the shard caern’s power begins to drain. If this state remains for a year, the caern’s rating is reduced by 1. If a shard caern’s rating falls to 0 in this way, the shard seed dies, and the shard caern is lost. Conversely, if the population of the shard caern doubles within six months, this growth provides an abundance of Gnosis for the caern totem and the shard seed, lowering the time required for it to grow in strength by half. 

- When significant danger threatens, the ultimate defense of a shard caern is to remove its seed and escape with it. Unearthing the seed is traumatic, and this act immediately decreases the shard caern’s rating by 1. For every month the seed remains unplanted after being unearthed this way, its rating decreases again by 1. If this reduction causes the seed to reach rating 0, the shard seed dies, rendering it inert. 

- Umbral storms are particularly dangerous to shard caerns and the seeds within them. If a shard caern is exposed to an umbral storm more than three times within 12 months, it immediately decreases the shard caern’s rating by 1. If this reduction causes the caern to reach rating 0, the seed shatters, and the caern is lost.

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

||**Shard Caern Rating **|**Shard Caern Bawn**||
|---|---|---|---|
||1|25 acres||
||2|50 acres||
||3|100 acres||
||4|150 acres||
||5|200 acres||

## Shard Caern Defenses 

Some positions within the sept, such as the Warder, have the authority to issue a call to arms. This act activates the following defenses for the shard caern: 

- A spiritual klaxon alarm is triggered. All members of the sept who have completed the Rite of Acceptance immediately hear the alert, up to 10 miles away, and understand that the caern is under attack. Garou who have ventured beyond the Penumbra can’t hear the alarm until they have returned closer to the Material Realm. 

## Seeding a Shard Caern 

A shard seed embodies hope for the possibility of a future for Garou and Fera alike. These mystical glowing crystals, grown from the caern heart of a great caern, pulse with the gnostic energy they contain. Shard seeds appear to be rock crystals combined with Gnosis to represent two elements— the physical and spiritual—becoming one. When ritually planted, a shard seed takes root within the world and grows into a shard caern. 

- Guardian spirits rise from slumber to defend the bawn under the command of the Sept Leader, Warder, or another designated leader of the sept. 

Once per game session, for each increment of rating a shard caern possesses, the caern totem can extend one of the following effects to any single Garou who is actively defending the caern. Anyone who can issue a call to arms can direct the caern totem to grant the following benefits in a time of crisis: 

Shard seeds are roughly bowling-ball sized fragments of crystal, though each is unique in size, color, and shape. These fragments are vulnerable when they are out of the ground, cut off from the Gnosis they require to survive and grow. Until a shard seed is planted and sealed in the ground through the Rite of Furrowed Earth, it has a number of health levels equal to the Gnosis it has managed to accumulate. If it loses its health levels, or is somehow drained of Gnosis, the seed dies and becomes inert. 

- **Guardian’s Expertise:** For the next five minutes, the target receives a +3 bonus to one skill chosen by the caern totem. The chosen skill should reflect the nature of the totem in some way. 

- **Battlefield Promotion:** The target receives a +1 bonus to her Rank background, used solely for determining the Gnosis costs of gifts. This benefit has no effect on a Garou’s actual Rank, or on any mechanic beyond Gnosis costs. 

Only a periphery has sufficient gnostic energy to support the growth of a shard seed into a caern. A shard seed turns into a shard caern when shapeshifters attune the periphery’s Gnosis to local spirits, when they summon a spirit willing to bind with the shard seed and stand as its totem spirit, and when they seal that spirit to the shard seed using the Rite of Furrowed Earth. Once this process is complete, the sept must protect this fragile caern from the dangers it will inevitably attract. 

- **Protector’s Blessing:** The target receives access to a general gift of level 1, 2, or 3. For the next five minutes, the target can use the gift as though she’d purchased it. The chosen gift should reflect the nature of the totem in some way. 

## Shard Caern Bawn Size 

The most commonly used form of protection is the use of the Rite of the Shrouded Glen. This ritual protects the totem spirit bound to the shard caern, and also protects other spirits that live within the sept’s territory. It also ensures that the gift Taking the Forgotten is rendered useless if it is used on a shard seed, in the event it is unearthed. While spirits and rites are potent defenses, nothing protects a shard caern better than the Garou and Fera who bind themselves to its defense. 

The rating of a shard caern determines the average size of its bawn. This limit can vary slightly as determined by the needs and location of the sept. To the right are the average sizes for a shard caern’s bawn. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

To seed a shard caern, a sept must take the following steps: 

## Step One: Preparation 

There are four major tasks involved in preparing to establish a shard caern. Each must be completed before the ritual to plant the seed can begin. 

- Find a periphery for the shard caern 

- Obtain a shard seed 

- Clear the periphery of threats 

- Prepare defenses 

## **Preparation Task 1: Locate a Periphery** 

Peripheries are locations in the Material Realm where Gnosis is prevalent, due to thin spots in the Gauntlet. These places are valuable to more creatures than just werewolves, as the forces of the Wyrm or other denizens of the **World of Darkness** seek to claim this power for themselves. To seed a shard caern, the Garou must locate a periphery they can secure and protect. The sept should consider the following criteria: defensibility of the area in case of attack, the livability of the surrounding area for Garou and kin, and the privacy that can maintained to keep the Veil safe. 

Some septs attempt to cleanse and claim a Broken Land to establish a specific periphery. This goal can be achieved through a quest (see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Quests** , page 390), or as the focus of an adventure or chronicle. This plan serves a dual purpose, cleansing an area from Wyrm Taint and possible corruption, returning it to a periphery zone, while also providing a possible location for a shard caern outpost, ensuring the corruption doesn’t return. For more information on Broken Lands and peripheries, see **Chapter Thirteen: The Umbra: The Peripheries** , page 604. 

## **Preparation Task 2: Obtain a Shard Seed** 

The most important preparation is obtaining the shard seed itself. Without this object, it is impossible to build a shard caern. However, the acquisition of a seed can happen before or after discovering the site it in which it is to be planted. Shard seeds can be given as rewards to a pack that has impressed the leadership of a great caern. Dangerous and ambitious packs have even gone so far as to reclaim what they believe to be shard seeds mistakenly given to those unworthy to hold them. They may also attempt to repurpose seeds that they feel were wrongly denied to them due to political reasons. The potential threat of shard seed theft is something every sept worries about. For that reason, the 

seeds are carefully guarded, and werewolves often restrict access to their septs’ caern hearts. 

## **Preparation Task 3: Clear the Periphery of Threats** 

When a periphery has been selected as the prospective home of a new caern, the next necessity is ensuring that it is safe. Clearing the area of supernatural and mundane threats can take time and effort. This task involves packs scouring the area for any source or site of corruption. They set up preliminary defenses and homes for those who plan to live on the bawn and surrounding area. Threats from humans must also be identified and neutralized. It can take months to ensure that risks presented by sources ranging from hiking trails to cameras on ATM machines do not threaten the Veil or the safety of the shard caern. This process can tax the limited resources required by a budding sept. Its members must be available to do extra work, and they sometimes need to call in favors from outsiders to create the best possibility of success. 

## **Preparation Task 4: Prepare the Defenses** 

Once the initial preparations are complete, actual permanent defenses need to be created. Spirits are called to the area to patrol the Umbra, schedules are created for the physical defenders to patrol the Material Realm, and any mechanical or technological security is erected. The sept continues to gather resources and erect the other support buildings required to support daily life at a caern. 

## Step Two: Ritual 

The planting and actual creation of the shard caern is incredibly ritualistic. From the Rite of Furrowed Earth to the Rite of Acceptance, these steps must be completed flawlessly to ensure the health and wellbeing of a newlyborn shard caern: 

- Perform the Rite of Furrowed Earth 

- Summon the spirit that will serve as the caern’s totem 

- Perform the Rite of Acceptance to bind the initial sept members to the caern and its totem 

## **Ritual Task 1: Rite of Furrowed Earth** 

The Rite of Furrowed Earth is the first ritual performed, formally beginning the process of seeding a new caern. Not only does this rite help invoke the necessary spirits to support this process, but it also opens the earth itself to receive the seed. This ritual prepares the land, binding the Gnosis within the seed to the earth itself. The culmination of this ritual closes the land over the seed and seals it within. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

This is the most dangerous moment for all involved. Not only is the seed exposed, but the land itself is also rendered vulnerable to possible attack or corruption. An attack at this moment could damage or corrupt the land, wrest the shard seed from its owners so that others can spirit it away to be used or destroyed, and undo all the sept’s hard work. 

## **Ritual Task 2: Summon Caern Totem** 

The next requirement is the presence of a spirit that will serve as the totem for the new caern. Theurges usually spend extensive amounts of time and effort to find the perfect spirit for this role. This choice can divide sept members, as choosing the spirit that will represent the sept colors the environment in which they will live. A caern devoted to war is a far different place that one that is overseen by a spirit of enigmas or healing. 

Once it is identified and summoned, a spirit can require members of the prospective sept to perform services for it, earning its good will and active participation before it makes a lifelong commitment to the sept. The more chiminage paid to it, the much more likely it is that the spirit will be willing to live and grow with the sept. 

## **Ritual Task 3: Rite of Acceptance** 

Once the two prior tasks have been completed, those who are going to bind themselves to the caern must undergo the Rite of Acceptance in order to empower the new shard seed as well as its totem spirit. This rite is the final step in the ritual phase, but it is also the first step in the politics that are sure to come. It’s very easy for Garou to become caught up in the danger and adventure of initially setting up of a shard caern, but once the Rite of Acceptance is complete, sept members naturally try to determine where they stand within the new sept hierarchy. Some come together, already knowing where they stand when the task begins, while others seek to gain the power and prestige that comes with being part of such an undertaking. Even those united in purpose can change their minds when the chance to lead becomes available. 

Whomever casts the Rite of Acceptance often becomes the Master of the Rite, since she has a great deal of power at this particular stage of caern formation. She enables the binding of a member to sept, and thus determines pool of candidates for the eagerly sought positions. However, she can expect to be challenged by Garou who are wrongfully denied admittance to the sept. 

## Step Three: Completion 

Once the ritual requirements are complete, the political aspects of a sept come to the fore. This is the time when the Sept Leader is determined, after which Garou fill in 

the other important roles within the sept’s hierarchy. To complete the establishment of the sept, shapeshifters must: 

- Determine the Sept Leader 

- Fill out the remaining sept hierarchy 

The most important role within the sept is the Sept Leader. When a new caern is seeded, the question of who will lead is often either highly contested or predetermined. Commonly, all the Garou who wish to lead come together to challenge each other for position. In the best of circumstances, these challenges are performed honorably, and they may even comprise part of the celebration of a new caern’s founding. At worst, these challenges become a deadly brawl that anoints the freshly planted seed in blood and rage. Hierarchy is vital to both wolf and man. 

Once the Sept Leader is identified, officers for the rest of the sept’s leadership positions must be chosen. Garou might challenge one another for these roles, or they might be appointed by the Sept Leader. Often, moots are held to determine position-holders and to add to the celebratory nature of the success they have earned by seeding the caern in the first place. Participation in these events lifts weary spirits, allows all involved to see the talent and achievement of the members of the new sept, and offers Garou the chance to shine together. For more information on moots and ideas on how they can be used in challenges, see **Chapter Nine: Social Systems: Moots** , page 433. 

In small septs, some roles may remain vacant, or some Garou might have to pull double duty. This extra work causes a great deal of stress to members of the sept. Now that their great quest is resolved and the caern has been seeded, they must strive to keep what they worked and bled to obtain. Not having enough suitably well-trained warriors to serve in the necessary roles can aggravate tensions within a sept. At the same time, trying to serve in too many positions and still maintain the vigilance necessary to defend against the threats that threaten a new caern can strain even the most stalwart of Garou. 

## The Hope of a New Great Caern 

The Theurges of the Garou Nation believe that, in time, new shard caerns may blossom into great caerns. Theoretically, once a shard caern has reached a shard caern rating of 5 and has maintained its rating for a full decade, it will transition into a great caern with a rating of 1. When this change happens, the new great caern will be able to open Moon Bridges to all other great caerns, it will enjoy a significant boost in power, and it may even begin to grow shards seeds of its own. Of course, additional risks accompany these advantages. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## External Threats and Dangers to Shard Caerns 

As mentioned, shard caerns must be seeded within a periphery. This place is an area of spiritual power, and werewolves are not the only supernatural creatures to value such areas. Mages can draw power far more easily in these places. Fae find themselves more in tune with their inner selves here. Wraiths can even use such areas to effortlessly interact with the world of the living. More familiar, and often more devastating, forces allied with the Wyrm are always eager to corrupt these areas of power into Broken Lands and Wyrm Tunnels. Even the pyrrhic victory of cleansing a Wyrm Tunnel destroys a periphery, robbing the world of a potential place to reconnect the severed threads of gnostic energy. These are just a few of the many different dangers that a sept must guard against when seeding a shard caern. 

Another threat that can attract unwanted attention to a new shard caern is the Gnosis that emanates beyond the boundary of the bawn. Seeding a shard caern raises the ambient gnostic energy in the entire area. This increase can cause other peripheries to manifest nearby, raising the burden on the Garou and Fera to protect these territories. Compromised areas degenerate into Broken Lands, and Wyrm Taint allows them to manifest into full-fledged Wyrm Tunnels. A home with old stories of haunting might suddenly gain the attention of wraiths and specters. A popular stone monument associated with the legends of mythical creatures might prompt sighting of strange animals. Occurrences such as these can draw attention from curious third parties, from ordinary humans, to hunters, to other supernaturals. And these are just some sources of danger that can cause a newly created sept to feel the stress of too many problems and not enough bodies to deal with them. 

Even when some of these issues are resolved, the presence of a shard caern keeps the danger levels high. It can begin to feel as if the moment one issue is resolved, another is already threatening the peace and safety of a sept. Patrols must be run constantly to identify new dilemmas and to monitor those already known. The creative use of backgrounds, such as Contacts, Allies, and Kinfolk, can lead clever Garou to the potential threat of Wyrm infection before it grows too great to fight. When it comes to caern defense, very little effort can be spared. 

## Stealing a Shard Seed 

In the Age of Apocalypse, there are few sins as heinous as the theft of a shard seed. The rarity of their existence combined with the fragility of their physical shell makes merely attempting the act dangerous and malicious. While many werewolves can and do justify such an act, the truth remains that being caught stealing a shard seed from a correctly established shard caern should not only result in the perpetrators being accused of violating a caern, it could also spark another War of Rage. 

In order to successfully rob a caern of its seed, its totem spirit must first be rendered discorporate, as it will try to interfere with any attempt to unearth a seed. Defeating such a spirit is no easy task, as it is in the heart of its power. Then the seed itself must be physically unearthed and removed from the periphery in which the caern was located. Even all this is only half the battle, as no crime is complete until the getaway. A stolen shard seed can be sensed by those bound to it for a time after the theft, hopefully allowing the wronged werewolves a chance to reclaim their seed and restore their caern. For more information, refer to the Rite of Acceptance in **Chapter Eleven: The Garou Nation, Rites, Rite of Acceptance** , page 546. All told, stealing an unplanted shard seed is far easier than wresting one from its home within a shard caern. 

**
## **Pressure Forges Diamonds: Increasing Drama and Tension** 

Balancing the excitement of successfully creating a shard caern with increased pressure and danger enhances the drama of any story. Forcing players to make decisions about what can be done, what should be done, and what needs to be done allows their characters to develop and grow. Storytellers should be careful not to overload their players to such an extent that success is impossible, and you should generally seek to avoid crushing the newly formed caern under too many enemies and threats before the players have a chance to savor the success they’ve achieved. Applying some pressure, however, gives characters chances to grow in different ways as they make hard choices. This challenge creates personal and political drama, which enriches the stories in any chronicle. 

**
Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Nevertheless, a stolen shard seed can be used for one of three things: 

- **Establishment of a New Shard Caern:** A stolen seed can be implanted using the correct rituals detailed above, creating a new shard caern. Doing so severs all previous ties to the seed, requiring a new caern totem to be bound to it, along with any members of a new sept. 

- **Empowerment of an Established Shard Caern:** A shard caern can artificially grow in power by absorbing the gnostic energy and life-force of another shard seed. By placing a stolen shard seed within the caern heart of an established shard caern and casting the Rite of Furrowed Earth, the power and energy of the now-dead shard seed is absorbed into the new shard caern. This act immediately increases the rating of the established caern by half the rating of the stolen seed (minimum of 1). This process has no effect on a shard caern with a rating of 5. 

## Destroying a Shard Caern 

Although it’s never easy to destroy a caern of any variety, shard caerns are more fragile than their deeply-rooted parents, the great caerns. The very nature of shard caerns as outposts tends to leave them especially isolated. Frequently, they are found far from most help, and this makes them very vulnerable. 

A shard caern can be destroyed if its totem has been rendered discorporate and its caern heart receives aggravated damage equivalent to its shard caern rating. This destruction is only possible if the target caern’s seed is unearthed. If it is properly sealed under the earth by the correct rituals, it is invulnerable to damage until it is removed. However, should attackers manage to remove the shard seed from the soil’s protective womb and damage it sufficiently, the shard seed will shatter, and the mystical power contained within it will be destroyed. 

- **Destruction of the Stolen Seed:** Primarily a tactic of the Wyrm and its minions, removal of a shard seed from its sept makes the seed far easier to destroy. 

Chapter twelve: Caerns 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Monkey-Wrenching At Magadon Pharmaceuticals 

Plasma Soul leaned heavily into the impact of the improvised ram the fomori slammed against the only door into the room. He looked barely able to maintain the flimsy barrier. The mighty Ahroun winced from the hit, even as he spoke to the rest of his pack. “Hurry. That vine must have alerted them, not much time left.” 

“Just one more minute, I almost have it,” Progress Through Persistence, the Child of Gaia muttered, her fingers dancing across the keyboard, burrowing through layers of encryption and obfuscation while ignoring the told-you-so of her Pack Alpha. “Get ready; we’ll have to break out once I get the copy.” 

Somsak and Dee shared a smile. Phases one and two, recon and entry, had gone rather well, not counting the Blood Vine. Phase three, the actual retrieval, was getting sticky. Phase four though, actually escaping with the information—now that was gearing up to be exciting. 

Moving those vines had seemed insignificant until they had come to life, snapping and attacking until they were sliced to ribbons. If that had been their only obstacle, their cover would have remained intact, but the sap released a scent that seemed to call Wyrm minions like bees to pollen. Their predicament left them trapped in a tiny office with no visible means of escape except the door the Wendigo Alpha currently held shut with his weight and will. Somsak, the young Silent Strider, could barely contain her glee at the thought of the Renown and acclaim she’d gain when they were finally able to tell their story,  after escaping from a Wyrm-den, clutching information in hand, and leaving bodies of countless minions in their wake. 

“Got it! Everything they have on the chemical dumping we found…probably the cause of the Broken Lands the others reported, too,” Progress paused, considering the implications and possibilities generated by unlocking this information. “I think that we can fix this now. I hope,” she ended her speech in a whisper. A quick flick of her wrist sent the flash drive winging its way over to Dee. 

Plasma Soul, still pressing firmly against the door, which was quickly losing its integrity from the constant barrage, outlined the plan. “Dee, no matter what happens, your escape is key. If things go badly, you take the drive and get it back to the sept. This place is far too Wyrm-tainted to escape through the Umbra, so we’ll need to fight our way out, but we are going to do it on our terms.” 

The Wendigo glanced around, assessing their available resources. He stomped a foot hard against the ground and noticed the subtle tremors it provoked. He eyed the rest of his packmates with a discerning look. When he nodded to himself and a sly grin appeared on his lips, the rest realized that their Alpha had a plan. “When I say ‘now,’ we all instantly shift to Crinos. The weight should easily shatter the floor supports below us. Be ready for the fall. Then retreat fighting.” 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

“NOW!” A cacophony of destructive sound echoed through the lab, as the flooring of the second-story office gave way under the weight of four Garou in Crinos form. They could hear the slam of the door above them, opening from the sudden lack of resistance. Even as they struggled to recover from the planned drop, two men followed the Garou through the collapsed floor. The harsh sound of gunfire echoed in the small room as the two reflexively pulled their triggers, spraying bullets as they fell. 

Somsak growled, “Silver!” to his pack, his voice hissing in a combination of Rage and pain from the trail of fire the bullet left along his leg. Progress dived in front of Dee, protecting Dee from injury in accordance with their plan. Plasma Soul and Somsak leapt to engage the men before they could recover their wits. 

The two Garou winced in unpleasant surprise at the resistance they met when their claws struck home. They had wrongfully assumed that the two men were simple humans with guns. When the uniforms shredded under their claws’ assault, the torn fabric revealed black insectoid carapaces instead of skin. Even the normally collected Progress was further enraged by the presence of Wyrm-spawn, and she added her own claws to the violence. Somsak and Progress focused the full force of their strength against the one fomor who still struggled to regain his feet. 

The other fomor slipped from Plasma Soul’s grip. Covered in some supernatural lubricant, it screamed a warning to its fellows still within the building. It was the last sound he made, as Dee leapt from behind Progress and sliced through his throat with her claws. She yelped in surprise, blisters forming on her hand from the proximity to the dying fomor. Just then, the sound of more gunfire began to fill the room, as the other members of the first team unleashed a hail of bullets down at the Garou with little care for the fate of their still-living ally. 

Plasma grunted as he raised a heavy reception desk to shield the other members of his pack from the withering rain of bullets. “To the windows! Out! OUT! We battle outside this Wyrm and Weaver lair!” 

Thinking quickly, Somsak dived forward and grasped the hapless remaining fomor. He swiftly pivoted and launched the wailing creature back through the window with a textbook shoulderthrow. The Ahroun Silent Strider laughed at the look on the Wyrm minion’s face as he flew.  Somsak quickly motioned for Dee to be the first to escape the attempted trap. As the rest of his pack copied the young Homid, diving through a window that was just barely large enough for their Crinos forms to fit through, Plasma stood with the remains of the desk as a shield. 

Dee watched in dismay as three more fomori dropped into the room. Their descent was far more controlled than their previous fellows’, as they continued to spray continuous fire into the quickly fragmenting improvised shield. These new opponents were obviously far more disciplined than the fodder that had first fallen. Two of them fired in series to ensure they could reload without ever letting up the rain of bullets, while the third called for reinforcements on his radio. 

“Alpha Alpha, this is Bravo, release the hounds. Two down, repeat, two down. Release the damn pig-” his voice cut off as Plasma heaved the remains of the desk at the three of them, before diving through the window to rejoin his pack. 

The Wendigo swiftly looked around at the state of his pack, even as he heard strange grunting calls coming from somewhere to their left. Luckily few of the rounds within the fomori’s clips were actually silver, as his wounds were already beginning to close.  Even as the pack formed up, with Dee in the rear, covered by Progress and Somsak at his left, three pale boars raced into view, grunting savagely as they caught sight of the Garou. “Fighting retreat. Progress on Dee, Dee opportunity strikes only. Somsak, we are the wall.” The Wendigo wasted no time barking orders at his packmates, each falling into their roles in well-practiced haste. 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Dee moved out first, with her Child of Gaia Pack Beta close to her in case of ambush. The mission came first, no matter her desire to turn and rend the minions of the Wyrm into tiny chunks of quivering flesh. Behind her, she could hear the squeals and growls warning her that her tribemate and her Alpha had engaged the enemy, holding them back and away from their precious cargo. A scream of pain almost stopped her, as her tribal Elder, Somsak, was hurt, inflaming her Rage and fueling the desire to fight by his side. The powerful blow of Progress’s hand between her shoulder blades was the only thing that kept her momentum up. “Run pup. Run until you see sept,” the Child of Gaia’s voice was rough, and her paw felt pebbled with the strength of stone, as she invoked her contract with the spirits of the earth. “I will get the others.” 

Dee shifted to her Lupus form, the flash drive gently clutched between her teeth, as she began to run. The last thing she saw, with a quick glance behind her, was Progress flashing to the side of hobbled Somsak with inhuman speed, preventing the goring tusks of a Skull Pig from ending the life of her fellow Silent Strider. As the pained shrieks of Garou and Wyrm-minions alike rose through the air, Dee ran on. 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
_“Time and space, as we understand the concepts, are meaningless in a place forged from perspective.”_ — Verity Argyris, Black Fury, Historian 

Astronomers looked up into the sky at the shadow cast by the moon and named it the “umbra,” from the Latin word meaning “ghastly” or “shade.” The _Umbra_ is the spirit world, a shadowy reflection of the Material Realm, inhabited by spirits, banes, ghosts, and other ethereal beings. It exists as a distorted mirror of the Material Realm and serves as a bridge to a multiverse of mystical realms and realities known as the _Tellurian_ . 

The _Material Realm_ , which humans know as Earth, is separated from the Umbra by a mystical barrier known as the _Gauntlet_ . A werewolf can attempt to traverse this barrier by taking a moment to mediatate and connect to the Umbra. Having done so, she wills herself into the _Penumbra_ . The Penumbra is the spiritual mirror of the Material Realm, distorted by the lens of metaphor and perception. As the first layer of the Umbra, it is the one closest to the Material Realm. In places where the Gauntlet is weak, it has been known to bleed over and cause spiritual changes to the Material Realm. Such areas are known as _peripheries_ . Within a periphery, the normal rules of reality are broken and twisted as they intersect with the Umbra. Garou have come to call these areas _Broken Lands_ , and werewolves make it a priority to do whatever they 

can to repair the Gauntlet in these areas in order to protect the Veil. 

The Penumbra is full of hidden pockets and entryways into differing umbral realms. These _anchorheads_ grant access to secret places, though some are guarded or require keys in order to open them. Taking forms ranging from correct offerings to ritually spoken phrases, keys to different umbral realms are as varied as the laws of the realms themselves. The Penumbra is also home to fierce and unpredictable _umbral storms_ that rage through the umbral landscape, bearing spirits, banes, or even other travelers caught within the fierceness of their winds to unpredictable— and often dangerous—new terrain. 

Deeper within the spirit realm, the Penumbra transitions into the _Near Umbra_ . Here, traveling the roads within its realms are not easy, but they are not as dangerous as the pathways to the Deep Umbra. Realms like Atrocity, the Cyber Realm, Wolfhome, and the different Tribal Homelands are all accessible through entrances located within the Near Umbra. The Near Umbra’s realms are often destinations for quests seeking enlightenment and knowledge. Garou are also sometimes sent to these places to accomplish tasks on behalf of spirits or their totems. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Beyond the Near Umbra lies the _Deep Umbra_ . Here one can find connections to the farthest umbral realms, including hidden pathways to the Shadowlands, the Arcadia Gateway, the Aetherial realms of the Celestines, or dread Malfeas itself. All of these myriad places and parts of the Umbra are collectively known to the Garou as the Tellurian. 

## The Gauntlet 

The Garou call the mystical membrane that separates the Material Realm from the Umbra the Gauntlet. It is a powerful barrier between the world of spirit and the world of man, woven from countless strands of the Weaver’s webs and maintained by the myriad _net spiders_ that serve it. In some places, it has been worn thin or even into shredded fragments by the depredations of the Wyld or the Wyrm. These places allow the gnostic energy of the Umbra to penetrate into the physical world, and, like beacons, they draw net spiders that try and repair the breach. Sometimes, these tears are so vicious that they require werewolves’ aid to repair for long enough so that the spiders can succeed in their work; this assistance is considered a necessary evil, understood by even the most anti-Weaver Garou. 

A werewolf can attempt to pass through the Gauntlet into the Penumbra by taking a moment to glance into a reflective surface and meditating. Once a journey across the Gauntlet has begun, a werewolf cannot abort it, and she must ride out any consequences. While materializing, a werewolf resists attacks with her normal defensive pools, but she cannot take movement actions, cannot declare an intention to fair escape, and cannot use actions to make attacks herself. 

## Entering Into the Umbra 

The Gauntlet’s strength directly responds to the intersection of a werewolf’s personal spiritual affinity and that of the geography within the surrounding Material Realm. Lands connected to the Wyld, such as a forest, a river, a swamp, or other natural settings remote from human civilization, have a greater permeability for most Garou, and therefore it is easier for them to slip through the Gauntlet into the Penumbra while located in such places. Areas thick with the Weaver’s threads and technology are more difficult to traverse, and in lands tainted by the Wyrm, the Gauntlet is impossible to cross. 

Some Garou have developed a spiritual affinity to the Weaver and find it easier to cross the Gauntlet in places where technology is strong, such as atop a skyscraper, within a factory, or along a city street. For these werewolves, it is more difficult to slip through in places where the Wyld is strong. 

When crossing the Gauntlet, a werewolf can bring with her any inanimate objects on her person, as well as any she can carry in her hands. If she chooses to, she may also bring across willing individuals who are in physical contact with her at the time of crossing. A shapeshifter must pay an additional point of Gnosis for each willing target she carries across the Gauntlet. 

To cross the Gauntlet, a werewolf must spend 1 point of Gnosis and then spend a variable amount of time and effort focusing on a reflective surface. The time required to cross the Gauntlet and enter into the Umbra depends on the where the Garou is trying to pass, according to the chart on page 585. 

**
## **The Nature of Spiritual Affinity** 

A werewolf’s default spiritual affinity is Wyld. The merit Wyld Affinity (see **Chapter Six: Merits and Flaws, Sanctum of Gaia Merits, Wyld Affinity** , page 316) cements this natural connection, but it is not required for the purpose of entering and existing the Umbra. Garou who wish to switch their affinity to the Weaver must purchase the merit Weaver Affinity (see **Chapter Six: Merits and Flaws, Concordat of Stars Merits, Weaver Affinity** , page 316). 

**
