Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## **Gauntlet Chart: Entering the Umbra** 

**Location within Time the Material Realm Required** An area with a strong affinity to Gaia 1 Turn (such as a great caern or shard caern) An area strongly associated with the Wyld (or, conversely, one associated 3 Turns with the Weaver for characters with Weaver Affinity) A neutral area balanced between the Wyld and the Weaver, such as 6 Turns a city park with wi-fi or a building without it. 

An area with a strong spiritual affinity not directly associated with the werewolf’s personal affinity. For example, a werewolf with Wyld Affinity finds it more difficult to enter into the Umbra from a location associated with the Weaver. 

5 minutes 

A Wyrm-tainted area such as a corrupted forest, a fallen caern, or a Wyrm Tunnel. Only Black Spiral Dancers can cross the Gauntlet in Impossible such locations. Garou spiritually affiliated with the Wyld or the Weaver can never cross the Gauntlet in such a location. 

## Crossing the Gauntlet 

Garou can cross the Gauntlet immediately, without cost, at specific locations: 

- Areas with a strong mystical connection to Gaia, such as a caern (including shard caerns) 

- A spiritual affinity zone, such as the one temporarily created via the gift Circle of Gaia’s Cleansing 

- A Broken Land 

- Any location with which the werewolf is familiar (see **Chapter Five: Gifts, Familiarity with Target** , page 214), such as her home, work, or a regular hangout 

Garou are barred from crossing the Gauntlet in the following locations: 

- Any location that has been contaminated with Wyrm Taint 

- Any location that has been marked as personal domain by a supernatural predator. This act creates a spiritual membrane that prevents entry from the Umbra, unless the Garou in question is familiar with the location. (See **Chapter Five: Gifts: Familiarity with Target** , page 214.) 

- Warded locations that have been protected via mystical means, such as places shielded by Spirit Ward, the Haven background’s Occult advantage (see **Mind’s Eye Theatre: Vampire the Masquerade, Chapter Three: Character Creation, Backgrounds, Haven** , page 102), or a hedge ritual learned from the Occult skill. 

## Exiting the Umbra 

Returning to the Material Realm from the Umbra is a jarring experience. The spiritual side of a Garou does not easily relinquish the presence of such gnostic energy, Garou find it difficult to return to the dreary mundane. The Umbra does not lightly return that which it claims as its own. 

Crossing the Gauntlet from the Umbra back into the Material Realm can be challenging, and doing so leaves a werewolf vulnerable if she hasn’t prepared ahead of time. The same difficulties that present themselves when crossing into the Umbra can also cause issues when attempting to exit it. The Gauntlet’s strength varies in relation to where a werewolf attempts to exit. If a werewolf tries to cross the Gauntlet in a place that is more resistant to her, the passing can take longer. This additional time can lead to danger, if any enemies happen to be present when she begins to cross back into the physical world. 

Returning to the Material Realm is dangerous. Without proper precautions, a traveler can materialize inside of a rock or surrounded by enemies. Crossing the Gauntlet requires a werewolf to locate an area of the Penumbra near the Material Realm with an appropriate spiritual affinity. Garou can naturally sense and locate such places. 

To exit the Umbra, a werewolf must first expend a standard action peeking across the Gauntlet (see **The Gauntlet: Peeking Across the Gauntlet** , below, page 586) to attune herself to the Material Realm; then, she can cross without getting lost or injured. A werewolf who risks the crossing without looking first may find herself caught in an umbral storm (see **Umbral Storms** , page 603). The time required to physically cross the Gauntlet and enter into the Material Realm depends on the where the Garou is trying to pass, according to the chart on the next page. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## **Gauntlet Chart: Exiting the Umbra** 

**Location within the Time Required Material Realm to Materialize** An area with a strong affinity to Gaia (such as a great caern Immediately or shard caern). An area strongly associated with the Wyld (or, conversely, one associated with the 1 Turn Weaver for characters with Weaver Affinity). A neutral area balanced between the Wyld and the Weaver, such as a city 3 Turns park with wi-fi or a building without it. 

An area with a strong spiritual affinity not directly associated with the werewolf’s personal affinity. For example, a werewolf with Wyld Affinity 5 Turns finds it more difficult to enter into the Material Realm within a location associated with the Weaver. A Wyrm-tainted area such as a tainted forest, a fallen Caern, or a Wyrm Tunnel. Only Black Spiral Dancers can cross the Gauntlet in such Impossible 

A Wyrm-tainted area such as a tainted forest, a fallen Caern, or a Wyrm Tunnel. Only Black Spiral Dancers can cross the Gauntlet in such locations. Garou spiritually affiliated with the Wyld or the Weaver can never cross the Gauntlet in such a location. 

## Peeking Across the Gauntlet 

A werewolf in the Penumbra can spend 1 point of Gnosis to peek across the Gauntlet and view her corresponding location in the Material Realm. The target location must match the Garou’s spiritual affinity. She can view the location for five minutes for each point of Gnosis spent. This act attunes the werewolf to the target location, helping her cross the Gauntlet without getting lost or injured, but it does not provide Familiarity (see **Chapter Five: Gifts: Familiarity with Target** , page 214). 

Peeking across the Gauntlet allows werewolves to view what is happening in the Material Realm while remaining safely within the Umbra. Peeking requires concentration, and a werewolf cannot move or fight without losing her connection. If she loses her concentration, she may resume it, as long as the initial five-minute duration has not expired. Resuming peeking does not require an additional expenditure of Gnosis. 

Werewolves cannot use gifts, fetishes, or supernatural powers to affect the other side of the Gauntlet, unless the power specifically grants her the ability to do so. For example, a Garou can activate Wolf Senses to heighten her sight, because that power specifically states that she may do so. However, she cannot throw fire through the Gauntlet at a target using Master of Fire. Peeking also cannot be used to obtain Gaze and Focus or Familiarity with a target, unless a power specifically states that it allows a werewolf to do so. 

## The Penumbra 

The Penumbra is the layer of the Umbra closest to the Material Realm. It coexists in the same physical space, but in slightly different dimensions, allowing for limited interaction between the two realms. This section of the Umbra can be spiritually enlightening or horrifically terrifying, depending upon a traveler’s journey. 

## Mechanics of the Penumbra 

The Penumbra has several effects that are persist consistently throughout it. Humans, unless spiritually enlightened, aren’t found here. In addition, time passes as it does in the Material Realm. This section contains special mechanics for each of the Penumbra’s realms, as well as guidelines for creating custom domains. 

Garou in the Penumbra can: 

- Exit the Umbra by crossing the Gauntlet (see **The Gauntlet: Exiting the Umbra** , page 585) 

- Spend 1 point of Gnosis to peek through the Gauntlet, viewing the physical realm at any location she could also cross the Gauntlet to leave the Umbra (see **The Gauntlet: Peeking Across the Gauntlet** , to the left). 

- While in the Penumbra, minor cosmetic alterations may be revealed, reflecting a Garou’s true nature, such as a Fianna’s fur glimmering with faerie magic or a Shadow Lord seeming to exude shadow. The effect is always cosmetic and does not provide a character with any benefits. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- Garou can spend Gnosis, at a rate of one per week, to sustain themselves in the Penumbra. Other creatures are rarely able to find sustenance within the spirit realms, and supernatural creatures that subsist on blood are unable to feed unless they bring humans into the Penumbra with them. If a creature starves to death while in the Umbra, it becomes a spiritual reflection—such as a Garou ancestor spirit, or a vampiric bane spirit. 

The spirits found in the Penumbra are primarily Jagglings and Gafflings. Incarna are very rarely seen here, unless they control a part of a realm in the Penumbra. In addition, although silvery light dimly permeates the entire Penumbra, supernatural creatures that are subject to penalties or bonuses based on the presence of sunlight are affected by sunlight as they would be in the Material Realm. 

## Geography of the Penumbra 

The Penumbra is the spiritual echo of the Material Realm, a reflection of not only its physical locations, but also its metaphysical scars and auras. While familiar locations and landmarks may exist, they are fueled by the emotions and actions of others. In the Penumbra, a mansion of an otherwise-respected city official in the Material Realm may appear as a twisted, yawning hell-mouth, revealing her secret transgressions against others. The simple flower garden of a child blossoms into wild jungle, beaming comfort to all within its walls. Anything that can impact the Material Realm with its spiritual energy impacts the Penumbra with its presence. 

Zones that correspond to the real world are known as _domains_ , areas of the Penumbra marked by the spiritual energy of others. Any strong emotion or impactful event can appear as a corresponding domain in the Penumbra. Ancient redwood forests appear as serene glens, toxic waste dumps as Wyrm-infested hellholes, or corporate buildings as web-covered glass and steel. 

The geography of the Umbra is mutable, and recent events have seen realms from the Near Umbra move closer to the real world, forming connecting domains in the Penumbra. 

## The Battleground 

In the onset of the Age of Apocalypse, even the most peaceful Garou came to understand the brutality of war and its consequences. As they raged in battle, their blood soaking into the ground, the realm known as the _Battleground_ seeped into the Penumbra. The Battleground 

plays a part in every tribe’s stories as the embodiment of all conflict since the dawn of time. On its fields, every war continues on and pays no heed to the boundaries of history—Spartans battle American Civil War soldiers on the same fields where the War of the Roses took place, remnants of tanks and catapults surrounding them. 

Only one thing is constant as the battles ebb and flow into one another: the _Signpost_ . The Signpost lists various battles, with various arrows on the sign pointing in the direction of specific fights, allowing Garou to find a specific battle and join in the bloodshed. The campaigns vary, and they sometimes even include personal skirmishes of the traveling Garou. If they continue on the track, they may eventually come across the only silent location in the Battleground: the Plain of the Apocalypse. This eerie location is completely flat, with little grass, and it is not tainted by the debris or battles found in the rest of the Battleground. Theurges believe that, if there is a last battle, it will occur here. Three armies, one each dedicated to the Wyrm, the Weaver, and the Wyld, struggle against each other at its borders. They never gain supremacy over the field, but nonetheless fight viciously to possess it. 

Garou who come to the Battleground often choose to hone their skills by sparring with the other combatants or by observing the conflicts to find new tactics. Those accused of cowardice or ineptitude often come to the Battleground to prove themselves. No matter their personal cause, all come to the Battleground with bloodshed on their minds. 

Entering the Battleground can occur by crossing the Gauntlet, but shapeshifters who have 2 or more points of Rage do not need to peek across the Gauntlet in order to safely arrive there. A Garou can exit the Battleground, either into the Material Realm or the Near Umbra, by winning a fight with its inhabitants. They can also leave the realm by dying in battle, allowing them to reappear in the Material Realm. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** The Battleground offers a unique opportunity to show the other sides of important battles and conflicts. A Garou in the Battleground can see and live through the experiences of those on opposite sides, and perhaps better understand the participants’ motives. It also offers chance to train and grow by fighting in numerous conflicts, both historic and relatively unknown. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Characters in this realm gain 1 additional point of Rage each turn on her Everyman initiative. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- Characters in this realm automatically fail any test to resist anger frenzy, unable to shake the overwhelming fury of the battlefields surrounding them. 

- Armor or weapons from this area can only be used in the Penumbra or Near Umbra, and have normal weapon or protective gear qualities. They can be used in defense or to attack spirits. 

- Characters in this realm cannot be killed in the traditional sense. If they die in the Battleground, they are shunted to the Material Realm and lose access to 2 points of Gnosis for the rest of the game session. 

## Cyber Realm 

Webs of steel, glass, concrete, and circuitry form the structures and inhabitants of this realm, layers upon layers of technology smother anything associated with the Wyld. Modern obsession with technology first bridged connections between this realm and the Penumbra, until its urban sprawl finally spilled completely over. 

Spider City most closely resembles modern cities—though its technology seems based on the sketches of mad scientists and hopeless engineers. Hover-cars and steam-powered carriages pollute the _Cyber Realm_ with equal fervor. Even its class system, strictly dividing the uptown privileged from the old town undesirables, carries echoes of the real world. The Pit extends under Spider City: a labyrinthine dump of discarded dreams, broken machinery, and living refuse. The Computer Web is a network of raw data, flowing in and out of the other areas in binary streams that can be tapped by those unafraid to tangle with the Weaver. 

The Cyber Realm stifles the Wyld, attempting to replace anything living with an improved mechanical counterpart. Glass Walkers occasionally seek out this realm, seeking meditation in an environment where they truly feel at home. Most other Garou, however, use it to obtain a piece of technology or to pay “Juice,” liquefied Gnosis, to one of the many Weaver spirits that infest the area in exchange for information. These werewolves desire to get in and get out of Cyber Realm as quickly as possible, before they are upgraded. 

Entry to the Cyber Realm is less difficult now than it was in the past. When it expanded its borders into the Penumbra, the patrolling robots and spider spirits found the perimeter much more difficult to maintain. Most Garou need to only avoid one of these patrols to enter. Exiting the realm is far more difficult, usually requiring paying one of the residents Juice to be smuggled out or navigating the mazelike conditions of the Pit to find an entry point to the Near Umbra or an exit to the Material Realm. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Garou use the Cyber Realm for everything from obtaining information from Weaver spirits, who are able to pierce the deepest levels of protected technology, to giving those with Weaver Affinity a place to mediate on that connection. The Cyber Realm is not exactly hostile to werewolves, but neither is it safe. It is a diffuse reflection of what the home of the Weaver itself might be. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Characters with the Weaver Affinity merit do not have to spend Gnosis to activate their gifts in this realm. 

- Characters with the Wyld Affinity merit cannot spend Willpower to retest failed challenges in this realm. 

## Wolfhome 

Some Garou consider it suprising that _Wolfhome_ has been pulled closer to the Material Realm, believing its requirement that travelers become closer to the beast to enter it a product of a bygone era. Others believe that in the Age of Apocalypse, the one thing Garou understand is being humbled and hunted. When the siege on the great caerns first began, Wolfhome appeared as a pocket realm in the Penumbra, which slowly spread as the Garou population dwindled, until it was fully cemented in the Penumbra. 

In Wolfhome, all shapeshifters are forced to take their animal form and assume the mindset of a hunted creature. They are unable to read signs or perform normal human functions, forced instead to rely on animal instinct and wits in order to survive. Other animal spirits live in Wolfhome and can communicate freely with Garou, advising them of human threats and telling them to avoid “The Town”—the central location where the realm’s humans live. Every abuse brought upon animals by humans is represented here, from the hunt to animal cruelty and experimentation. Shapeshifters can find themselves in shelters (filthy kennels), the zoo (to be jeered at and prodded), or in camps (experimental laboratories). If a shapeshifter is lucky, she may escape to the countryside, where she can be free, but only if she can escape roving bands of hunters. 

Some Garou come to Wolfhome to learn more about their primal natures and how to survive in the most harrowing of circumstances. Others, particularly those who shun their dual nature or who are abusive to kinfolk or spirits, are sent to the realm to learn humility and to come to an understanding of their animal instincts, forced by their circumstances to feel what their animal kin may feel. Sometimes, a shapeshifter enters the realm by accident or because of spiritual transgression and must be rescued by her pack or sept. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Those Garou who go looking for an entrance to Wolfhome can easily find it; however, a werewolf rarely enters Wolfhome willingly. The spirits, or some say Wolfhome itself, can punish an arrogant Garou by slightly extending the borders of Wolfhome while the Garou is already in the Penumbra. To exit the realm, a Garou must fully understand what it is to be an animal—from its instincts to its fears. When this understanding occurs, as determined by the Storyteller, a Garou can then exit this realm. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Wolfhome presents opportunities for Garou seeking to learn from their animal natures, both voluntarily and not. Some are sent to this realm as punishment, forced to stay in Wolfhome until they humble themselves and remember they are also beasts. Others already understand their need to become closer to their animal natures, and come voluntarily to Wolfhome to better epitomize both wolf and man. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- In this realm, all shapeshifters are forced into their animal form. Garou take their Lupus form. Fera take on their equivalent form. Shapeshifters cannot change out of this form while in Wolfhome. 

- Characters in this realm cannot understand human written language and have a difficult time deciphering spoken language. 

- In order to use gifts in Wolfhome, a Garou must spend an additional point of Gnosis. Characters cannot use rites in Wolfhome. 

- All spirits not bound to a fetish or other item cannot enter Wolfhome. 

## Pocket Realms 

Like domains, _pocket realms_ are created by strong emotions and needs. For many Garou or Fera, these realms pop into being long enough to hide within or to provide shelter from an umbral storm. They appear erratically, and their nature is transient. They can appear suddenly in the wake of a traumatic event and disappear just as quickly. 

Fera also used these realms for shelter and escape, having mastered the process of stabilizing them for those purposes. However, since the advent of umbral storms, many of these pocket realms were destroyed, and all except the Ananasi have been unable to recreate them with any consistency. 

## How to Build a Custom Domain 

Domains in the Penumbra usually correspond to real world locations, places with strong emotional history or other impactful memories. The locations in the Penumbra show the spiritual truth of the real world they reflect. When building a custom domain in the Penumbra, keep the following tips in mind: 

- Determine which real world location, event, or issue the Penumbra domain reflects. This place can be anything from an actual location, like a historical building, to an event such as an exuberant political rally gone terribly awry. The domain should have a theme that brings together its elements. 

- Describe the domain. What does it look like? How do the scenery, mood, and NPCs in the domain support the theme? What spiritually enlightened people may appear here? 

- Determine how a character enters and exits the domain. 

- Create some specific ways in which the domain limits or amplifies the various aspects of characters within it. 

**Example:** _Jason is running a scene for his players. They are attempting to obtain a fetish that is part of an evolution exhibit at the Natural History Museum, and they have decided to sneak into the building by going through the Penumbra. Jason decides to create a domain named “Origin,” which is based on the theme of transformation. He determines the players can enter this domain by changing their form while holding onto a copy of the book_ Origins of the Species _. This act masks them as a changing thing that can easily avoid detection. When they approach the building, he describes the structure as a living entity, constantly growing new limbs or additional eyes, just to have them dissolve into some other appendage. Even the air around outside the reflection of the museum smells as though it is electrified, causing the characters’ skins to tingle and itch, as though they too should change._ 

_Inside of the building, a group of Gaffling spirits have manifested as bipedal, intelligent chimpanzees. They spend their time carefully monitoring and maintaining the exhibits, which are animated and able to speak, trying to shout down each other and their caretakers by reciting their histories. The players determine the fetish they are looking for is guarded by an equally intelligent Jaggling spirit-ape; however, the large primate has reached the end of her rope with the exhibits’ constant harassment, and it has little desire to barter or broker a deal. Once the characters obtain the fetish, they are able to exit the domain by rejecting the transformation offered, changing to their Homid form and tearing out the pages of the book they used to enter._ 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

_While in the domain, Jason has decided shifting forms requires no expenditures of Gnosis. He also determines that when the characters shift forms, they have no control over what form they take next. For example, a Garou may go into Hispo when she intended to be Crinos, and she can only continue shifting forms until she gets to the desired one._ 

## The Near Umbra 

The Near Umbra lies between the Penumbra and the Deep Umbra, a place dotted by numerous realms that orbit the Material Realm. Navigating the Near Umbra is not always safe, since the realms, domains, and vistas constantly swirl around its center. The Near Umbra is especially turbulent in modern times, and travelers have discovered some of its realms have been flung to the far corners of the Penumbra and Deep Umbra. To enter the Near Umbra from the Penumbra or Material Realm a Garou must complete a complex quest with a difficulty rating of 1, and the applicable consequences for a complex quest apply (see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems: Quests** , page 390). 

Some of the aspects of the Near Umbra include: 

- Domains, realms, and zones are more dangerous and are elevated extensions of realms and domains in the Penumbra. 

- The _Tribal Homelands_ , idealized versions of the concept of home for each of the tribes, reside in the Near Umbra. 

- _Vistas_ appear in the Near Umbra. These are realms that Garou cannot enter, but they can view them, as though looking through a picture window. 

- The Near Umbra’s inhabitants are primarily Jagglings and Gafflings. Incarna frequently claim territory in the Near Umbra, using its realms as an extension of their power from the Deep Umbra. 

## Mechanics of the Near Umbra 

This section contains special mechanics for each of the Near Umbra’s realms, as well as guidelines for creating custom realms. 

The mechanics of the Near Umbra are as follows: 

- Even if a realm doesn’t specify a requirement for entry, the Storyteller may apply specific requirements to enter certain Near Umbra realms, such as having a member of one’s travelling party currently possessing Wyrm Taint in order to enter Scar. 

- While in the Near Umbra, the minor cosmetic alterations reflecting a Garou’s true nature become even stronger than in the Penumbra. For example, a Bone Gnawer’s fur may become covered in a soot that cannot be washed away, and the scent of fresh asphalt may linger around her. 

- Garou can spend Gnosis, at a rate of 1 point per week, to sustain themselves in the Near Umbra. Other creatures are rarely able to find sustenance within the spirit realms, and supernatural creatures that subsist on blood are unable to feed unless they bring humans into the Near Umbra with them. If a creature starves to death while in the Near Umbra, it becomes a spiritual reflection—such as a Garou ancestor spirit, or a vampiric bane spirit. 

## Geography of the Near Umbra 

The Near Umbra consists of numerous realms, domains, zones, and vistas, which are like planets that orbit around the Material Realm. There is no map that points a Garou in the right direction. The environment is too erratic— shifting, dilating, and shrinking—to ever efficiently navigate via the same path twice. 

Realms in the Near Umbra formed by the mingling of the spiritual energy of the Material Realm with the powerful energies of the Wyld, Weaver, and the Wyrm. These realms developed complex underlying laws of matter and energy that govern them and, in many ways, fuel them. Astute shapeshifters prefer to remember these capricious mandates rather than the realm’s physical locations, finding the laws more helpful in maneuvering through the Near Umbra. 

## Atrocity 

The damage and pain inflicted on others is not a brief moment in time, delivered then done. The _Atrocity_ realm exists as reminder that, although wounds may heal, scars remain on a creature’s soul, on her life, and on the lives of others. In this realm, every victim still suffers, her physical and spiritual wounds bleeding into the air around her. Every victimizer stands tall, waiting to bring more pain. Every horror brought on another—animal, human, or supernatural—plays out again and again. 

Tainted heavily by the Wyrm, the air of Atrocity reeks overwhelmingly of the coppery scent of blood and bile. Screams of tortured agony interrupt coherent thought. Atrocity is the primary spawning pool for banes, and their terrifying larvae prowl the maze-like corridors of flesh, bone, and bodily waste, ready to strike out and devour any trespassers. Shapeshifters who must travel through this realm 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

are forced to watch horrific violations and victimizations of others, unable to stop the massacre for more than a few moments. A Garou with a dark past may find herself face-to-face with her crimes, her atrocities playing out on gruesome repeat in front of her companions. 

Despite a natural desire to avoid this place, many shapeshifters find themselves here. Some are forced to come to terms with brutal crimes they committed and examine them in order to find a different path into their futures. Others come here for information and to determine the nature of another’s violation, so they can arm themselves with this information, using it in the Material Realm to bring an end to crimes against others. Atrocity is a place where suffering is not a secretive, furtive act, and many find there are lessons to learn here. 

Unlike many other realms, Atrocity doesn’t require deliberate effort to enter. A guilty conscience, inquisitive mind, or vengeful heart can all lead a Garou to this realm, especially if they are already traveling through the Umbra. However, leaving is not as simple as thinking happy thoughts. Gifts are ineffective for leaving Atrocity, and other pathways out of the realm are non-existent. In order to leave the realm, a Garou must take on the role of a victim, suffering as they suffered, and dying at the hands of one of the vicious emanations in the area. The empathic connection in death allows a Garou to wake up in another realm or in the Material Realm. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** The realm of Atrocity can be used as a teaching tool or as a punishment, as Garou witness the deepest sins the Wyrm inflicts on others or see their own sins reflected back at them. Stories about personal adversity and discovering the true depths of an accused’s crimes all fit well with Atrocity as a backdrop. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- The area is infested with spiritual echoes of victimizers and their victims. These spiritual echoes cannot physically cause damage to Garou; however, if a Garou dies at their hands, she is considered to have empathized with the victim and can use this act to leave the realm. 

- A Garou can be injured by other shapeshifters, other visitors from the Material Realm, or any one of the banes that prowl the area. Garou take normal damage based on the character type attacking them, and if they lose all of their health levels, they can choose to leave Atrocity and exit into the Penumbra. 

- A Garou who comes to Atrocity witnesses her own crimes against others first. She may witness multiple scenes of her transgressions. During each new scene 

or instance of witnessing her personal crimes, a Garou must spend a point of Willpower to avoid entering fox frenzy. During each new scene while witnessing another’s crimes, she must spend a point of Willpower to avoid entering anger frenzy. 

- When witnessing her own atrocities against another, a Garou can attempt to atone for her crimes. She can do this by performing the Rite of Contrition after witnessing her crime. If she does this, she regains all her spent points of Willpower, her atrocity disappears from the realm, and she is not haunted by it if she returns. 

- Garou in this realm are overwhelmed by sensory information from the scent of blood and the cries of the anguished. Those who enter this realm lose their Mental focus for the duration of the time they are in the realm. 

## Dream Zone 

The collective dreams of humans, supernatural beings such as shapeshifters, spirits, and animals create the landscape of the _Dream Zone_ . This realm allows Garou to explore both the familiar nature of their own dreams and the primal fears, needs, and desires that run through the souls of every creature, whether made of flesh or spiritual essence. The Dream Zone is like a gem, with myriad facets to discover and delve into. 

The geography of the Dream Zone is ever-changing, shifting from the personal dreams of individuals to those of the collective consciousness. Each dream is a postmodern story, with the chain of events often difficult to understand, but they are meant to be explored and examined with the hope of being understood. As each of these stories play out to their end, a new facet of the Dream Zone is revealed. These dream-quests are the primary reason to enter the Dream Zone, as they offer an exploration of ideas and thoughts, mingling a Garou’s personal subconscious with that of more primordial ideas. Sometimes problems spring up that require more introspection than battle, and the Dream Zone suits that purpose well. 

To enter the Dream Zone, a Garou must go to sleep in the Penumbra—a dangerous exercise of trust and vulnerability. Leaving the Dream Zone is easier, as a Garou must only wake up or complete her dream-quest. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** The Dream Zone is an excellent setting for stories of prophecy and omens. Interpretations of events, viewed through a dreaming eye, are reflected in such a way that they offer a powerful backdrop for foreshadowing upcoming plotlines in your game. Events in the Dream Zone also clarify things that have happened in a character’s past. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Characters slain in the Dream Zone do not die in the Material Realm, but suffer a loss of 1 point of Willpower for each death they experience while there. 

- The Dream Zone offers a number of ways to slip unnoticed into other realms in the Near Umbra. A character who wishes to enter another realm from the Dream Zone must locate a dream-facet that ties to the target realm, risking a possible death in the process, and then spend 1 point of Gnosis to cross over to the desired realm. 

## Erebus 

In the Umbra, Heaven and Hell are real places, even if they mask themselves with different names and aspects. Some are damned. Some are destined to ascend. Others are caught in between, meant to purge their wicked deeds through suffering, and eventually, understanding. Those Garou who acknowledge that their sins are great, but understand that their need for absolution is greater, travel to the purgatory realm of _Erebus_ , a realm where molten silver rivers and lakes shimmer and hiss in blackened shale banks—where the Garou come to burn their souls clean. 

Erebus is ruled by an Incarna known as Charyss, a creature of many forms and moods. While many belligerent Garou see her as a Wyrm spirit, she is neutral in the affairs of the Triat. Purifying Garou is her sole concern. She is served by spirits known as the Brood: an army of hairless Crinos werewolves with black or red skin who torment the unfortunate souls sentenced to burn in Erebus. Those werewolves not under their power must still avoid the rewards they offer, such as servitude and legendary fetishes, which they bet to lure Garou into playing games with them. Wary Garou know not to play any game where the rules are set solely by their enemy, because losing means burning in the molten rivers until the terms set by the Brood are met. 

All rivers in Erebus lead to a reservoir in the center of the realm known as the Silver Lake. A fortress stands on the island in the middle of the lake: the Citadel of Charyss. This lake does not burn, but remains as a place of calm and comfort in a realm otherwise meant for torment. Those who wash up on the shores of the fortress are granted a piece of knowledge from Charyss herself: that Erebus can cleanse the body, but redemption of soul comes only at the behest of penitent Garou. 

Garou come here for one reason: to purify themselves of their sins by going through the Silver Forge. They must fully immerse themselves in the molten liquid and 

suffer the agony of its touch until they are cleansed of their wrongdoing. As their sins are purged in the silver rivers or lakes, the penitents suffer hallucinations of their past deeds, living them out over and over again. Even Black Spiral Dancers, perhaps seeing their corruption for what it is, can come here to ignite their souls, burn away their trespasses, and rejoin the Garou in their fight. Fera cannot enter Erebus, but they have their own domains where they suffer as the Garou do, in ways appropriate to their breeds. For example, a Corax may burn in golden cages, sacrificing her freedom and gossip until she has achieved purification. 

Erebus is a realm Garou seek with purpose. To reach it, they can follow the Silver Road until they reach the gates of the realm. Cerebus, the three-headed dog of Greek myth, guards the gates, each of his heads representing an aspect of the Triat. He does not bar entry; he only guards against premature escapes, and he will kill any creature that dares to attack him. To exit, a Garou must find her way to the Citadel. Within its walls lies a portal to other realms. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Erebus is a place of cleansing sought when every other attempt for earning forgivness has failed. Anyone who has recently undergone purification can learn the truth of redemption from Charyss herself, as she trusts that they speak truthfully when they claim they are innocent. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Those who attack Charyss or one of her Brood, except when invited to do so as part of a challenge, are immediately transported to burn in Erebus’ silver rivers. 

- In this realm, aggravated damage inflicted in pursuit of penance is regenerated through the same means as normal damage. Characters immersing themselves in the molten silver receive aggravated damage, but heal an equal amount of damage in the same turn. They are in purgatory, and anguish is more important than death. 

- There is no light in this realm but the glow of molten silver. Characters with the Perception attribute focus lose access to any benefits of this focus, including those related to exceptional successes. 

- All Wyrm Taint can be cleansed in Erebus, including the fifth stage. Storyteller discretion determines on how long this purging takes. 

- Black Spiral Dancers can be cleansed in Erebus, but they lose all access to gifts and rites. They start anew, as Cubs from a tribe of their choosing. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Flux 

The Wyld is a force of creation, its presence emanates energies that trigger chaos and change. The _Flux_ realm represents the epitome of the Wyld’s mutable nature: a realm where geography constantly forms and then dissolves, flesh constructs and deconstructs, and the laws of physics are suggestions meant to be flouted. Time, weather, forms, and locations are all erratic. Nothing is stable and everything changes; the Flux realm takes this adage as its sole rule and guiding principle. 

Its primary inhabitant is a Wyld Incarna known as the Coil. Embodying the realm, it changes form and features with ease, causing earthquakes or umbral storms with a wave of its arm. As a manifestation of Wyld energies, it can appear as a hurricane in one form or as a mass of hundreds of writhing tendrils in another. While most Garou in Flux tend to avoid its notice, others have caught its attention and become victims of its desire “to help.” Some believe the Coil, embodying the mercurial nature of the realm over which it lords, has evolved into an avatar of the Wyld, making it an even stronger presence to avoid or seek out. 

The Flux is useful despite its dangers. As a realm of the Wyld, subject to no internal laws of matter, energy, or chemistry, it can provide many otherwise unattainable materials or a location for one’s most impossible feats. Garou come here to mend wounds that no other method can heal, or to use the contractions (or, to their dismay, dilations) of time to get to other locations quickly. Some of the most powerful weapons against the Weaver—items able to sever its webs and introduce Wyld energy—were forged in the Flux from materials found within its confines. 

Garou and other shapeshifters are able to enter the Flux as long as they have Wyld Affinity. The Weaver’s strength and dominance has increased, however. If a Garou does not have an affinity for the Wyld, she must make a simple test using her Mental attribute + Survival skill versus a difficulty rating of 15 to locate this realm. If she fails, she is not able to cut through the Weaver’s influence, and ends her travel in a different realm. Exiting Flux depends on chance and luck, as exits appear at random. Usually, a Garou can locate these egresses by locating areas where the weather and geographical disturbances are the most turbulent. If they avoid being struck by any of the Wyld energy in the area, they may even come out mostly unchanged on the other side. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** The Flux is a realm where the impossible becomes real even to those supernatural creatures used to bending the natural laws as they wish. Here, Garou can use the flexible nature of time itself to travel incredible 

distances in a blink, arriving in time to save a caern under siege or seek out rare materials to forge a weapon to combat an otherwise-invulnerable threat. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- To enter the Flux realm, a character must have either Wyld Affinity or must succeed in a simple test using her Mental attribute + Survival skill against a difficulty rating of 15 to locate the realm. 

- A character can forcefully attempt to change the environment around her by making a simple test using her Social attribute + Empathy skill against a difficulty rating of 15. This may help them navigate the landscape more easily. 

- Characters are able to change forms in this realm using a simple action, and they do not have to spend Gnosis to do so. 

- All characters who enter this realm are freed from any mind-controlling effects, including blood bonds and other supernatural bonds. Gifts or other powers that induce these effects do not work in Flux. 

- Spirits bound into fetishes escape in Flux, though they may willingly decide to stay within their fetish. These freed spirits return to the fetish when it leaves Flux. 

## Legendary 

In the _Legendary_ realm, terrible monsters still roam the earth and great deeds earn kingdoms. In a culture where myth, heroism, and storied feats are factual history, this land is a living testament to great power and noble rulers. It is the place where legendary ancestors dwell, where shapeshifters can learn at the feet of those glorified Garou, and where werewolves go on grand adventures in a land born from their ideals and dreams made manifest. 

The Legendary realm has many kingdoms scattered throughout it; all of them belong to Garou heroes of myth and legend. These kingdoms reward werewolves for incredible feats of strength and succeeding at seemingly impossible quests. Each stone and every flower springs forth from the attributes of these heroes, from the bitterly cold and brutal kingdoms of a once-mighty Get of Fenris to the fairytale-like kingdom of a Fianna who had intimate knowledge of the fae. Every shapeshifter whose glory was proclaimed to others, spread by whispered word and boisterous song for the most spectacular of deeds, finds her home here. In between each of these kingdoms is a vast, wild place known as the Wylderness, where mythical beasts wait, guarding unfathomable treasures and magical groves. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

No legend is made without darkness, however. The Legendary realm faces threats from the Midnight Land, the Fimbulwinter, and the Great Dust, each of which represents enemies and ideals capable of destroying the Garou Nation. The Midnight Land is an extension of Malfeas that withered and destroyed a once-lush kingdom until it was nothing more than plague and ash; its inhabitants became undead and banes. The Fimbulwinter assaults the legendary realm from the north, freezing both kingdoms and souls, while it destroys memories and legends. Where the Fimbulwinter does not reach in the south, the Great Dust instead takes hold. It is famine and desolation, starving kingdoms to death with its blight. 

Garou come to the Legendary realm to seek adventure, to learn from their ancestors, and to prove themselves to their packs, septs, tribes, and the nation. In this realm, they can seek treasure and glory, and participate in feats of wit and strength. Some shapeshifters find their way here to start handling the threats of the Midnight Land, Fimbulwinter, and the Great Dust, before they take hold in other realms or the Material Realm. 

To enter the Legendary realm, a character must have a goal to achieve or a challenge they must best; everything in this realm is a story, and any character who comes here must have one they wish to tell in mind. Even after meeting this qualification, they must still avoid banes and other dangers while traveling to this realm. To leave the Legendary realm, a character must complete her hero’s journey, having been transformed mentally, emotionally, or physically by her experience. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Garou seek the Legendary realm for the Renown that comes with success in proving their worth to any who doubt them. All stories that take place in the Legendary realm start with their own story, their own goal to accomplish, or their own challenge to overcome. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Time passes more quickly in the Legendary realm. While years or decades may seem to pass in this realm, turning a young Garou into a grizzled elder, only days or weeks pass in the Material Realm. For each day a Garou spends in the Legendary realm, only an hour passes in the Material Realm. 

- No spirit in this realm can be destroyed, as they each have an eternal role to play within their kingdoms. If they are killed, they later reform and take their places once more. 

- Only spirits bound to fetishes or to a shapeshifter can enter this realm. 

- While in this realm, all characters temporarily possess the Ancestors background at 5 dots, whether they possess this background on their sheet or not. Silent Striders do not gain this bonus. 

- Unlike in some other realms, where characters can use death to open exits or experience the realm more fully, if a character dies in the Legendary realm, her death is permanent. The character is not sent to another realm, the Material Realm, or the Penumbra, but is both physically and spiritually dead. 

## Scar 

This realm embodies the fear that, ultimately, the Garou fight a futile battle against an overwhelming foe with infinite resources. Some werewolves claim that the existence of _Scar_ proves this theory. It was once razed to its foundations, but the realm rebuilt itself on the ashes, smog, and the devastation of oil spills and polluted wastewater from fracking, proving true the darkest thoughts of some shapeshifters: the Weaver and the Wyrm will win. 

Scar is a massive blight realm dedicated to stripping spirits and other hapless trespassers of their Gnosis: their spiritual essence. Natural resources are ravaged in whole, strengthening both the Weaver and the Wyrm. An eternal smog lingers over the factories, mines, and mills in the Scar, coating them with thick, black soot. The most devastating effects of the industrial revolution live on forever here, everything oozing misery; the ground eternally leaks poisoned water, the sky is grey from smoke and pollution, and spirits are melted down until they become mindless emanations forced into horrifying working conditions. Any interloper captured is pressed into service as another worker. Banes pretend to be CEOs, the Barons of Industry, competing with each other for personal wealth and prestige, regardless of the catastrophic consequences. They set their guardsmen loose to abuse anyone who speaks out, who doesn’t put her head down, or who doesn’t put in their 100 hours of work a week. 

Most Garou avoid Scar. The chance of ending up on the assembly line or at the other end of the guardsmen’s fists is all too likely. However, Garou often accidentally find themselves in Scar without an easy way out, or they may be brought into the realm against their will. Other Garou come here to rescue packmates or captured spirits, trying to find them before they are turned into gaudy, cut-rate fetishes or soulless automatons. 

Getting into Scar isn’t challenging. While other realms have tricky pathways to navigate, spirits to avoid, or obstacles to overcome, it’s easy to stumble from other realms into the Scar, becoming caught in traps laid by pattern spiders that infest 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

the realm. Once in the realm, a shapeshifter may be captured by one of the many patrols in the area or turned in by the sickly emanations that crowd its filthy streets. But even once detected or captured, a Garou can attempt to escape by using the rickety machinery, cut-rate buildings, and disgruntled workers as distractions, or by sabotaging equipment, starting fires, or encouraging dissent and rioting among the employees. They must still be able to travel to another realm of the Umbra to truly escape, praying it’s better than Scar. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** All too often, the Garou fall into the Scar accidentally. Those who seek it out usually do so to rescue a captured pack or septmate, to save a spirit bound to the assembly line, or to seek Renown by sabotaging such a soulless realm. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- The air in Scar is massively polluted and the buildings are dim. Characters cannot attempt challenges using the Awareness skill. Characters with the Perception attribute focus lose access to any benefits of this focus, including those related to exceptional successes. 

- It always takes a character five turns to try to cross the Gauntlet out of Scar. Pattern spiders have heavily reinforced the Gauntlet with webbing to prevent escape. 

- Spirits in this realm have no Gnosis to spare, and often attempt to scavenge it from others, even forcefully. 

- Characters increase a stage of Harano for every 24 hours they are in this realm. 

## Summer Country 

An Eden conceived in drunken stories or vivid hallucinations, _Summer Country_ is a nothing more than a fairy tale to most Garou. A place where the lamb lays with the lion in eternal summer, peaceful and whole, seems too much like someone’s desperate hope for a fate other than the constant struggle of the Garou Nation. Summer Country, however, isn’t an imaginary paradise dreamed into being by idealists and hopeful Garou, but is a very real utopia created for the pure of heart and mind. 

Summer Country is a realm of harmony with Gaia and a reflection of the good that still exists in the Age of Apocalypse. Animals and plants long-extinct from the Material Realm still wander the earth here, mingling with and unafraid of the enlightened human emanations, shapeshifters, and spirits. Mountains and cloud aviaries exist in untouched beauty. The Isle of Self remains here as the foremost shrine to Gaia and the location of the Well of Life, which can cure any illness. Despite the encroaching 

influences of the Wyrm and Weaver fracturing and tainting the essence of other realms, Summer Country remains unaffected, but every one of its inhabitants wonders how long this will remain true. 

The Summer Country is a purifying realm, and many Garou capable of finding it come here to cleanse themselves of dire disease and sickness. Those who realize the realm is more than an empty promise also come to protect it and keep it from falling under outside influences. The Summer Country is place of epiphany and peace: a cool stream for the thirstiest Garou and a balm for their souls. 

Always difficult to reach, Summer Country became even more elusive as umbral storms wrecked Moon Bridges and anchorheads. A Garou can sometimes wander into the realm if she is at peace with her heart and soul, having reached equilibrium with herself, the world around her, and Gaia. Less-enlightened individuals sometimes find the entrance to Summer Country through the Forest of Longing, where they might transmute their bitterness, desires, and anxieties into the tranquility of self the realm requires before granting entry. Exit is immediate if the balance of soul ever shifts in one direction or another, or if a Garou is disrespectful to the realm or any of its inhabitants. If a shapeshifter ever desires to leave, an exit portal appears before her. Summer Country holds no one who does not wish to be there. Once she leaves, the bright memories of paradise eventually fade, and any of her recollections sound like the vague ramblings of the mad. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Summer Country offers a setting of tranquility and reflection. It is a place of healing for those poisoned by the bitterness of Harano or Wyrm Taint. It is a paradise that offers a few moments respite for the weariest of souls. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Those who exit Summer Country into other realms or the Material Realm may choose to return to any point in time in those realms, as long as they return to a time after they left. They can also return to any location that is not claimed by the Wyrm or the Weaver. 

- If a Garou resorts to violence in Summer Country, she is immediately ejected from the realm. Any creature or spirit native to Summer Country that is killed is resurrected at dawn. 

- Characters do not suffer from wound penalties in this realm. 

- Characters who enter this realm are immediately restored to full health, Gnosis, and Willpower. In addition, any detrimental effects from gifts are removed. 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- Those who are in any stage of Wyrm Taint cannot enter this realm. 

- Anyone who drinks from the Well of Life is cleansed of any disease, toxin effect, Harano, or other detrimental effect at the discretion of the Storyteller. 

## Tribal Homelands 

After the initial shock of the return of umbral storms, many Garou were surprised to find their _Tribal Homelands_ were pushed from the Penumbra into the Near Umbra. While some werewolves blame the chaotic nature of the storms, others believe this shift was a result of their own hubris and self-interest in the Age of Apocalypse. Those Garou whisper quietly about their losses, including their ties to their totems and tribal ancestry, lest they be heard and condemned for their pessimism. 

Despite their relocation, the Tribal Homelands still exist as the spiritual home of each tribe, emanations made all the stronger by the tribal totem spirits that rule them. Each of the Homelands speaks to the nature of the totem that oversees it, continuing on as the ideal home for each tribe. For example, Bone Gnawers have vast cities they can explore, Shadow Lords look down from lofty mountain peaks as they jockey to be first in all things, and Red Talons roam back-country forests untainted by human hands. Only Silent Striders have no home, alone in their inability to place roots. 

The Tribal Homelands are places the Garou come to be at one with the tenets of their tribes. Some are sent to these realms when they are found lacking, expected to learn harsh lessons from the most notable of their kind. Others seek them out as points of reconnection with their heritage and spiritual ancestry. 

As the Homelands were pushed further into the Umbra, they have become difficult to enter. Only those characters with 4 or more dots of the Rank background can enter them without an escort, and those Garou must avoid the banes that patrol the paths between other realms and the Tribal Homelands. Exiting the Homelands is hindered even more greatly, as though banes can smell the presence of the Homelands, gathering at their egress gates and waiting for an unlucky Garou to emerge. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** Connecting with ancestor spirits to gain knowledge, to learn about tribal secrets, or to seek answers about lost treasures of the tribe are all reasons to travel to the Homelands. It can also help Garou who have lost their tribal identity to relearn or reconnect with the tenets that make them who they are. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Only those characters with 4 or more dots of the Rank background can enter the Homelands without being supervised by another character of Athro rank or better. 

- Tribal gifts and merits with limitations on their use can be used freely in the Tribal Homelands. 

- Characters with the Totem or Ancestors backgrounds refresh any of those backgrounds’ abilities, and regain their Willpower and Gnosis upon entering the Tribal Homelands. 

## How to Build a Custom Realm 

Realms in the Near Umbra are comprehensive locations dedicated to ideals or other deep-seated energies. The Scar was born from the strong emotions and effects of the Industrial Revolution, while the Flux was created from chaotic Wyld energy. Realms are usually tied to a member or multiple members of the Triat and reflect their influence in some way. When building a custom realm in the Near Umbra, keep the following tips: 

- Determine which member of the Triat is tied to the realm. This choice can include any combination of the Weaver, the Wyld, or the Wyrm, or may be none of them, existing entirely outside of their influence. However, those realms that exist outside of the influence of the Triat are prime territory for war to determine which member will ultimately influence the realm. 

- Determine which strong emotion or archetype is reflected in the realm. For example, is the realm about an idealized notion of chivalry, or the destructive nature of greed? Unlike domains, this archetype should reflect a primal understanding of commonly shared fears or desires, and how those concepts might manifest through NPCs, locations, and other thematic elements. 

- Describe the Realm. What does it look like? How do the scenery, mood, and NPCs in the realm support its theme? What spiritually enlightened people may appear here? 

- Determine how a character enters and exits the realm. 

- Create some specific ways in which the realm might limit or amplify the various aspects of characters within it. 

**Example:** _Lucinda wants to create a custom realm involving what has become a very common theme within her game— the noble sacrifice. She believes there is a greater link to enlightened self-interest and martyrdom, and she is interested in allowing her players to explore their choices through a quest. Lucinda names her realm “Witness,” tying it to the original_ 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

_intent of martyrs, and basing it on the nuances and impact of self-sacrifice. Lucinda chooses not to align the realm to a member of the Triat, but tells her players they can constantly hear the sounds of battle in the distance, even if they cannot locate its source. She determines that characters can enter this realm accidentally, through traveling through the Umbra while musing about the cost of such sacrifices. She also determines they can enter it intentionally through the Dream Zone, as they explore facets of blood, death, and pain._ 

_Lucinda describes the realm for her players, explaining that it appears as a series of beautiful and intricately carved monuments, each of them detailing the deeds of someone who sacrificed their life for another. She tells them that even the trees are hung with ornaments describing these acts, but that the realm is otherwise eerily empty, aside from a great stone castle that shimmers in the distance. In those times when the distant sound of battle subsides, the characters can hear weeping, always from a far away, unknown source. Lucinda decides the realm is watched over by an Incarna known as The Red Lady, a spirit of loneliness and loss surrounded by her field of bones. Lucinda wants this realm to be a reflection of cost, and so her players pass by some monuments that are incredibly personal to their characters, reflecting those who have passed on in the protection or service of others or parts of the characters’ lives that they have sacrificed._ 

_Lucinda allows her players to leave the realm only when they understand the cost of their lives, and know who has died for them. While in the realm, she determines that players can use gifts that benefit others without spending Gnosis. In addition, as a realm where pain is intrinsic to understanding, gifts that inflict damage have their damage output doubled._ 

## The Deep Umbra 

The Deep Umbra is a vast spiritual realm outside of the Penumbra: an immense void containing thousands of hidden umbral realms, where alien creatures of incomprehensible intellect and unimaginable power walk in the empty space between the realms. Merely traveling to this dangerous aspect of the Umbra requires a Heroic Quest. 

Some of the aspects of the Deep Umbra include: 

- Home of the Aetherial Realms (the dwellings of the Celestines) 

- Sept of the Stars (the umbral Stargazer sept) 

## Mechanics of the Deep Umbra 

This section contains special mechanics for each of the Deep Umbra’s realms, as well as guidelines for creating custom realms. 

Garou in the Deep Umbra can: 

- Travel to its various realms, domains, and sub-realms. 

- Speak and interact with powerful Incarna and Celestines. 

- Undergo quests to achieve personal goals, such as knowledge or cleansing. 

- In the Deep Umbra, outside of the realms themselves, the intrinsic nature of the different tribes shines through without doubt. For example, Silver Fang coats shine like molten silver, and Shadow Lords’ fur absorbs light like a moon-dark sky. 

The Deep Umbra is full of powerful Celestines and Incarna that claim vast territories within the realms and their surroundings. Spiritual minions can be found going about the work of their masters, and rarely, a terrifying creature from the depths beyond can be found making attempts to establish a foothold in true reality. 

## Geography of the Deep Umbra 

Garou reach the Deep Umbra by flying to the highest part of the Earth’s atmosphere, buoyed upward by gifts or the blessing of spirits until they can no longer feel the influence of Gaia. Once there, they must cross over the _Membrane_ , a Gauntlet-like skin that separates the Penumbra from the dark things that linger in the roiling depths of the Deep Umbra. 

Although the Deep Umbra was once a vast nothingness, umbral storms shifted some realms into its depths, making them more difficult to reach. These shifts have empowered those realms, their inhabitants growing more dangerous and their landscapes wildly unpredictable. Even shapeshifters who knew the realms well when they were located in the Near Umbra think twice about entering them now in their new locations in the Deep Umbra, knowing they are one easy misstep from disappearing into the belly of the unknown. 

## Abyss 

The _Abyss_ is a deep rift obscured by thick mists where every footstep is treacherous and every handhold a deceit. It is one of the most feared realms in the Umbra, where all that is lost—from Garou themselves to items both mundane and magical—can be found. Even the most powerful Garou fear to step into the Abyss, as they know it is all too easy to be lost to its pull. However, those who do not immediately retreat from the unknowable may find the deaths they fear or the glory they desire. 

Three paths, their walls wet and jagged, spiral into the darkness that is the heart of the Abyss: The Golden, the 

Chapter thirteen: the Umbra 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

Iron, and the Silver Paths. While it is possible to climb down the walls of the Abyss, the danger of falling into the center of the realm and never being heard from again is great. Risk is not completely mitigated by walking the Paths of the Abyss, but most Garou find them to be marginally safer. 

The Golden Path is lined with veins of gold and precious gems. It is the most physically difficult path to traverse, with large gaps that must be leapt over and slick rocks to land on. Wealth, power, and forgotten secrets reward those who reach the end of the Golden Path. 

The Iron Path is filled with caverns and alcoves holding the bones of lost children, Garou, and other shapeshifters who were paralyzed and then starved by the fear and madness the Abyss emanates. Rarely, a Garou driven mad is found still alive and rescued by others who walked the same path. Such an act is heroic and a source of great Renown for any who can accomplish it. The Iron Path rewards those who navigate it with lore about themselves and others. 

The most mysterious and deadly path is the Silver Path. Some claim the Eater of Souls lies bound at the end of the Silver Path, while others believe that the Silver Crown lies at its end, waiting to be worn again by the next King or Queen of the Garou Nation. The Silver Path has no reward at its end of which the Garou are aware, but nevertheless, some follow it, hoping to find lost fetishes and items of power that they sometimes stumble upon as they delve deeper down the Silver Path. 

The Abyss is an easy realm to discover by accident. It calls to those who are lost, and many find themselves navigating its precipice as a result of losing their way. Escaping is not as easy. The Abyss steals the will to survive from those who linger too long within it. Those who can muster the strength to escape its paths and return to the mists have the chance to leave the Abyss with whatever will they have managed to retain. 

**Storytelling Hooks:** The Abyss is a realm full of treasures and secrets for those brave enough to wrest them from the grasp of oblivion. Lost fetishes can be found or secrets unknown to even the Celestines revealed. For those driven by material gain, they can retrieve countless gems and other wealth. Those seeking adventure can also find it, as they search for ways to navigate the onslaughts of umbral storms and to escape the hungry clutches of the Abyss. 

## **Special Mechanics:** 

- Time within the Abyss wears at the will of any who enter. Every hour within the Abyss causes the loss of 1 point of Willpower that cannot be regained until the character leaves the confines of the Abyss. 

- Willpower can only be regained through supernatural means within the Abyss. Any character with no remaining points of Willpower finds herself instantly removed to a cave on the Iron Path, bound in madness until death or rescue claim her. 

- To begin to travel on the paths of the Abyss or to scale its walls, characters must succeed in a test using their Physical attribute + Athletics skill against a varying difficulty rating, depending on the route they choose to take. To follow the Golden Path, characters must succeed in a challenge against a difficulty rating of 6. To follow the Iron Path or to scale the walls, they must succeed in a challenge against a difficulty rating of 8, and to follow the Silver Path, they must succeed in a challenge against a difficulty rating of 10. As characters travel deeper into the Abyss, they must make additional tests as the way becomes more and more impassable. The difficulty rating of each successive test increases by 2. If a character fails a test, she falls from the path into the center of the Abyss, lost forever. 

When the difficulty rating reaches 20 or higher, the character must also test to avoid entering fox frenzy to master her fear and remain on the path or to flee as fast as she can to exit the realm. If she has 3 points or fewer of Willpower when she reaches this point, she must instead make a static Mental attribute + Willpower challenge against a difficulty rating of 12. Failing this test causes the character to throw herself into the center of the Abyss. She can spend a point of Willpower to avoid making this test, but once spent, it cannot be recovered until she exits the realm. 

## Aetherial 

High within the sky in the Umbra, above the clouds, lies the _Aetherial_ realm—the home of the Celestines and their servants. Spiritual reflections of each of the planets exist within this realm, containing sights both wondrous and terrifying. Along with the different planetary Celestines, the Aetherial realm is also home to the only known caern within the Umbra itself: the Sept of the Stars. 

The Sept of the Stars is a Stargazer sept, welcoming and advising those who visit regarding their explorations within the Aetherial realm. Travelers can seek out advice about the differing laws that affect each of the different planetary realms or seek suggestions about how to avoid the dangers that exist along the paths between planets. Members of the sept, seeking to better understand the powerful spirits of the Aetherial realm, consider it their responsibility to guide and advise those who come seeking the Celestines and their realms. Members of this sept also observe the three Reaches of creation: the Wyld, Weaver, and Wyrm. Some believe 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

the study of these areas from the heights afforded by the Aetherial realm grants some forewarning of the works of each member of the Triat. 

The rest of the Aetherial realm is made up of different planets that the planetary Celestines call home. These planets also include the asteroid belt known as the domain of Rorg, as well as the Sun and Moon of Helios and Luna. These planets each have their own laws and dangers, and some of the Celestines have an auspice affinity or an affinity for one of the different tribes of Garou. Reaching these destinations offers the unique opportunity to gain forgotten lore, create powerful fetishes, and gain alliances with spirits many have only heard of in the tales of Galliards. The challenges found in each of these areas, as well as the paths to reach them, are trials that forge those who walk them into suitable vessels for the gifts they may receive if they are tested and found true. 

Entrance to the Aetherial realm comes easily to those who can fly, soaring above the clouds to stand on the Moon Bridge that leads to the Sept of the Stars. The only other way to reach the lofty heights is to scale the mountains in the Umbra that lead to the Sept of the Stars. Celestines, as a whole, are far too powerful in most cases to manifest directly before Garou. Each planetary realm is home to the avatar Incarna of each of these colossal powers.

||Luna – Moon|Sohkta/Phoebe|||
||Helios – Sun|Hyperion/<br>Katanka-<br>Sonnak|Wendigo (who<br>refer to Helios<br>as Katanka-<br>sonnak)||
||Mercury|Mitanu|Bone Gnawer/<br>Ragabash||
||Vulcan (Dark<br>Twin of<br>Mercury)|Hakahe|Uktena/<br>Theurge||
