## **Gaffling NPC** 

Animal Spirits 

Gaffling – Nature Spirits 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 3 

**Specializations:** Animal Ken, Brawl, Gifts: Theurge 

**Willpower:** OOO 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO O **Health Levels:** OOO 

**Special:** Animal Spirits have a test pool of 6 in most challenges. In challenges involving Animal Ken and Brawl, their test pool is 11. Animal Spirits have access to the first three levels of Theurge-affinity gifts. 

These spirits are the reflection of the souls of the animals they represent. Foxes are cunning and stealthy, while turtle spirits are slow but armored in defense. These spirits are focused on their natures, with the desire to hunt, mate, and defend their territory high on their lists of personal goals. 

## **Gaffling NPC** 

Elementals 

Gaffling – Element Spirits 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 3 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Science, Gifts: Homid 

**Willpower:** OOO **Gnosis:** OOOOO O **Health Levels:** OOO 

**Special:** Elementals have a test pool of 6 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness and Science, their test pool is 11. Elementals have access to the first three levels of Homid-affinity gifts. 

These spirits can manifest as any commonly found element, such as Air, Earth, Fire, Water, or rarer elemental representations such as Electricity, Glass, or Atomic. While these spirits are often minor Gafflings, they can also be found as Jagglings. 

Spirits will sometimes require a _ban_ as part of an exchange with a werewolf. For example, a Garou may have to agree to accept a ban against killing small rodents in order to gain assistance from one of Rat’s brood. Garou may also voluntarily accept a spirit’s ban as a show of good faith and solidarity with a spirit she is petitioning for assistance. 

If a Storyteller introduces a spirit into her game, she should know what its ban is, even if she believes it will never come into play. 

Garou who break a spirit’s ban after they have agreed to uphold it run the risk of finding themselves on the bad side of that spirit. Characters who do this may temporarily gain the flaw Spirit Notoriety (see **Chapter Six: Merits and Flaws: Flaws, Spirit Notoriety** , page 327).

Net Spider

- A Jaggling can spend 1 point of Gnosis to manifest in the physical world for one hour. While manifested, the spirit can attack, be attacked, and use powers on individuals in the physical world as if the spirit were a normal creature. If a spirit dies while manifesting in the physical world, it is sent into slumber, immediately returning to its home domain bound in sleep until it regains enough Gnosis to manifest once more. The higher the the spirit’s rating, the faster this process occurs, assuming it has access to Gnosis. 

- A Jaggling has a number of health levels equal to twice its Stock NPC rating. 

**Health Levels:** OOO 

**Special:** Net Spiders have a test pool of 6 in most challenges. In challenges involving Computer, Investigation, or Stealth, their test pool is 11. Net Spiders have access to the first three levels of Homid-affinity gifts. 

Net Spiders are popular spirits with Glass Walkers, and are often used to gather information and data from computer networks. They are particularly useful at pirating data. 

## Jagglings 

_Jagglings_ are moderately powered spirits that possess intelligence and self-motivation. They serve the Incarna and Celestines, with loyalty to their masters built into their nature. They often do the bidding of their spirit masters, although they do have a measure of free will in order to complete their duties. Powerful Jagglings have the ability to create Gafflings and bind them as their own minions. Many of these spirits are called upon to teach gifts to werewolves and assist them in their never-ending war. Particularly powerful Jagglings are able to summon Gafflings, small gnostic spirits who exist to provide Gnosis to other spirits or to provide Garou, and sometimes entire septs, with gifts of Gnosis. 

To create a Jaggling, use the same rules for creating standard supernatural Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- Jagglings’ Stock NPC rating is limited to 3 through 5, and they cannot take the Elder specialization. 

- Jagglings can specialize in Gnosis, which allows them to have a Gnosis pool equal to four times their Stock NPC rating.

Lunes are servants of Luna that appear as shimmering gold and blue lights. They communicate empathically with other spirits, and are quite fond of the Garou. They are intelligent and wise, though as mercurial as their mistress.

These spirits of War and Revenge are said to aid Fenrir’s lost children. They appear as large white wolves with blue eyes, and often travel in packs. 

## Incarna 

Spirits that have gathered enough strength and influence to attain the level of _Incarna_ are powerful advisors, soldiers, and consorts for the godlike Celestines. Although they lack some of the sheer strength of their spirit lords, these are the most powerful creatures that most Garou will ever encounter while in the Umbra. Intelligent and willful, Incarna often act of their own volition, working with werewolves or against them as their whims or nature dictate. Some of the most powerful Incarna possess their own domains in the Umbra, while others merely inhabit the homes of their masters. Many Incarna foster their power in hopes of eventually ascending to Celestine status. 

The Incarna spirits that most often appear to Garou are the tribal totem spirits. Often, the creature that interacts with a werewolf is merely an avatar of the totem spirit itself. 

To create an Incarna, use the same rules for creating standard supernatural Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- Unlike normal Stock NPCs, which have NPC ratings between 1 and 5, Incarna have ratings between 6 and 15. 

- Incarna can spend 1 point of Gnosis to manifest in the physical world for one hour. While manifested, the spirit can attack, be attacked, and use powers on individuals in the physical world as if the spirit were 

**
## **Storyteller Warning: Incarna** 

Although Incarna can be created using the Stock NPC rules, a fair amount of planning and thought should be put into the creation of one of these spirits. They should not be made frivolously or on-the-spot. Remember, each Incarna is a singular one of its kind. It is the defining spirit for its brood. Serious consequences should befall anyone who trifles with these powerful spirits. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

a normal creature. If a spirit dies while manifesting in the physical world, it is sent into slumber, immediately returning to its home domain bound in sleep until it regains enough Gnosis to manifest once more. The higher the the spirit’s rating, the faster this process occurs, assuming it has access to Gnosis. 

- Incarna have a Willpower pool equal to twice their Stock NPC rating. 

- Any time a character that is not an Incarna, Celestine, or member of the Triat is involved in a challenge with this spirit, the Incarna gets a free retest. This retest is in addition to any retest gained through the use of Willpower or overbidding. 

Incarna Bestiary 

## **Incarna NPC** 

The Harvester 

Incarna – Luna’s Brood 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 8 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Empathy, Gifts: Theurge, Intimidation, Leadership, Lore, Occult, Survival 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O **Health Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO 

## OOOOO O 

**Special:** The Harvester has a test pool of 16 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Empathy, Intimidation, Leadership, Lore, Occult, or Survival, its test pool is 21. The Harvester has access to all Theurge-affinity gifts. 

The Harvester appears during the harvest season to encourage farmers as they clear their fields. Legends tell of the Harvester appearing to defend farmers in their fields while they are under attack. This spirit often only appears during a Harvest Moon, but it will sometimes be sent by Luna for other occasions. 

- The Storyteller has the option to make any gift possessed by an Incarna into a custom power. Storytellers are encouraged to create powers that match the theme of the NPC. To create a custom power, take a standard gift and make one of the following adjustments: 

   - › Remove one of the power’s limitations. For example, the spirit could use the level 2 gift Burden of Doubt multiple times for a cumulative reduction to the target’s Physical attack test pool. 

   - › Double the numbers of one of the power’s effects. For example, the level 5 gift Conduit of Pain would inflict 6 points of aggravated damage on the target when used as a custom power. 

## **Incarna NPC** 

The Great Trash Heap Incarna of Lost Things 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 8 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Empathy, Gifts: Ragabash, Gifts: Bone Gnawer, Investigation, Lore, Stealth, Survival 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O **Health Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O 

**Special:** The Great Trash Heap has a test pool of 16 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Empathy, Investigation, Lore, Stealth, or Survival, its test pool is 21. The Great Trash Heap has access to all Ragabash- and Bone Gnawer-affinity gifts. 

The Great Trash Heap is an Incarna spirit that is often called on by those who are searching for something that has been lost to them. This spirit rules over landfills, pawn shops, and gutters full of discarded items. It exists in a small, obscure realm, and some believe that this realm is a kind of psychic dumping ground, a place where Garou can go to search for any item that has been discarded.

The Tree of Life is known by many names throughout the world: the Cosmic Tree, the World Tree, and the Tree of Knowledge. Wise and steady, this Incarna rules over its brood of oak Jagglings and Gafflings, and it provides a place to seek wisdom and guidance for those who are willing to listen.

Charyss is the ruler of Erebus, the umbral realm where Garou go to atone for their transgressions. When a Garou enters Erebus, she encounters a series a tests. Should she pass them, she is brought before Charyss, who then judges her. If she is found to be worthy, Charyss will pass on the secrets of the realm and the secrets of atonement to the petitioner. 

## Celestine 

_Celestines_ are the most powerful spirits werewolves are actually able to encounter in the Umbra. They are so massive in strength that their realms can span entire planetary bodies. Luna and Helios are two examples of the most powerful Celestines, holding the Moon and Sun as their representative realms. These spirits are never seen within the Material Realm, as their presence alone would shatter whole cities into writhing masses of Delirium and 

## **Incarna NPC** 

## Coyote 

Incarna – Nuwisha totem spirit **Stock NPC:** Rating 14 

**Specializations:** Animal Ken, Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Empathy, Gifts: Lupus, Gifts: Nuwisha, Gifts: Ragabash, Intimidation, Leadership, Lore, Occult, Stealth, Survival 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOO 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOO 

## **Health** 

## **Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOO 

**Special:** Coyote has a test pool of 28 in most challenges. In challenges involving Animal Ken, Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Empathy, Intimidation, Leadership, Lore, Occult, Stealth, and Survival, his test pool is 33. Coyote has access to all Lupus-, Nuwisha-, and Ragabash-affinity gifts. 

Wild and unpredictable, Coyote is the totem spirit of the Nuwisha. He teaches his children to be independent and to push the others around them, seeking to teach with his tricks. It was one such trick played on Luna that led her to deny the Nuwisha any auspice aside from Ragabash. In an attempt to appease her, coyotes sing to her nightly, in hopes of soothing ancient wounds. 

disbelief. If need arises, they each have avatar aspects: Incarna servants they can and do send to do their bidding or enforce their will. 

Each of these spirits claims an umbral realm as its personal territory. These umbral realms reflect their temperment. (For more information, see **Chapter Thirteen: The Umbra, The Near Umbra** , page 590 and **The Deep Umbra** , page 597.) The only spirits greater in power than Celestines are the Triat themselves, and Gaia, who created all. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

To create a Celestine, use the same rules for creating standard supernatural Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- Unlike normal Stock NPCs, which have NPC ratings between 1 and 5, Celestines have ratings between 15 and 30. 

- Any time a character that is not a Celestine or member of the Triat is involved in a challenge with this spirit, the Celestine gets a free retest. This retest is in addition to any retest gained through the use of Willpower or overbidding. 

- Celestines have access to every gift, unless the Storyteller rules a particular gift is opposed to the spirit’s nature. 

- Celestine have specializations in all skills, unless the Storyteller rules a particular skill is opposed to the spirit’s nature. 

- Each time a Celestine uses a gift, it can apply one of the following adjustments. These adjustments are made when the gift is activated and may vary between each power’s use. 

   - › Remove one of the power’s limitations. For example, the level 5 gift Bridge Walker could allow you to bring a single passenger with you. 

   - › Double the numbers of one of the power’s effects. For example, the level 2 gift Jam Technology could be used to disable an item within 40 steps of you. 

## Celestine Bestiary 

## **Celestine NPC** 

Luna 

Celestine – Realm of the Moon 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 30 

**Specializations:** All 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO

**Special:** Luna has a test pool of 60 in most challenges. In challenges involving any appropriate skills for the spirit, her test pool is 65. Luna has access to all appropriate gifts. 

Luna is the Celestine that claims the Moon as her realm, a mercurial and temperamental creature whose whims change with the phases of her physical manifestation. She is often associated with insanity, and her personality changes drastically to reflect the auspices of Garou. She resides in the Aetherial realm of the Umbra, which some believe blends into the penumbral reflection of space. Luna is incredibly important to the Garou, and she blesses them with many gifts. This close tie to Luna is the reason werewolves are sensitive to silver. Luna is also especially fond of the Bastet, who know her in her aspect of Seline.

**Special:** Helios has a test pool of 60 in most challenges. In challenges involving any appropriate skills for the spirit, his test pool is 65. Helios has access to all appropriate gifts. 

Helios is the Celestine who claims the Sun as his realm, and he is Luna’s brother. He is also a patron of the Garou and, like his sister, he grants his patronage to a few select Fera. The Corax and the Mokolé in particular view Helios as their patron, and this relationship leads to those Fera’s sensitivity to gold. Helios makes his home in the Aetherial realm of the Umbra. 

Tambiyah

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO

Tambiyah is the Celestine ruler of the realm of Venus. This realm is a lush jungle paradise, a place like the world before humanity began to destroy it. She is a spirit that cares deeply for women and children. Her handmaindens are song spirits, whose crystalline tones warn her of any seeking entry to her realm. Black Furies hold a special place in this spirit’s heart, though they often refer to her as Aphrodite. 

**
## **Storyteller Warning: Celestine** 

Although Celestines are created using the Stock NPC rules, a fair amount of planning and thought should be put into the creation of these spirits. They should not be made frivolously or on-the-spot, and their interaction within a local chronicle should be limited. Celestines are the most powerful spirits beneath the Triat themselves. They do not leave their home realms, instead relying on Incarna avatars of themselves and powerful Jaggling servants in order to enforce their will or agenda on other umbral realms or the physical world. 

**

Nergul is a Celestine devoted to violence and bloodshed. He is fierce and dedicated, a spirit who stops at nothing to achieve his ultimate goal. He is a popular spirit among Get of Fenris and Ahroun. Nergul pushes his children to their limits, always encouraging violence as a means to an end. Those who follow Nergul are assertive and strong-willed.

## **Celestine NPC** 

Zarok 

Celestine – Realm of Jupiter **Stock NPC:** Rating 28 

**Specializations:** All 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O 

Once a pack or caern has formed, its members seek out a totem to represent them. This totem spirit provides the pack or caern with support in the form of gifts and spiritual guidance. In exchange, the Garou dedicated to the spirit are expected to adhere to the totem’s ban, in order to ensure its continued assistance and cooperation. While totem spirits often work alongside their pack or caern, they do not fight battles for them. Their purpose is to inspire and assist, not overcome challenges for the characters. 

The relationship between a pack and its totem is a sacred and sometimes complicated one. It is a reciprocal bond, with each party lending strength and guidance to the other. Some packs believe that a totem spirit is theirs to own and control, though they often find out the hard way that this is not the case. A pack that disrespects and mistreats its totem often finds itself left without any spiritual guidance at all, the pack weakened until it can find another spirit willing to bond with it and make it whole in body and spirit. 

**Health** 

**Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO O 

**Special:** Zarok has a test pool of 56 in most challenges. In challenges involving any appropriate skills for the spirit, his test pool is 61. Zarok has access to all appropriate gifts. 

Zarok is the Celestine ruler of the realm of Jupiter. He is a spirit of rulership and justice, with an affinity for the Silver Fang tribe and for Philodox. Zarok is a powerful and dominant spirit, which abhors lies and dishonesty. He is quick to punish any who dare commit such acts in his presence. 

## Totems 

Totems are powerful Incarna spirits that act as patrons to packs, septs, and even entire tribes as a tangible manifestation of the ancient pacts between Garou and spirits from the dawn of time. These spirits serve to help protect the Garou through their blessings, share their wisdom, and safeguard the sacred places of the Wyld. As with other Incarna, totems seldom materialize into the Material Plane, and they will not seek to directly fight the Garou’s battles for them. However, they may decide to send aid to the faithful in the form of powerful Jaggling spirits. 

## Creating Totems 

The following steps guide players and Storytellers through creating appropriate totem spirits. While a totem may be created utilizing the Stock NPC rules, care should be taken when creating the spirit’s personality. Although they are NPCs, these spirits are integral to a chronicle. 

A pack totem’s Stock NPC rating is equal to the bonuses available to the totem, based on the pack diversity. For more information, see **Chapter Four: Character Creation, Step Eight: Assign Additional Backgrounds, Background Descriptions, Totems** , page 201. 

## Determine Totem Type 

When creating any totem spirit, the first step is to determine what type of totem you would like to represent your pack or caern. Totem spirits generally fall into the category of Cunning, Respect, War, and Wisdom. The last three are loosely associated with the types of Renown. While there are any number of totem spirits available to players, they are often, but not always, associated with these categories: 

- **Totems of Cunning:** Totems of Cunning have become more popular in the Age of Apocalypse as Garou have been forced to change their tactics in the war. These totems focus on trickery and stealth, though there are Totems of Cunning who specialize in waging war in the modern age. While some werewolves feel these totems are untrustworthy, they have found a great deal of purpose in the Garou Nation today. Coyote, Fox, Cuckoo, and Raccoon are all well-known totems of Cunning. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- **Totems of Respect:** Totems of Respect embody virtue and honor, and are often called on by packs looking for assistance with leadership and diplomacy. Some of the greatest leaders within the Garou Nation have followed totems of Respect. Falcon, Bison, Grandfather Thunder, and Pegasus are well-known totems of Respect. 

- **Totems of War:** Totems of War are the spirits of battle, tactics, and rage. Ancestral warriors and predator spirits are often called to serve as totems of War. Although this category is popular for packs with dedicated warriors, War also requires scouts and healers, who may also find themselves drawn to these totems. Bear, Boar, Clashing Boom-Boom, and Weasel are popular totems of War. 

- **Totems of Wisdom:** Totems of Wisdom are spirits that are keepers of secrets. Werewolves often ally with these spirits in order to uncover hidden truths and information lost to the Garou Nation. Chimera, Owl, Raven, and Unicorn are popular totems of Wisdom. 

## Determine Totem Goals 

Each totem spirit has a goal or a set of objectives that it wishes to achieve, and these goals should align with the goals and objectives of the pack or caern it represents. These goals might be as broad as “Defeat the Wyrm” or as narrowly defined as “Kill the vampire who murdered our Sept Leader.” 

The goal of the pack or caern should align with the type of totem chosen. For example, a totem of Cunning would be poorly suited for a pack whose goal is “Lead the Sept of Broken Tears successfully in the Age of Apocalypse.” 

Some sample goals for each type of totem include: 

- **Totems of Cunning:** Free the Wyrm from the Weaver; infiltrate a local hive; bring the Garou Nation into the modern age 

- **Totems of Respect:** Protect sacred places; guide lost Garou; teach young werewolves; locate, plant, and protect a shard caern 

- **Totems of War:** Destroy agents of the Wyrm; retake a fallen caern; protect the weak 

- **Totems of Wisdom:** Explore the depths of the Umbra; uncover lost Garou lore; encourage werewolves to live in harmony; find lost Cubs 

## Determine Totem Ban 

When a totem spirit accepts a pack or caern, it always imposes a ban on those who follow it. Each spirit has a 

unique ban and expects its followers adhere to it. Example bans include “Never wear the color orange,” or “Always sleep outdoors.” Some bans may be significantly more complex and demanding, such as “Always take the opportunity to discover lost lore.” 

Sample bans for each type of totem spirit follows. Players and Storytellers should work together to find a suitable ban for their group. This ban should be something that characters will commonly encounter; it is intended to represent a sacrifice for the privilege of working with the chosen totem spirit. 

- **Totems of Cunning:** Do not participate in fox hunts; exploit fortune and be opportunistic; always find more septs and build more caerns 

- **Totems of Respect:** Do not tell the truth to anyone you do not respect; aid children of all species; always show respect for prey 

- **Totems of War:** Never hunt or eat boars; never pass up the opportunity for a worthy fight; never kill vermin 

- **Totems of Wisdom:** Always seek enlightenment; never kill cockroaches; carry no wealth; aid and protect the weak and exploited 

## Choose Totem Bonuses 

Each totem bestows certain gifts to its followers: blessings that are intended to aid in the fight against the Wyrm. For additional information on the bonuses provided by pack totems and how to create a pack totem, see **Chapter Four: Character Creation, Step Eight: Assign Additional Backgrounds, Background Descriptions, Totems** , page 201. 

Caern totems also provide benefits to their followers; however, these are more limited, and can only be accessed if the caern is in danger. In the Age of Apocalypse, these spirits are often much weaker and more vulnerable than even pack totems, and they must be protected. For additional information on the creation of caern totems for a specific caern, see **Chapter Tweleve: Caerns, Shard Caerns, Seeding a Shard Caern, Ritual Task 2: Summon Caern Totem** , page 575. 

## Finishing Touches 

Once your totem spirit has been built, it is time to breathe life into it. These creatures are integral to the existence of Garou, and each should have a unique personality and appearance. Although these NPCs may not come into play every game session, they should make regular appearances within any given chronicle. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115)

Neutrinos 

Incarna Totem of Wisdom 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 10

**Totem Ban:** Neutrinos’ children must always carry some amount of Weaver-tech on them at any given time. 

**Totem Goals:** Neutrinos wants to help usher the Garou Nation into the modern age by encouraging the reasonable use of Weaver technology to take the fight to the Wyrm. 

**Special:** Neutrinos has a test pool of 20 in most challenges. In challenges involving Academics, Brawl, Computer, Dodge, Firearms, Science, or Security, his test pool is 25. Nuetrinos has access to all Glass Walker-, Homid-, and Theurge-affinity gifts. 

Neutrinos is a relatively young totem spirit; he has only recently realized his potential. It is his hope to usher the Garou into the modern age and to harness the power of nuclear fission for good.

Narwhal 

Incarna of War 

## **Stock NPC:** Rating 9

**Totem Goals:** Narwhal wants to raise awareness about the oceans, fighting those who murder and destroy its creatures when necessary. 

**Special:** Narwhal has a test pool of 18 in most challenges. In challenges involving Animal Ken, Brawl, Dodge, Intimidation, Melee, or Survival, his test pool is 25. Narwhal access to all Lupus-, Ragabash-, and Wendigo-affinity gifts. 

Although Narwhal has the potential to be a relatively gentle and benevolent spirit, he is a totem of War, and he has come to understand that his goals must be met through blood and violence. Fierce and tireless, Narwhal drives his children relentlessly. Although he asks a heavy price from his children, he is a dedicated and fierce patron.

Clashing Boom-Boom is a single-minded spirit bent on the destruction of the Wyrm. Her limited focus can make her reckless, and her packs often follow in her footsteps. Despite this behavior, she tends to choose those who are dedicated and talented.

**Totem Ban:** Sphinx’s children must never refuse a riddle challenge, and they may gain their totem’s ire if they lose such a challenge. 

**Totem Goals:** Sphinx believes in using wits and cunning to fight the war against the Wyrm. 

**Special:** Sphinx has a test pool of 20 in most challenges. In challenges involving Academics, Brawl, Dodge, Empathy, Investigation, Lore, or Occult, her test pool is 25. Sphinx has access to all Galliard-, Philodox-, and Theurge-affinity gifts. 

Sphinx is strong and cunning, able to beat her foes in combat, either Physical or Mental. She chooses her children from those who excel at thinking on their feet.

## The Wyrm 

_The Wyrm_ is a spirit more powerful than any Celestine, whose pain and madness are the source of every drop of corruption and decay within the entirety of the Umbra and Material Realm. Trapped within the strands of the Weaver’s web, the Wyrm’s very movements bring it agony beyond mortal comprehension, driving it deeper into madness. As it writhes and thrashes, it leaks its twisted spiritual corruption into Malfeas and beyond, where it is spread abundantly by the many minions who serve it. Once the embodiment of entropy, clearing that which 

## **Sample Caern Totem** 

## Boar 

Incarna Totem of War 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 7 

**Specializations:** Athletics, Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Gifts: Ahroun, Gifts: Get of Fenris, Intimidation 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOO **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOO 

## **Health** 

**Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOO **Totem Ban:** Boar demands that his children never hunt or eat his earthly brethren. 

**Totem Goals:** Boar is dedicated to rooting out the Wyrm wherever it may dwell. 

**Special:** Boar has a test pool of 14 in most challenges. In challenges involving Athletics, Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, or Intimidation, his test pool is 19. Boar has access to all Ahroun- and Get of Fenris-affinity gifts. 

Savage and powerful, Boar is feared by those who would attempt to make him prey. He is a ferocious warrior, often fighting long after others would step away from the fray. Many young war packs choose Boar as their representative, following him headlong into battle. 

had no use and purifying it to be used in creation once more, the Wyrm now seeks only to twist and corrupt all that surround it, forcing the world itself to suffer with it in endless and soul-shattering pain. 

The Age of Apocalypse sees the Wyrm continuing to focus on the spread of destruction, chaos, and darkness. Everywhere, signs of the Wyrm exist: a pervasive virus that is slowly working to bring Gaia to her knees. Its touch is enough to send a person spiraling into madness or to turn the pristine waters of a mountain lake to poisonous depths that contain dark creatures only found in one’s imagination. The taint 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

of the Wyrm can be found in all corners of the earth, and it only continues to spread. For additional information on the function of Wyrm Taint, see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Wyrm Taint** , page 384. 

## Wyrm Affinity 

Creatures that have completely surrendered their will to the Wyrm are considered to possess Wyrm Affinity. Gifts such as Sense the Balance ( **Chapter Five: Gifts, General Gifts, Level 1 Gifts, Sense the Balance** , page 267) reveal that creatures with this quality are associated with the Wyrm. Some creatures (such as **Blood Vines** , page 667) can sense fellow devotees of the Wyrm. 

## Banes 

_Banes_ are the twisted spirit children of the Wyrm. They embody the most negative and corrupting aspects of the world itself. While Gaian spirits can become tainted reflections of their former selves, banes are born from the darkest aspects of human and supernatural natures. From the _Phantasmi_ , the bane-spirit remains of fallen fomori forced to serve the Wyrm even after their physical bodies have died, to sickly _Hoglings_ , elementals born of smog and pollution, banes are spirits that live to the spread the very type of corruption from which they were born. These creatures make their homes in the Umbra, just as any other spirit, and their power can range from being relatively weak elemental banes to daunting Nexus Crawlers. 

Bane Bestiary 

## **Stock Bane NPC** 

## Ooralath 

Jaggling Bane Spirit **Stock NPC:** Rating 5 

**Specializations:** Athletics, Brawl, Gifts: Black Spiral Dancer, Intimidation, Survival **Willpower:** OOOOO **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO **Health Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO 

**Special:** Ooralath have a test pool of 10 in most challenges. In challenges involving Athletics, Brawl, Intimidation, or Survival, their test pool is 15. Ooralath have access to all Black Spiral Dancer-affinity gifts. Ooralath are the Hounds of the Wyrm, creatures that appear to be a cross between hounds and dinosaurs. They hunt through the Umbra, looking for lone travelers and spirits that they can devour. These spirits are fierce and vicious, always looking for their next kill. 

Banes may be created at any spiritual power level, from Gaffling to Celestine, and they should be created in the same manner as any other spirit, with the following exceptions: 

- Any bane with a spirit-rank of Jaggling or higher has the potential to inflict Wyrm Taint on Garou who are within melee range of it. If a Garou is hit by or successfully hits a bane, she finds herself Wyrm-tainted. If this taint is not cleansed, it has the potential to corrupt a Garou (see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems: Wyrm Taint** , page 384). 

- Banes can be defeated and sent into slumber. However, permanently destroying one requires a Heroic Quest, in which Garou must assemble the necessary components for the task and defeat the bane in its home realm of Malfeas.

Bitter Rages exist to drive Garou insane with Rage. Their goals are to goad and push the most violent urges forward until a werewolf is lost within the grip of frenzy and then push to her to fall into the Thrall of the Wyrm.

In challenges involving Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Empathy, Intimidation, Lore, Occult, Stealth, Subterfuge, or Survival, they have a test pool of 33. Dream Makers have access to Black Spiral Dancer-, Galliard-, and Theurgeaffinity gifts. 

Dream Makers are a twist on the Djinn of old, creatures that aim to grant the wishes of their masters. These banes were once powerful servants of the Weaver and the Wyld, helping to create the world, but they fell to the Wyrm centuries ago. In modern days, Dream Makers target people who are morally and spiritually bankrupt, whose wishes would further the goals of the Wyrm. These creatures are malicious and ruthless, and while they strive to grant the wishes of their human or supernatural masters, these wishes always have an evil twist. Nothing comes for free. 

## Fomori 

_Fomori_ are humans who have become so corrupted that banes are able to possess and then fuse with their bodies, transforming them into direct servants of the Wyrm. The nature of fomori and their powers and complications ensure that, while the process of becoming a fomor may have been unwitting, any choice the human may have had in determining her own future no longer exists. Once banes have fused with humans to create fomori, those humans are irredeemable; their bodies and minds indelibly twist into something unrecognizable. Fomori often go to great lengths to hide the deformities and madness caused by their complications, until they reach a stage of taint so deep they no longer care if they are discovered. Any bane spirit can create a fomor; however, Jagglings are most commonly found occupying the bodies of humans. 

## Fomori Taint Powers and Complications 

## **Taint Powers** 

Fomori NPCs have access to _taint powers_ , a series of abilities that make them terrifying creatures to encounter. When building a fomor Stock NPC, the Storyteller may choose to assign it one taint power per Stock NPC Rating. For example, a Rating 4 fomor may choose four of the powers below. These powers should be chosen in such a way as to complement one another and create a creature that is terrifying for Garou to face. 

- **Brain-Eating:** Using this power, a fomor can steal the mental faculties of her victim. The brain must be from a sentient being and requires one full turn to devour. After consuming the target brain, a fomor with this power gains a +1 bonus to its Stock NPC rating, and she may choose one power or ability as a new specialization with full access to it for the duration of this power. A fomor can only target one victim in this manner per game session, and the bonus lasts for 24 hours. This power does not work on spirits or other creatures that do not have a physical brain. 

- **Darksight:** With this ability, a fomor is able to see in complete darkness. The fomor suffers no vision penalties in any amount of light. When in complete darkness, the fomor may use the Fighting Blind combat maneuver (see **Chapter Seven: Core Systems, Complex Scenarios: Combat, Combat Manuevers, Fighting Blind** , page 344) without expending Willpower to do so. 

- **Hide of the Wyrm:** With this ability, a fomor possesses supernatural armor granted to her by the Wyrm. This may manifest as a hard carapace over her body or 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

as iron-lined skin. This power grants the fomor the Ballistic and Hardened protective gear qualities, and she becomes immune to Armor Piercing weapon qualities (see **Chapter Fifteen: Influences and Equipment, Equipment, Protective Gear** , page 709). 

- **Mind Blast:** With this ability, a fomor is able to send a blast of energy into her target’s mind, immobilizing her target with pain. The fomor must spend her standard action focusing on her target. Then, if the fomor has a complete character sheet, she must succeed in a Mental attribute + Investigation skill challenge versus her target’s Mental attribute + Willpower. If the fomor is a Stock NPC, the Storyteller should use its standard test pool plus any bonus it may receive from the Investigation specialization, if it possesses it. Upon succeeding at the challenge, the fomor’s target takes 2 points of aggravated damage and loses her next simple action, as her mind is wracked with pain. 

- **Unnatural Weaponry:** Thanks to the bane inhabiting her body, the fomor has become as twisted and hideous on the outside as she is on the inside. She has developed natural weaponry on her body, such as razor-sharp bones protruding from her joints; curved horns jutting from her head; a vicious, barbed, elongated tongue; or even retractable claws. These natural weapons grant the fomor +1 damage on any successful melee attack when using these weapons. The damage from these attacks is always aggravated. 

- **Poison Tumors:** The fomor is covered in tumors or pustules. Although they do not harm her, they may injure anyone who comes into contact with the acidic pus inside of them. If the fomor is damaged by a melee attack, the attacker is splattered with an acidic fluid that deals 1 point of aggravated damage to the attacker. If this fluid is ingested, the target takes 3 points of aggravated damage. This damage cannot be reduced or negated. 

- **Roar of the Wyrm:** With this ability, a fomor releases a guttural scream that strikes terror into the hearts of those near her. The fomor must expend a standard action and spend 1 point of Gnosis to initiate the attack. If the fomor has a complete character sheet, she then must make a Social attribute + Intimidation skill challenge versus the target’s Social attribute + Willpower. If the fomor is a Stock NPC, it uses its standard test pool plus any bonus it may receive from the Intimidation specialization, if it possesses it. Everyone within hearing range of the fomor must make this challenge. Should the formor spend 1 point of Willpower to retest any of these opposed challenges, she also gains that retest 

for the remainder of the opposed challenges without spending further Willpower. Victims who fail the challenge immediately begin to flee from the source of the sound. Once the affected target has left earshot, this power’s effect ends. 

## **Taint Complications** 

For every taint power that a fomor possess, she also possesses one _taint complication_ . These are the embodiments of the Wyrm’s influence on a human body. 

- **Derangement:** The introduction of a bane into a human’s soul and psyche often twists the victim’s mind as well as her body. This taint functions identically to the Derangement flaw (see **Chapter Six: Merits and Flaws, Flaws, Derangements** , page 328). Where appropriate, a Storyteller may assign more than one Derangement to a fomor. 

- **Inner Volcano:** Sometimes, when a bane overtakes a human, the spirit is too powerful for its host and begins to burn the human from the inside, leaving the fomor with a constant high-grade fever. Whenever the fomor is under stress or exerts herself, her fever intensifies to such a degree that she begins to cook from the inside. For every minute of hard physical labor, such as combat or lifting, or extreme emotional or mental duress that the fomor undergoes, she takes 1 point of aggravated damage. Additionally, anyone within melee range of her during this time takes 1 point of aggravated damage from her radiant heat, unless they have shielded themselves appropriately. This complication allows the fomor to be identified, as she can be seen sweating or felt changing the ambient air temperature in the room. 

- **Ugly as Sin:** With this taint complication, a fomor is hideously disfigured in some manner, thanks to the extreme stress put on her body by the bane spirit that possesses it. She might be rotting away from the inside, destined to eventually succumb to the rapid deterioration. She may be so hideous that she is unable to go out in public due to her appearance. Or perhaps her body is infested with worms or other small insects, and parasites occasionally slip from her body. 

- **Social Pariah:** Pair this taint complication with one taint power. Whenever the formor uses that particular power, the fomor risks suffering a permanent loss in social acumen. After a scene in which she has used that power, she must make a Social attribute + Willpower challenge with a difficulty rating of 12. If she fails this test, her appearance takes on a monstrous mien, clearly marking her as a fomor. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- **Special Diet:** The biological changes wrought by the fomor’s bane have ensured the fomor cannot gain sustenance from normal food sources. No matter how much she tries to eat, she never feels satisfied. Instead, the fomor requires a food source that is difficult and disgusting to obtain, such as fresh human flesh or livers harvested from children. Going without her food source eventually causes the fomor to wither away and die. For each day that she does not ingest her particular food, she suffers 1 point of normal damage that cannot be healed until she has eaten her fill. 

- **Walking Bomb:** Pentex likes to keep track of its employees. With this taint complication, the company has taken its viligence a step further. Pentex, or another corrupt mastermind, has implanted a safeguard in the fomor—a bomb inside her head that only the company can detonate. When activated, the bomb explodes in the fomor’s head, immediately killing her. Additionally, anyone within three steps of her immediately takes 3 points of aggravated damage. While only the one who implanted the bomb has the ability to detonate or disarm the bomb from a distance, an explosives expert can attempt to disable the device in person. 

## Creating Fomori 

To create a fomor, use the same rules for creating bane Stock NPCs (see **The Wyrm: Banes** , page 643), with the following exceptions: 

- Fomori exist solely in the physical world. They cannot cross the Gauntlet into the Umbra. 

- When fomori purchase the Gifts specialization, they may choose Black Spiral Dancer or auspice affinity gifts. They cannot purchase gifts from any other tribes or breeds. 

- In addition to gifts, fomori gain taint powers and taint complications, detailed above. 

- Despite being partially human, fomori are immune to the effects of the Delirium. 

- Fomori gain Rage at the same rate as Garou and also benefit from the same regeneration rules, additional damage rules, and additional actions granted as they increase in Rage. Fomori are unable to frenzy, but can continue to progress on the Rage track, ignoring any effects of anger frenzy. See **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Rage** , page 376 for additional information on gaining Rage. 

- Fomori have the ability to mask their Wyrm-tainted nature by wrapping themselves in the aura of either the Wyld or the Weaver. This power does not completely mask the Wyrm Taint possessed by a fomor, but it does overpower all evidence of their corruption, save for their visible taint complications, which they are forced to hide through other means. Garou can still attempt to sense a fomor’s Wyrm Taint by using the gift Sense the Balance. 

- Fomori have health levels equal to twice their Stock NPC rating. 

## Fomor Bestiary 

## **Stock Fomor NPC**

Jaggling Fomor **Stock NPC:** Rating 4 

**Specializations:** Computer, Gifts: Galliard, Investigation, Occult **Willpower:** OOOO 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO OOO 

**Taint Powers:** Brain-Eating, Dark Sight, Mind Blast, Roar of the Wyrm 

**Taint Complications:** Derangement: Obsession, Social Pariah: Brain Eating, Special Diet: Children, Walking Bomb 

**Special:** Joannie Smith has a test pool of 8 in most challenges. In challenges involving Computer, Gifts, Investigation, or Occult, her test pool is 13. Joannie has access to the first four levels of Galliard-affinity gifts. 

Joannie Smith is a young college student who was taken over by a bane. She spends her time delving into the lore and myths surrounding the Garou, in hopes of finding additional means of fighting them. She is a remarkably intelligent researcher, and she avoids physical confrontation.

Gilda is an older woman who has been a fomor for many years. She is wily and wise, and understands a great deal more than she ever lets on. Gilda spends the bulk of her time working for the Wyrm as a security expert. She uses her skills to ensure Wyrm-managed facilities stay under tight lock and key. 

## Black Spiral Dancers 

_“In another life, I was part of the nation. I praised Gaia. I fought her wars. I saw my pack fall for her misguided beliefs. And then I found the Spiral, I found my place in the world. The other me died as she waltzed through the Labyrinth, and I was reborn.”_ 

— Angel Johannsen, Fallen Black Fury 

Once, there were more than thirteen tribes of Gaian Garou. The fearsome Picts, natives of northern Scotland, clashed with Vikings and Romans alike, protecting their lands from foreign incursion. Among them fought the White Howlers, a tribe of brutal and daring werewolves. But the White Howlers are no more—corrupted from within by the Wyrm, the tribe has transformed into a twisted, vile shadow of its former savage nobility. Calling themselves the Black Spiral Dancers, these werewolves devote themselves to directly serving the Wyrm and opposing the tribes of the Garou Nation. They practice dark rituals that defile their physical forms and twist their minds, causing deformation and insanity alike. 

Just as Gaian Garou maintain septs, the Black Spiral Dancers have their _hives_ . Hives often function in a similar fashion to Gaian septs; however, the leader of a hive tends to have absolute power, and they are willing to kill to keep their position. Hives are often found below ground, surrounded by maze-like tunnels intended to confuse and disorient anyone from the outside. Rather than a caern heart, hives possess a _pit_ . Some of these pits were Gaian caerns that have since been desecrated by the Wyrm, while others have been carefully carved out by the Black Spiral Dancers. They are filled with the toxic green light of balefires that burn incessantly. 

Black Spiral Dancers believe the Wyrm’s imprisonment led to severe multiple-personality disorder, splitting it into three heads: the Beast-of-War, the Eater-of-Souls, and the Defiler Wyrm. The Beast-of-War is the Wyrm of Calamity, a monster of endless rage that embraces the Wyrm’s love of chaos. The Eater-of-Souls is the Wyrm of Consumption, which fulfills the Wyrm’s desire to unify through devouring everything. The Eater-of-Souls once nearly manifested, and it was only stopped by the sacrifice of the Croatoan tribe. The Defiler Wyrm is the Wyrm of Corruption, focusing on the Wyrm’s desire to destroy from the inside. 

**
## **Black Spiral Dancers as an Antagonist Faction** 

Some Storytellers may wish to allow Black Spiral Dancers as player-characters in a nontraditional setting. For more information on how a Storyteller might build such a custom setting, see **Chapter Ten: Storytelling, Crossover Settings: Antagonist Factions** , page 467. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
## **The Dark Litany** 

The Black Spiral Dancers have their own laws that they must obey. These laws are sacred, and the Black Spiral Dancers are vicious about punishing their own when they stray too far from these twisted beliefs. Often, the punishment for failing to uphold the Dark Litany is death, preferably preceded by torture. 

- **Serve the Wyrm In All Its Forms:** Black Spiral Dancers must be willing to work with the forces of the Wyrm, such as Pentex and Sabbat vampires. Unity within the tribe is the only way they will succeed in their tasks. 

- **Beware the Territory of Another:** Black Spiral Dancers guard their territory jealously. While a Dancer may be allowed to travel through the tunnels of another hive, should she endanger another hive in any way, she will be tortured and devoured. 

- **Slay Those Who Will Not Join You:** Prisoners are often offered the chance to walk the Black Spiral and join the Wyrm, rather than face a lengthy torture and death. Prisoners who reject this dubious honor are destroyed. 

- **Respect All Those Who Serve the Wyrm:** Some Black Spiral Dancers view this edict as a forbiddance of the death of Garou or others who may be on the road towards eventual corruption; however, others merely view it as a demand to respect those who have already pledged their loyalty to the Wyrm. 

- **The Veil Shall Not Be Lifted:** The Black Spiral Dancers understand the necessity of prudence and discretion. When they act in secrecy, they can prevent Gaian Garou from discovering the nature of their plans. Although Black Spiral Dancers will work with Sabbat vampires from time to time, they vehemently disagree with Sabbat plans that call for any compromise of this secrecy. 

- **Do Not Suffer Thy People to Tend Thy Sickness:** Much as their Gaian brethren, the Black Spiral Dancers do not allow the weakness of one to compromise the strength of the many. Those too weak or too insane to do their jobs are sacrificed to the Wyrm. 

- **The Leader May Be Challenged at Any Time in Peace:** Any leader unable to defeat his own followers cannot be trusted to lead against their enemies. 

- **The Leader Shall Not Be Challenged in Time of War:** The agents of the Wyrm must remain united against their common foe. This distinction often refers to skirmishes and battles, as the Black Spiral Dancers are always at war. There are exceptions to this law. For example, if a non-Dancer leads in battle and does so poorly, she may be killed, or if a Dancer refuses to appoint a more fit leader if she is unable to lead, she may be destroyed. 

- **Ye Shall Take No Action That Causes a Pit to Be Violated:** Black Spiral Dancers hold their pits as sacred places, and only agents of the Wyrm are allowed within. Any Black Spiral Dancer who brings an outsider into a pit will be killed. 

**
## Organization 

There are no camps within the Black Spiral Dancers, as all members share the same fanatical worship of the Wyrm. 

## Appearance 

Twisted and hideous, the outward appearance of Black Spiral Dancers tends to mirror their inner depravity. Time spent 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

within a hive and near radioactive balefires tends to lead to deformities in all forms; however, this sign is most evident when Dancers are not in their breed forms. The Crinos form of a Black Spiral Dancer often has a huge jaw, with pointed and hairless bat-like ears. Scars are highly coveted among the tribe, and ritual scarification is a common practice during one’s Rite of Passage. 

Those Garou who have left Gaia to join the Black Spiral Dancers are often less malformed than their true-born brethren, a fact that many defectors come to resent. While a fallen Gaian Garou retains the traits of her tribe of birth, she often attempts to either hide these traits through scarification or self-mutilation, or she works to enhance them into a twisted mockery of her original birthright. 

While other tribes have seen a continuous decline in numbers, Black Spiral Dancers continue to grow. Their willingness—and eagerness—to create Metis, and their fanatical recruitment tactics have resulted in a tribe that is larger than all others within the Garou Nation. While the majority of the tribe is born to their fate, it is estimated that at least a third of the current Black Spiral Dancers have defected from the nation, for one reason or another. 

## Weakness 

The tribe’s eponymous initiation ritual requires each werewolf to confront various aspects of the Wyrm and oneself during a vision quest. The process invariably shatters the mind, inducing madness. All Black Spiral Dancers suffer from a permanent, incurable derangement. They may acquire and recover from other derangements, but they can never recover from their primary derangement. Black Spiral Dancers do not receive flaw XP for their primary derangement, but they do receive flaw XP for additional derangements as normal. Resetting a Black Spiral Dancer’s Derangement traits places her at 1, rather than 0. For more information on Derangement traits, see **Chapter Six: Merits and Flaws, Flaws, Derangements** , page 328. 

## Black Spiral Dancer Merits 

## **Cuckoo Nest (1 point merit)** 

You understand the necessity of secrecy and the skills required to infiltrate Gaian Garou. The downfall of the Wyrm’s enemies comes in many ways, but perhaps the most satisfaction is found when they crumble from the inside. You have learned how to hide the nature of your taint from others, allowing you to seamlessly integrate yourself into any situation. With this merit, you can spend 1 point of Willpower to hide your tainted nature for 24 hours. During that time, any Wyrm Taint you possess cannot be 

determined. Additionally, you have managed to create an identity that allows you to easily infiltrate the Garou Nation. This merit grants you an alias equivalent to 4 dots of the Alternate Identity background. (For more information, see **Chapter Four: Character Creation, Step Eight: Assign Initial Backgrounds, Background Descriptions, Alternate Identity** , page 193.) 

## **Danced the Spiral (2 point merit)** 

Although you were once part of a Gaian tribe of Garou, eventually you learned the error of your ways. Over time, you embraced your corruption, eschewing the beliefs of your family. Despite leaving behind the trappings of your past, you have maintained a small connection to your former tribe. With this merit, you can purchase gifts from your original tribe as affinity gifts in addition to the Black Spiral Dancer tribal affinity gifts. 

## **Claws of the Wyrm (3 point merit)** 

Your proximity to the Wyrm has given you a special gift: physical mutations that you wear as a badge of honor. Your physical mutation has manifested as a natural weapon that exists in all of your forms. While this ability means that you cannot walk among mundane humans comfortably, you don’t find that this detracts from your life. With this merit, you can apply one of the following weapon qualities to part of your body, making it a natural weapon: Accurate, Armor Piercing, Deadly, Fast, Trip, Impaling, or Reach. In addition, your natural weapon automatically has the Quick Draw quality (see **Chapter Fifteen: Influences and Equipment: Equipment, Melee Weapons** , page 706). 

## Black Spiral Dancer Gifts 

In addition to selecting appropriate affinity gifts from their auspice or breed, Black Spiral Dancers may also select from the following list of Black Spiral Dancer tribal gifts: 

## l Ears of the Bat 

_Your ears transform, growing larger, sharply pointed, hairless, and leathery. Spirits serving the Black Spiral Dancer totem, Bat, expand your sense of hearing, allowing you to use minute sounds and reverberations in lieu of eyesight as Bat’s children use echolocation to navigate through the darkest underground caves._ 

**Affinity:** Black Spiral Dancer 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and a simple action to activate Ears of the Bat for one hour. While this power is active, your ears become large and bat-like, allowing you to fully 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

sense your surroundings using nothing but your ability to hear, even in unnatural darkness or supernatural silence. Normally, characters who cannot see while in combat must use the Fighting Blind combat maneuver, but while this gift is active, you can fight without needing the Fighting Blind combat maneuver. 

## **Focus [Perception]** 

When using Ears of the Bat, you also gain the ability to recognize and track others by sound, similar to the Lupus form benefit, but using hearing instead of olfactory senses. 

## ll A Thousand Voices 

_You release the madness from the confines of your mind, allowing it to manifest in order to hinder your prey. Gibbering whispers, distant howls, and distasteful scents surround your victim, distracting your opponents with illusions generated from insanity itself._ 

## **Affinity:** Black Spiral Dancer 

**Test Pool:** Mental attribute + Subterfuge skill versus target’s Mental attribute + Willpower 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis, expend your standard action, and engage your target in an opposed challenge using your Mental attribute + Subterfuge skill versus your target’s Mental attribute + Willpower. If you succeed, you surround your target with disturbing illusions, causing sounds and scents that only she perceives. Your target suffers a -3 wild card penalty to all attack test pools for the next three turns, as she tries (and fails) to separate fantasy and reality. After three turns, your target becomes more capable of separating reality from illusion; for one hour, she suffers a -1 wild card penalty. Penalties from A Thousand Voices do not apply to defense test pools. 

Using this power on an individual with a derangement gives her 1 Derangement trait. Multiple uses of A Thousand Voices cannot be applied to the same target. 

## **Exceptional Success** 

Your victim suffers a -3 penalty to her attack test pool for five turns, and afterward suffers a -1 penalty to all attack test pools for the rest of the game session. In addition, using this power on an individual with a derangement gives her 2 Derangement traits, instead of 1. 

## **Focus [Intelligence]** 

While under the effect of A Thousand Voices, your target loses access to her Mental attribute focus. If she has more than one Mental focus, your target chooses which focus is temporarily removed. 

## lll Wyrm Hide 

_You draw on your connection to the Wyrm, allowing it to seep under your skin and ooze out through your pores. As it fills you with noxious power, your skin toughens into a bulbous, rubbery, pustule-covered hide, protecting you from bullets and blows alike._ 

## **Affinity:** Black Spiral Dancer 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and expend your simple action to toughen your skin into a natural, leathery armor for one hour. While Wyrm Hide is active, your skin has the Hardened and Ballistic armor qualities (see **Chapter Fifteen: Influences and Equipment, Equipment, Protective Gear** , page 709). The power is obvious and revolting to observers, and your appearance while using it may endanger the Veil. 

Mundane attacks that normally pierce armor, such as a gun firing Armor Piercing ammunition, do not pierce this armor, and do not negate the effects of this power. Supernatural powers that provide the Armor Piercing quality, such as Razor Claws, function normally. 

## **Focus [Perception]** 

Your Wyrm Hide protects you from Armor Piercing attacks from both mundane and supernatural sources. 

## llll Crawling Poison 

_You summon the Wyrm’s toxic nature, thickening your saliva and fouling your breath with noxious odor. Your fangs lengthen and become coated with a mottled black and brown plaque, reminiscent of disease and decay, though your bite is as strong as ever. Your fangs become capable of delivering a concentrated disease, which can kill humans and which inhibits other werewolves’ regenerative powers._ 

**Affinity:** Black Spiral Dancer 

## **System** 

Spend 1 point of Gnosis and expend your simple action to fill your mouth with a disgusting cocktail of supernatural sickness and toxins, as your teeth, saliva, and breath become 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

foul. For the next hour, when you successfully damage a target with a bite attack, she immediately becomes diseased. 

The disease infects the target’s blood and immune systems, inhibiting her ability to regenerate for the next five minutes. If a diseased target has the regeneration ability, the target’s current regeneration level is reduced by one, starting at the beginning of the next Everyman round. Her current amount of Rage is unaffected, as are other benefits she may receive from her current Rage level. Only her regeneration benefit functions as though it were one level less. 

For example, if your target is at Rage 7 and is receiving the benefit of Regeneration Level III, she still receives the benefit of Additional Damage II and can act on Rage rounds 1 and 2. However, at the start of her next Everyman round, she will only heal 2 points of normal damage from her Regeneration, instead of 3. 

Multiple uses of Crawling Poison do not reduce the target’s regeneration further, but do extend the duration of the original penalty by an additional five minutes. Crawling Poison cannot cause a target’s regeneration to fall below 0. 

Crawling Poison’s disease affects targets without the ability to regenerate as a virility 15 blood-targeting toxin. For such targets, Crawling Poison does not fade after its normal duration; instead, the disease persists until the toxin is resisted or treated, or the target runs out of blood, as per the standard rules for drugs and toxins. For more information on toxins, see **Chapter Seven: Core Systems, Health and Damage, Damage Types,  Drugs and Poisons** , page 350. 

## **Focus [Perception]** 

Your Crawling Poison increases in potency to a virility 20 blood-targeting toxin and reduces your target’s regeneration level by 2, instead of 1. 

## lllll Balefire 

_The hellish landscape of Malfeas is dotted with fiery pits of coalesced corruption. With a hurling gesture, you mentally force a small portion of the Wyrm’s energy to manifest as a ball of stinking, sickly green flame. Where it strikes your enemies, horrific burns result. Within moments, the burnt flesh twists and morphs into ghastly mutations, causing your victims’ own bodies to betray them in combat._ 

## **Affinity:** Black Spiral Dancer 

**Test Pool:** Mental attribute + Subterfuge skill versus the target’s Mental attribute + Willpower 

## **System** 

Making a hurling gesture, spend 1 point of Gnosis and expend your standard action to make an opposed challenge against a target within 20 steps of your location, using your Mental attribute + Subterfuge skill versus your target’s Mental attribute + Willpower. If you succeed, your target is struck by what appears to be a large ball of green-colored fire, which smells of sulfur and rotting meat. Your target takes 2 points of aggravated damage and gains 1 stage of Wyrm Taint; this degredation cannot cause a target to go into the fourth or fifth stages of Wyrm Taint. 

Further, the burns caused by the balefire mutate into small deformities, such as bulging blood-shot eyes, pus-filled nodules, patches of scales, or grotesque tentacles. These mutations cause your target’s body to move inefficiently for three turns, causing a -3 wild card penalty to all of your target’s attack test pools. The mutations dissolve after three turns, but the damage and Wyrm Taint persist as normal. 

Balefire can be used repeatedly against a target; however, subsequent successful Balefire attacks in the same combat only cause aggravated damage. This is an exception to the rule that prohibits a character from using a Mental power on the same target immediately after failing. 

A target can only receive Wyrm Taint from Balefire once per game session, regardless of whether or not multiple characters successfully use Balefire on that target. A target can receive Wyrm Taint from both Balefire and a Black Spiral Dancer’s inherent ability to cause Wyrm Taint to Garou within melee range. 

Subsequent successful uses of Balefire, regardless of whether or not they stem from the same source or multiple characters, increase the target’s mutation penalties for another three turns, but the penalty remains at -3. Balefire mutation penalties cannot be applied multiple times; successful subsequent uses only increase the duration, not the amount, of the penalty. 

## **Exceptional Success** 

Your Balefire causes your target’s Wyrm Taint to increase two stages, instead of the typical one-stage degredation. This degredation cannot cause a target to go into the fourth or fifth stages of Wyrm Taint. 

## **Focus [Intelligence]** 

Your Balefire causes 3 points of aggravated damage per successful use, instead of 2 points. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Creating a Black Spiral Dancer 

To create a Black Spiral Dancer, use the same rules for creating standard werewolf player-characters or Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- Even if a Black Spiral Dancer was once part of a Gaian tribe, she can no longer possess the merits Heroic Lineage or Legendary Lineage. Her service to the Wyrm has cut her off from benefiting from familial accomplishments. 

- A Black Spiral Dancer cannot retain any tribal merits from the tribe of her birth. They have new tribal merits that they can purchase, detailed above. 

- The flaws Spirit Notoriety and Spirit Infamy for a Black Spiral Dancer only effect corrupted spirits, as all Gaian spirits already have an aversion to the character. 

- Black Spiral Dancers gain access to the Black Spiral Dancer gift list and to the gifts from their native auspice; however, they cannot purchase any further gifts from any other tribal lists unless they possess a merit that permits them to do so. 

## Additional Bonuses and Drawbacks 

- When a Black Spiral Dancer succeeds in damaging a Gaian Garou or Fera within melee range, the target’s Wyrm Taint increases one stage. If the taint is not cleansed, it will begin to corrupt the target Garou. For more information, see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Wyrm Taint** , page 384. 

- The first time a corrupted werewolf is damaged by a Gaian werewolf or Fera in combat, the Black Spiral Dancer gains 1 additional point of Rage. This Rage gain may exceed the normal limits of gaining Rage; however, this effect can only occur once every five minutes. 

- A Black Spiral Dancer can enter the Umbra in a corrupted area with one turn of focus. Entering the Umbra in any other area requires three turns of focus. Black Spiral Dancers can only exit the Umbra in a heavily corrupted area, in a fallen caern, or in a hive. 

- Black Spiral Dancers are not natural, and do not have an innate spiritual connection to Gaia. They lack the ability to make werewolves through the Bite. (For more information, see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, The Bite** , page 365.)

Angel was once a Black Fury, but she chose to join the Black Spiral Dancers. Unlike many of her brethren, who are twisted and deformed, she prefers to take a subtler approach. She acts as a face for her tribe, drawing targets into her hive.

Felix was born and raised as a Black Spiral Dancer. He is a fierce combatant and mentally unstable, and his only goal in life is to destroy Gaian Garou and everything they stand for. 

Births the Apocalypse Lupus Black Spiral Dancer Theurge 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 4 **Specializations:** Dodge, Gifts: Theurge, Lore, Occult

**Special:** Births the Apocalypse has a test pool of 8 in most challenges. In challenges involving Dodge, Lore, or Occult, her test pool is 13. In addition, Births has access to access to the first four levels of Theurgeaffinity gifts. 

Births is a master of spirits, leveraging them as spies. She never strikes directly, but always acts sideways through spirits against her enemy’s family and allies. 

She was present to see the birth of Green Dragon at the Trinity Hive, and she has learned much from his tutelage, adding a subtle spin to it to keep from being straightforward. 

Births has been blessed by Green Dragon and gave birth to a child prophesied to play a major part in the Apocalypse. She has a long-standing rivalry with Charles Rex, for he assumed control of the hive she was destined to lead.

## **Sample Black Spiral Dancer NPC** 

## **Sample Black Spiral Dancer NPC** 

Charles Rex 

Homid Black Spiral Dancer Ahroun **Stock NPC:** Rating 4 

**Specializations:** Athletics, Brawl, Gifts: Ahroun, Intimidation 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOO 

**Special:** Rex has a test pool of 8 in most challenges. In challenges involving Athletics, Brawl, or Intimidation, his test pool is 13. In addition, Charles Rex has access to access to the first four levels of Ahroun-affinity gifts. 

Charles Rex believes he is the reincarnation of Charlemagne. He has the tactical expertise to back this belief up to any who might dismiss him. His command of tactics led him to seize control of a hive from under the nose of Births the Apocalypse when he returned from dancing the Spiral. He prefers more direct approaches to his problems, but he is not above using some level of subterfuge to conceal his direct involvement. 

Cadence Fairweather 

Metis Black Spiral Dancer Galliard **Stock NPC:** Rating 5 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Empathy, Gifts: Galliard, Lore, Performance 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO 

**Special:** Cadence has a test pool of 10 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Empathy, Lore, or Performance, her test pool is 15. In addition, Cadence has access to all Galliard-affinity gifts. 

Cadence is an Elder Black Spiral Dancer Galliard who has survived many years in a large hive. She is intelligent and wily, and has resisted many assaults on her life. Whether she is really that talented, or just that lucky, remains to be seen. 

Rex is the son of a Silver Fang Elder who is highly respected in his sept. As his son grew, he came to resent his father and the fame that the man boasted. The pressures of living up to his father’s expectations were too much for Rex, and he set out to find a different way. In looking to stand out, he found a place where he could make his mark.
