## **Sample Black Spiral Dancer NPC** 

Clay Farnsworth 

Lupus Black Spiral Dancer Philodox **Stock NPC:** Rating 4 

**Specializations:** Brawl, Empathy, Gifts: Philodox, Subterfuge **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOO 

**Special:** Clay has a test pool of 8 in most challenges. In challenges involving Brawl, Empathy, or Subterfuge, his test pool is 13. In addition, Clay has access to access to the first four levels of Philodox-affinity gifts. 

Clay was once a Shadow Lord, but he fell to the Wyrm after his pack was destroyed. He went into the hive alone to try to take his revenge, and ended up taking a trip through the Black Spiral instead. He is a quiet Garou, dedicated to his new cause. 

## Skin Dancers 

_“They told me I would never be Garou. I was nothing more than breeding stock. I watched my husband die at the hands of the Wyrm. There was nothing I could do, and nothing I could offer. The Garou took everything from me. So I fought back.”_ 

— Caleigh Anderson, Ahroun, Skin Dancer 

_Skin Dancers_ are kinfolk who have murdered and taken the skins of five Garou in order to take by force the shapeshifting ability they believe to be their birthright. By using a twisted ritual called the _Rite of Rebirth_ , they experience a mockery of the First Change. Some of these misguided kinfolk believe they are actually helping the Garou Nation, choosing to skin only Black Spiral Dancers, intending to serve Gaia 

and join the Garou in their war. They fail to realize that the overwhelming focus on their desire opens the door to the corruptive whisper of the Wyrm. 

Samuel Haight was the first kinfolk to discover the rite. After many years feeling as though he had been cheated of Gaia’s gift, he found the key to becoming a werewolf himself. The Rite of Rebirth is extremely unpleasant, and it takes a particularly depraved and angry kinfolk to enact it. Haight quickly rose to infamy as he traveled the globe, slaughtering Gaian Garou and Black Spiral Dancers alike. As he traveled, he recruited kinfolk who shared his views and bitter jealousy. He gathered followers and taught them the rite, and together they became the Skin Dancers. Haight and his followers believed they could create a new tribe: a tribe of werewolves who had proven their mettle by taking what they desired by force. 

The Age of Apocalypse has emboldened the Skin Dancers. As caerns fell and chaos reigned, these kin took advantage of their Garou brethren, murdering them and taking their hides whenever possible. Through the contacts they have carefully maintained within the Garou Nation, they have discovered the secrets about the halted progress of the Unborn, and the process of using the Bite. The Skin Dancers are threatened by these new anomalies, resentful of the relative ease through which Bitten transition into werewolves. 

Those who become Skin Dancers are dangerous enemies to Garou. They understand the intricacies of the Garou Nation, and they often have first-hand knowledge of the weaknesses suffered by their shapeshifting brethren. They know exactly how to strike their targets, and they are so desperate to take their place within the nation that they stop at nothing to complete their gruesome task. 

## Organization 

Skin Dancers understand that there is strength in numbers, and that their unity may be the only thing standing between them and annihilation. While they may not always agree on the best course of action, they still remain in solidarity with one another. Although Skin Dancers are not a true tribe, it is their ultimate goal to eventually gain sufficient numbers to rival other tribes. 

**
## **Skin Dancers as an Antagonist Faction** 

Some Storytellers may wish to allow Skin Dancers as player-characters in a non-traditional setting. For more information on how a Storyteller might build such a custom setting, see **Chapter Ten: Storytelling, Crossover Settings: Antagonist Factions** , page 467. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
## **The Rite of Rebirth** 

The Rite of Rebirth is a vile ritual that allows a kinfolk to become a werewolf. In order for her to enact this ritual, she must first kill and skin five Garou. Once she has murdered her victims, she completes the rite by stitching the skins together during an elaborate ritual, culminating in a painful and brutal First Change for the new Skin Dancer. All of the murdered Garou must be killed during the same phase of the moon in order for the ritual to succeed. 

A kinfolk who has undergone this rite always carries a trace of Wyrm Taint. The only way to escape this corruption is to find five willing Garou who give their knowing consent, freely allowing the kinfolk stitch their skins together—an outcome so unlikely that it is functionally impossible. 

**
## Appearance 

The majority of Skin Dancers carry with them Wyrm Taint, an echo of the corruption-laden ritual required to grant them their gifts. Skin Dancers can be found in all parts of the world. When a Skin Dancer is in any of her forms, she bears tell-tale stitch-like scars. In Crinos, Hispo, and Lupus form, her fur is particolored, showing distinct patches from each of the five Garou whose skins were used in the ritual. 

## Weakness 

Most Skin Dancers avoid Garou, unless they are actively hunting them. While they will interact with other Skin Dancers, they understand that the majority of Garou wish to see them dead, and they are not above striking first to ensure their continued survival. Due to this practice, Garou consider most Skin Dancers to be Ronin, with all of the drawbacks that come with that moniker. Very rarely, Skin Dancers have managed to gain acceptance into a tribe, and these werewolves will take on the weakness of their new tribe. 

A Storyteller may choose to create a Skin Dancer as a kinfolk who is on a quest to obtain Garou skins. In this case, the NPC should be created as a kinfolk character. 

## Additional Bonuses and Drawbacks 

- A Skin Dancer can purchase gifts with affinity to tribe she once claimed as kin, but she is not considered to be a member of that tribe. These tribal gifts are considered nonaffinity gifts and are purchased at the non-affinity XP costs. 

- Skin Dancers are considered Ronin werewolves, and they cannot gain Renown or rise in Rank (see **Chapter Nine: Social Systems: Renown** , page 417), unless they have the **Tribal Membership** merit, as detailed below. 

- Skin Dancers are not natural, and do not have an innate spiritual connection to Gaia. They lack the ability to trigger another’s First Change using the Bite. (For more information, see **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, The Bite** , page 365.) 

## Skin Dancer Merits 

## Creating a Skin Dancer 

To create a Skin Dancer, use the same rules for creating standard werewolf player-characters or Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- A Skin Dancer is always in stage two, or higher, of Wyrm Taint. 

- A Skin Dancer’s auspice is based on the phase of the moon during which she murdered her victims, all of whom must have been killed during the same moon phase. 

- A Skin Dancer cannot possess any of the following merits: Heroic Lineage, Legendary Lineage, or Tribal Affinity. 

## **Split Moon (1 point merit)** 

Throughout the years, Skin Dancers have discovered a means to change the Rite of Rebirth, allowing them to take advantage of more than one auspice. To benefit from this alteration, a Skin Dancer must murder six Garou instead of five: three during each phase of the moon they wish to replicate. As a Skin Dancer who completed this version of the rite, you are innately able to learn the gifts of two different auspices, both of which can be purchased at affinity cost. 

## **Skin Bonded (2 point merit)** 

One of the skins sacrificed in your Rite of Rebirth ceremony bonded with you, passing along a limited affinity for a different tribe. Choose a single level 1, 2, or 3 gift that is 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

normally restricted to another tribe. You can purchase that gift as an out-of-affinity gift. This purchase doesn’t require a teacher to learn. This is an exception to the rule preventing werewolves from learning out-of-affinity tribal gifts. 

## **Tribal Membership (3 point merit)** 

Although many Skin Dancers revel in their hated status, you understand that there is strength found among the Garou of your tribe. You have managed to hide the nature of your First Change and have found acceptance within your tribe. You are an official member of your tribe, with all of the benefits and drawbacks that this entails; however, you always run the risk of being discovered in your deception. Additionally, you are no longer considered Ronin, and you are able to earn Renown and rise in Rank. 

## Skin Dancer Gifts 

In addition to selecting appropriate affinity gifts from their auspice or breed, Skin Dancers can purchase gifts from their tribe of birth. There are rumors that some Skin Dancers are attempting to create gifts of their own, yet another step towards bringing the Skin Dancers into their own as a tribe.

Morgan was born and raised without ever knowing of his Garou heritage. He encountered a Skin Dancer as a young man, and the life of a powerful shapeshifter attracted him. Working alongside his new mentor, he embraced his transformation. His only interaction with the Garou Nation, besides to obtain his skins, has been to avoid them at every turn. 

**
## The Pentex Corporation 

Perhaps the most pervasive of the Wyrm’s tools is the global corporation _Pentex_ . Very few employees actually understand the breadth of what Pentex is doing, but this ignorance does not stop the megacorporation from continuing to grow and pollute the earth. Pentex was initially founded under the name Premium Oil by Jeremiah Lassater, who found himself under the sway of a powerful bane when drilling released 

## **Sample Skin Dancer NPC** 

Satya Eros 

Homid Skin Dancer Galliard 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 5 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Gifts: Galliard, Lore, Occult, Performance 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO **Wyrm Taint:** OO 

**Special:** Satya has a test pool of 10 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Lore, Occult, or Performance, her test pool is 15. In addition, Satya has access to all Galliard-affinity gifts. 

Satya travels throughout the nation, hiding her true self as best she can. Originally kin to Silent Striders, she continues interacting with her family, traveling and learning the tales of the other werewolves she encounters. She is relatively peaceful for a Skin Dancer, and she wishes to be left alone. 

the creature. In time, the corporation changed to its current iteration: a company run by vile creatures who are focused solely on achieving the spiritual, moral, and environmental bankruptcy of the planet. With thousands of employees at its disposal, Pentex has a never-ending supply of minions to unwittingly do its bidding. Additionally, Pentex has at its disposal any number of supernatural creatures, including banes, Black Spiral Dancers, fomori, and even vampires. 

Although Pentex is based in North America, its taint has spread throughout the globe, with subsidiaries found on every continent. Recent years have been kind of Pentex, and it has thrived in the wake of the Age of Apocalypse. From _Endron International_ —the largest oil producer in the world—to _Tellus Enterprises_ —a company dedicated to electronics—the Wyrm has found its way into nearly every home in the United States. 

Pentex is a persistent enemy, and one that the Garou Nation can never hope to truly eradicate. The growth of the company and its subsidiaries is unstoppable, and all the werewolves can do is hope they can stem the wave before the war turns against them. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Pentex Groups 

## **First Teams** 

_First Teams_ are groups of highly trained operatives that work in squads of six to ten mercenaries. They are well-equipped for any situation and are often comprised of fomori, psychics, Black Spiral Dancers, and neonate vampires. They are expertly trained for combat, espionage, and other esoteric specialties. They run special missions that might range from desecrating a Garou caern to recovering a missing scientist. 

## **Project Aenid** 

_Project Aenid_ focuses on recruiting and training for Pentex. It largely directs its attention towards finding psychics who can aid Pentex in its war against Gaia as well as finding thrillseekers and adventurers who would make suitable fomori. Oftentimes, these recruits are placed in troubleshooting or investigative positions at Pentex’s subsidiaries. 

## **Section 12** 

_Section 12_ is a group of researchers that focuses heavily on unraveling the mysteries behind lycanthropy. Its members look to find ways to cure the cause of the disease, and sometimes, they look for ways to incite the First Change. These scientists are diabolical and stop at nothing to find the answers they seek. 

## **Vampires** 

Pentex has forged a special relationship with vampires, offering them power and sustenance in exchange for their abilities. Pentex higher-ups are more than willing to turn a blind eye to employees eating the mail boy. Although there are sometimes differences in approach, Pentex has found its vampire allies to be an essential asset, as the company continues its spread throughout the world. Unsurprisingly, ambitious Sabbat vampires often find themselves working alongside the corporation. 

## **Fomori** 

Fomori have long been popular minions of Pentex. Through Project Aenid, Pentex has worked to intentionally breed fomori for certain traits and behaviors. These genetically engineered fomori are nearly perfect creatures, able to function well among humanity. They often hold highly placed positions on the corporation’s organizational charts. 

## Pentex Bestiary 

## **Sample Pentex NPC** 

Karl Richter 

Kinfolk – First Teams Operative 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 4 

**Specializations:** Athletics, Brawl, Dodge, Firearms 

**Health Levels:** OOOO 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO 

**Special:** Karl has a test pool of 8 in most challenges. In challenges involving Athletics, Brawl, Dodge, or Firearms, his test pool is 13. 

Karl is one of the foremost kinfolk among the members of the First Teams. He is a ruthless killer, willing to go to great lengths to take care of whatever task needs doing. Raised in a hive, Karl was hired by the First Teams as a young adult, and he has risen quickly through the ranks. He now commands a team of his own, and it is often dispatched to deal with human or kinfolk opponents. 

## **Black Spiral Dancers** 

Black Spiral Dancers may work closely with Pentex when their goals align. Pentex often disapproves of the Black Spiral Dancers’ violent urges, while the Dancers feel that they could win the war more quickly if they work directly against Gaia, rather than wading through the red tape that Pentex’s business requires. Despite their differences, Pentex calls on its allies among the Dancers when it needs a strong hand to handle a situation. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## **Sample Pentex NPC** 

Allan Michaels 

Vice President of Research, Development, Testing & Evaluation 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 8 

**Specializations:** Elder, Dodge, Empathy, Intimidation, Leadership, Melee, Science, Survival 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO OOO **Wyrm Taint:** OO 

**Special:** Allan has a test pool of 16 in most challenges. In challenges involving Dodge, Empathy, Intimidation, Leadership, Melee, Science, or Survival, his test pool is 21. 

Allan Michaels rose to prominence within Pentex as a young man. His ascension through its ranks was suspiscious, as he rapidly went from being an entry-level engineer to a manager within mere years. Rumors fly that scheming, back stabbing, and perhaps even murder assisted him in his ascent. When he achieved his current position of Vice President, he suddenly seemed quite content with his position within the company. Throughout the years, he has survived numerous assassination attempts instigated by his higher-ups. His position gives him access to all of the experimental technology and drugs that Pentex develops, which many attribute to his survival. 

Although he is a Vice President, he has never lost his taste for experimentation. Michaels remains one of the brightest, if depraved, minds at Pentex, using whatever subjects he has at hand on which to conduct his experiments. Human, supernatural, or undead—he doesn’t care what his subject’s nature is, provided that it can provide him with new innovations through which to serve the Wyrm or help him survive.

Alice is the Head of Human Resources department for Endron, recruiting and hiring suitable new candidates. Although she is pleasant and cheerful on the surface, she is vicious when it comes to her job. 

## Wyrm Creatures 

_Wyrm creatures_ come in two different types: those that are created and those that are corrupted into service. The majority of these entities were twisted by the foul poisons and toxins of the Wyrm, causing them to become servants unwillingly. Black Spiral Dancers and Maeljin Incarna tirelessly search out other creatures from the deepest dark to bind to into service, willingly or not. Just a few of the myriad servants of the Wyrm include the _Thunderwyrms_ , ancient serpentine beasts born of the Wyrm in the depths of the earth, which serve the Wyrm from the moment of their birth. Another example, _Skull Pigs_ are said to be the twisted and corrupted kinfolk of the once-proud _Grondor_ , wereboars that, before their extinction, were tasked by Gaia to cleanse the most dangerous elements within their own bodies. No matter how they came to be, born or corrupted, the sheer variety and number of servants the Wyrm can call to war means that its servants can take nearly any shape. 

While some of the creatures used by the Wyrm were once natural animals that were twisted and corrupted, the majority of them are monsters that have lurked in the dark for centuries, slipping out to prey on the unlucky. Some of 

these creatures are maintained within hives by Black Spiral Dancers, who feed them in exchange for their services, while others are unknown even to the other minions of the Wyrm. 

Although there are some Wyrm creatures that are wellknown to the Garou, it is impossible to know how many different creatures there truly are. Storytellers are encouraged to use the sample creatures below, or devise monsters from the depths of their own imaginations. 

## Wyrm Affinity 

Creatures that have completely surrendered their will to the Wyrm are considered to possess Wyrm Affinity. Gifts such as Sense the Balance ( **Chapter Five: Gifts: General Gifts, Level 1 Gifts, Sense the Balance** , page 267) reveal that creatures possessing this quality are associated with the Wyrm. Some creatures (such as **Blood Vines** , page 667) can sense fellow devotees of the Wyrm. 

## Creating a Wyrm Creature 

To create a Wyrm creature, use the same rules for creating bane Stock NPCs (see **Banes** , page 643), with the following exceptions: 

- When a Wyrm creature purchases the Gifts specialization, she may choose Black Spiral Dancer or auspice gifts. She cannot purchase gifts from any other tribes. 

- Wyrm Creatures gain Rage at the same rate as Garou. See **Chapter Eight: Dramatic Systems, Rage** , page 376. 

## Wyrm Creature Beastiary 

## Nexus Crawlers 

Deep in the Umbra exist the most-feared of the Wyrm’s minions: _Nexus Crawlers_ . Massive spider-creatures with the power to warp the fabric of the Umbra around them, they are possibly the most dangerous of the Wyrm’s many servants. More than mere monsters, Nexus Crawlers are intense forces of nature, with the ability to strike fear into the hearts of even the most stalwart Garou. 

Some Theurges believe that these nightmare monsters are not native to this reality, and that they have come from some other place. Although Nexus Crawlers have exhibited some level of sentience, their minds are alien and irrational. Often, even other minions of the Wyrm find themselves uncomfortable in the presence of these creatures, a fact that the Wyrm uses to its benefit. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

The very presence of a Nexus Crawler threatens to erode the fabric of reality. In their natural states, these creatures appear as a black shimmer in the air, bringing with them a warbling vibrato sound and a feeling of general unease. They have the ability to change their form, appearing in whatever shape strikes the most fear in their enemies. Nexus Crawlers are very often seen in the form of enormous spiders, with hardened carapaces that serve as armor. Their mandibles glisten with poison, and their many-faceted eyes are rumored to be able to see into many different realms all at the same time. Storytellers are encouraged to use the **Giant Monster** rules (page 613) for these NPCs. 

Nexus Crawlers have the ability to create chaos through the forms in which they manifest, although they have no comprehensible agenda. They are almost impossible to control; however, they can sometimes be lured to the site of desired destruction and mayhem. Potent Black Spiral Dancer Theurges have been able to force some of smallest, weakest Nexus Crawlers to obey them for short periods of time, allowing them to drop them like bombs into areas where they wish chaos and havoc to take hold. Whether used as a distraction or as a weapon, the use of a Nexus Crawler in this way is a double-edged sword. They are just as likely to attack their supposed allies or masters as the enemies they are directed towards. 

Perhaps the most feared ability of a Nexus Crawler is its power to warp reality. Creatures of raw entropy, they can change the nature of the world around them. Some of these mutations are minor, barely blurring the lines between what is real and what is not. Others have the power to harm those around them, such as when Nexus Crawlers change the air to poison gas. Nexus Crawlers are one of the examples given regarding the importance of closing Wyrm Tunnels and repairing Broken Lands. The thought of one of these creatures managing to slip through a tear in the Gauntlet and entering the physical world has kept many a Theurge from sleeping soundly at night.

Nexus Crawlers have the ability to warp reality. For each point of Gnosis it spends, a Nexus Crawler can affect some aspect of reality. For example, spending 1 point of Gnosis allows a Nexus Crawler to shift the color of the sky. Spending 5 points of Gnosis permits a Nexus Crawler to throw its victim into a terrifying alternate reality, complete with dangerous inhabitants.

## Blood Vines 

Although Garou once believed _Blood Vines_ had been completely eradicated, the Age of Apocalypse showed this was not the case. These semi-sentient predatory vines are similar in appearance to kudzu, save for the long, thin, 

flexible thorns on their vines. Blood Vines exist solely to devour and multiply, and if left unchecked, they have the ability to overrun a small town or suburb within days. 

Blood Vines infect their hosts by forcing themselves into a body and devouring it from the inside. Although the host shows no physical changes during this intrusion, those who know the victim realize that she is not quite herself, as the Blood Vines are only able to mimic human actions through the simplest memories of their hosts. Once the Vines have completely devoured their host, they burst forth from their victim, leaving behind nothing more than an empty shell and looking for their next meal. Once Vines begin devouring a victim, removing them is impossible without supernatural aid. 

While Blood Vines aren’t difficult to kill, the challenge comes in ensuring that none escape the purge. If even one Vine remains, the cycle will continue. Alone, a Blood Vine is a relatively weak opponent. However, Blood Vines rarely grow alone. They are swift-reproducing plants, capable of growing multiple Vines in a short period of time. Each plant has three to five Vines that must be completely destroyed in order to kill the plant. Failure to eliminate every piece will quickly see the plant restored within 24 hours. 

## **Sample Wyrm NPC** 

Blood Vines 

Wyrm Creature 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 2 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Brawl **Health Levels:** OO 

**Special:** Blood Vines have a test pool of 4 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness or Brawl, their test pool is 9. When a Blood Vine is destroyed, it releases a pheromone into the air that those with Wyrm Affinity can easily sense. Anyone with Wyrm Affinity within one mile of a destroyed Blood Vine is able to smell the pheromone and follow its general direction to its source. 

Blood Vines are often found in large groups, and they can be formidable foes when they attack en masse.

## **Sample Wyrm NPC** 

Blood Vines 

Wyrm Creature 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 2 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Brawl **Health Levels:** OO 

**Special:** Blood Vines have a test pool of 4 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness or Brawl, their test pool is 9. When a Blood Vine is destroyed, it releases a pheromone into the air that those with Wyrm Affinity can easily sense. Anyone with Wyrm Affinity within one mile of a destroyed Blood Vine is able to smell the pheromone and follow its general direction to its source. 

Blood Vines are often found in large groups, and they can be formidable foes when they attack en masse.

Blood Vines are often found in large groups, and they can be formidable foes when they attack en masse.

## Heart Eaters 

_Heart Eaters_ serve as information-gatherers and spies for the Wyrm. Normally they appear almost human in form, if such a shape were to be missing every inch of its skin and be more flexible than rubber. Heart Eaters’ horrifying forms are specifically crafted to fit within the flayed skin of their victims, allowing them to impersonate their prey. Devouring the hearts and minds of their targets grants a Heart Eater access to their most recent memories, and donning their victims’ skins makes the deception complete. Of course, the Wyrm does not mind if its servants spread misery and corruption in their wake during a mission. 

Heart Eaters are among the cruelest of the Wyrm’s creatures: minions that thrive on pure emotion and suffering. A Heart Eater often targets lovers or others who are in close relationships, choosing one to attack and devour. It adopts the personality of its victim as it flays its skin and wears it as a seamless costume. Once it has been disguised, a Heart Eater works to cause the most pain and suffering it can among those who loved the victim. Often, a Heart Eater leaves its victims’ loved ones alive, in hopes that their sense of betrayal and suffering grows into something worse. Fear, sorrow, and bitterness often spiral into rage, frustration, and devastation, making its secondary victims more vulnerable to corruption. 

Heart Eaters are cowards, preferring to deal in emotional trauma rather than physical harm. Their purpose is to corrupt from the inside, not to murder. While they frequently target humans, Garou and other supernatural creatures may also attract a Heart Eater’s attention, as these targets’ isolation from humanity makes the betrayal of a loved one that much more painful. This behavior also grants a Heart Eater the chance to learn as much as they can about possible enemies, including their strengths, weaknesses, and defenses. 

## **Sample Wyrm NPC** 

## Heart Eaters 

Wyrm Creature 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 5 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Empathy, Investigation, Performance, Subterfuge **Health Levels:** OOOOO 

**Fomori Taint Power:** Poison Tumors **Special:** Heart Eaters have a test pool of 10 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Empathy, Investigation, Performance, or Subterfuge, their test pool is 15. 

After making a successful Physical attack, a Heart Eater can poison her target using her Poison Tumors taint power. Additionally, Heart Eaters share the following ability: they can mask their Wyrm-tainted nature by wrapping themselves in the aura of either the Wyld or the Weaver. This power does not completely mask Wyrm Taint possessed by a Heart Eater, but it does overpower it. Garou may still attempt to sense a Heart Eater’s Wyrm Taint by using the gift Sense the Balance. 

Heart Eaters are often used specifically to target kinfolk. They take over the mates or children of Garou, both for the information they may already have about a sept and its defenses, as well as for the opportunity to wreak more suffering before abandoning a Garou to the misery of finding a dead family member, devoured by the Wyrm.

## Skull Pigs and Voodoo Pigs 

_Skull Pigs_ appear to be very large wild boars with thin, pale flesh covering their heads, lending them the appearance of having a head that is nothing more than a skull. It is rare to see a Skull Pig traveling alone, since these creatures tend to travel in packs of three to seven. They are carrion eaters, subsisting largely on flesh, garbage, and toxic waste. Their unique diet gives them their abilities. A Skull Pig that has managed to devour bones from a Wyrm-tainted creature gains an abnormal intelligence and the ability to learn gifts. These terrifying creatures are known as _Voodoo Pigs_ . 

The flesh of Skull Pigs is toxic, and it is dangerous to anyone who bites them. Anyone who successfully bites a Skull Pig becomes poisoned with a virility rating 15 toxin. For more information, see **Chapter Seven: Core Systems, Health and Damage, Damage Types, Drugs and Poisons** , page 350. These creatures rarely attack humans, and the Delirium allows them to pass unseen among them. While some Skull Pigs have little more than animal cunning, there are some who possess a much higher intelligence, and they adjust their attacks accordingly. 

Skull and Voodoo Pigs are most often found in places where they can scrounge the bones of the deceased and devour them for power. This behavior leads them to graveyards as well as other places where they will not be disturbed seeking their meals. These pigs do not care if the bones they desperately wish to devour are still moving when the eating begins. They are useful as both shock troops or as guardians for locations the Wyrm finds useful. Inherently lazy creatures, they do so love it when food comes to them.

Skull Pigs often travel in packs, making them difficult foes. The flesh of the Skull Pig is toxic and dangerous to anyone who bites them. Anyone who successfully bites a Skull Pig is poisoned with a virility rating 15 toxin. For more information, see **Chapter Seven: Core Systems, Health and Damage, Damage Types, Drugs and Poisons** , page 350.

## Thunderwyrms 

_Thunderwyrms_ are gigantic worm-like creatures that tunnel through the bowels of the earth, spreading destruction in their wake. They are used both as tools in unexpected ambush attacks on key sites and as beasts of burden to hollow out caverns and tunnels below ground for Black Spiral Dancer hives and pits. The Uktena believe that these monsters were first spawned from radioactive soil at nuclear test sites, though they have evolved since their first appearance. While these creatures used to only be seen in rural areas, they have expanded into urban and aquatic environments as well. Thunderwyrms resemble giant earthworms, with massive maws lined with rows of jagged teeth. Aquatic Thunderwyrms look like massive leeches and lurk along the bottom of bodies of water. 

Thunderwyrms pose a unique threat to the Garou, because they are massive creatures that are often very difficult to find and kill. Their penchant for burrowing deep within the earth makes it challenging to find their trail, until they surface and wreak havoc above ground. Garou have observed that Thunderwyrms are quite intelligent, and that they have learned to use their burrowing ability to create new hives and tunnels under the surface. Due to their massive size, Thunderwyrms have double the usual number of health levels. Storytellers are encouraged to use the **Giant Monster** rules (page 613) for these NPCs. 

The Wyrm has many uses for these gigantic beasts. They carve out large sections of earth as secret bases for other minions, and they can be used to destroy even the strongest defenses. The most intelligent of these creatures are even known to create massive labyrinthine lairs that connect to natural cave systems, sleeping with their jaws wide. Many an unfortunate soul has found herself exploring the depth of a Thunderwyrm’s stomach instead of the caves and caverns she expected. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115)

Thunderwyrms tend to focus their attacks in rural areas, and will often travel alone. 

## War Wolves 

_War Wolves_ are the result of twisted experiments undertaken on captured Garou, wolves, and dogs, deep within Pentex labs. These emaciated wolves have an air of desperate hunger eternally surrounding them. They are feared for their immunity to the Delirium, for their enormous fighting power on par with any Garou, and for their abominable monstrosity. They seem to be hunger itself, given form. 

War Wolves hunt in packs, always seeking the flesh of Garou or kinfolk to temporarily slake the worst of their pressing hunger. They look like mangy, starved wolves and dogs, and they exhibit a variety of coat colors. While they do carry Wyrm Taint and often have a sickly odor, many kinfolk fail to realize the danger of these creatures until it is too late. 

War Wolves are not Garou, but they do possess some similar qualities. They are vulnerable to silver, and they

are able to shift into Crinos form. When in Crinos form, they have the same bonuses as Garou in that form. Thankfully, however, they only have these two forms, and are unable to assume Homid, Glabro, or Hispo forms. They have a feral intelligence, sufficiently allowing them to be trained to act as guards and to follow simple instructions, but their strength lies in their ferocity and hunger, not their brains. 

War Wolves are used by the Wyrm when muscle is required, but when the full force of Black Spiral Dancers does not need to be brought to bear, or when other creatures may be too noticeable. They use their mangy Lupus forms to appear as wild dogs or neglected pets and accompany other agents on dangerous missions. At their leader’s command, they can instantly shift into their Crinos forms and rain destruction down upon whatever hapless victims have incurred the wrath of the Wyrm.

## Zmei 

Shazear. Goluko. Gregornous. Illyana. Rustarin. Trevero. Sharkula: these are the seven names of the dragons of legend. Their names alone inspire despair in the hearts of Gaia’s children, both because of the havoc they have wrought before and out of fear of the possibility that they may return to freedom. These mythical beasts—the _Zmei_ — have remained bound, hunted down, and restrained since the Silver Fang tribe struck ancient alliances with kinfolk born as mages. Too powerful to destroy entirely, the Zmei were bound and hidden away deep within the earth to be forgotten and lost for eternity. 

From 40 to 100 feet long, clad in armor-like scales and whose wingspans stretch wide, these monsters have fangs the size of daggers and talons that resemble swords. In addition to their immense physical prowess, they each possess their own unique powers, making them some of the most dangerous threats in the Wyrm’s arsenal against Gaia. 

Sharkula, one of the seven, is the only Zmei to have been destroyed. The others, save for Shazear, were captured, bound, and sealed beneath the most powerful hidden sacred sites, keeping them guarded and forever locked away from the world. Shazear alone managed to escape this fate, hidden from discovery by the Garou. He spent his time hiding within the broadening swirl of humanity in an effort to prevent hints of his existence from reaching modern loremasters. In the aftermath of the Siege of the Dark Brigade and the death of so many caerns, Garou Elders have begun to worry if the myriad creatures they bound beneath the earth may soon escape their seals. 

Since his disappearance, Shazear has taken command of an army of minions, and the likelihood of his siblings’ return grows greater. They are disasters merely looking for a crack through which to emerge, though these savage beasts possess a cunning intelligence that makes them far more dangerous than most Wyrm creatures. If they were to ever combine their strength and align their goals, Gaia only knows the havoc they could unleash. 

Zmei have the following attributes and abilities in common: 

- Zmei make take two forms: Homid and Great Serpent. In Great Serpent form, Zmei become dragons of ancient myth, with powerful wings capable of flight. They fly at three times normal movement rates. 

- Zmei regenerate 3 health levels every turn, on their Everyman initiative, as long as they remain alive. 

- Zmei in Great Serpent form can spend an entire turn and 10 points of Willpower to rend a hole in reality. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

**
## **Storyteller Note:** 

Zmei are incredibly potent and powerful, possibly even chronicle-ending threats, on par with Celestines. As such their use should be very carefully considered. These creatures’ capture was achived by whole tribes, causing high casualties. Storytellers should use the **Giant Monster** rules (page 613) for Zmei, and should only include them in their chronicles with the utmost forethought and care. 

**
This rift leads directly back to their home in the Deep Umbra realm of Malfeas, and the rift closes after 10 turns. 

• Zmei scales are formidable armor, infused with primal forces. They automatically reduce the damage of a successful Physical attack by 2 points of damage. 

## **Shazear** 

Zmei 

Dragon Wyrm Creature 

**Stock NPC:** Rating 25 **Monster Category:** Delta **Specializations:** All 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO **Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO **Health Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOOOOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO 

**Special:** Shazear has a test pool of 50 in all challenges. Due to their primal connection, Zmei have access to all gifts. 

**Shapeshifting:** Shazear has the ability to perfectly take on the shape of another creature, mimicking its natural form and abilities. His long life and history of infiltration has given him more than enough information to pass as one of even the most reclusive supernatural creatures, given enough planning. 

Shazear is completely immune to detection, mind-reading, or emotion-manipulating powers. This power is always active. 

Shazear is the General of the Dark Brigade. He is a master infiltrator and manipulator, and loves to personally sow chaos in the homes of his targets before sending his minions in to finish the job. He knows the location of Illyana’s resting place, and he has the power to awaken her, possibly learning where their other siblings are entombed as a result. However, he fears that if his siblings were to discover that he remained unbound without assisting them, their vengeance would be terrible. Therefore, he has saved the option to free Illyana and the other Zmei as a last resort. 

In serpent form, Shazear is the smallest of the Zmei, measuring only 40 feet long from tip to tail. His scales are a shimmering grey that reflects light like crystal.

## **Trevero** 

Zmei 

Dragon Wyrm Creature **Stock NPC:** Rating 25 **Monster Category:** Delta 

**Specializations:** All 

**Willpower:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO 

**Gnosis:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOOOOOOO OOOOO OOOOO OOOOO 

**Special:** Trevero has a test pool of 50 in all challenges. Due to their primal connection, Zmei have access to all gifts. 

**Poison Spray:** Trevero can expend 3 points of Gnosis to breathe forth a sizzling mist of poison gas at any number of targets, so long as they are within 15 feet of each other. If he succeeds in an opposed challenge using his test pool of 50 versus a target’s Physical attribute + Dodge skill, the gas inflicts 3 points of aggravated damage from poison on the target. In addition, for the next three turns, at the beginning of the afflicted target’s Everyman initiative, she takes 1 point of aggravated damage. 

The most brutal and bloodthirsty of the Zmei, Trevero fondly remembers bearing one of the Maeljin Incarna into battle during a war against the Garou long ago. He is a devout servant of the Beast-of-War aspect of the Wyrm, and he is glorious in bloodshed and battle. His release from captivity would result in an explosion of violence never before seen or even contemplated. 

Trevero is the most physically intimidating of the zmei, measuring a total of 70 feet long, including a 20-foot-tail, in his serpent form. Most impressive is his sheer muscle mass, unlike the sleeker but longer Zmei. His blood-red scales bear serrated edges, making his whole body a weapon of war. 

## Other Denizens of the World of Darkness 

The **World of Darkness** is filled with creatures big and small. Some of these factions work alongside the Garou; others work counter to them. For all of the knowledge that the Garou Nation possesses, there is still much that is unknown about these other creatures. Even those werewolves who have dedicated their existences to uncovering the secrets of these strangers lack all of the facts. Below, you will find information about the various creatures in the **World of Darkness.** 

## Hunters 

_Hunters_ are humans who have been touched by the supernatural world. Some have unique powers that they 

have honed for hunting the creatures that have hunted them now and in the past. As careful as the Garou are to maintain the Veil, there are humans who inevitably discover the truth of the world. The world of werewolves is often violent and messy, and not all messes can be cleaned up. Those humans who discover the truth sometimes feel that it is their duty to rid the world of the monsters they have seen in the darkness. Thus, a hunter may rise. 

Hunters come in different creeds and castes. Some are born to families with centuries of experience hunting the monsters of the wide world. Others awaken to the supernatural in horrific ways, learning how to best combat the creatures that hunt them battle by battle. Whether as individuals or in groups, hunters use any and all tools at their disposal in a fight for survival, and they strive to protect humanity from learning the awful truths that they have discovered. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

With the violence inherent in werewolves, those who choose to hunt Garou often band together to find safety in numbers. The Age of the Apocalypse and rising numbers of Lost Cubs have inspired even more hunters for Garou to try to avoid. More people have come to realize that monsters exist, and these humans are becoming increasingly dangerous to the Garou and their cause. 

Werewolves are divided on the issue of hunters. In some ways, they appreciate humanity’s attempts to assist Gaia’s chosen warriors in eradicating minions of the Wyrm, but they also condemn humans for failing to realize the Garou are their allies, not their enemies. Many werewolves feel hunters should be wiped out, purged as they were before. Most Garou, however, would rather stay out of their way and point them at the real enemies whenever possible. If hunters are going to exist, at least they can be useful. 

## The Inquisition 

_The Inquisition_ was founded in the 12[th] century. Initially formed as a Catholic secret society dedicated to ridding the world of werewolves and other supernatural creatures, it has gone through many iterations since its birth in Europe. Today, it is known as the _Society of Leopold_ , a group of scholars and researchers who know more about the supernatural than any other group in the world. Although the Society of Leopold claims to be purely interested in the research and cataloging of the supernatural, its membership is made up of many of the world’s most successful hunters, who are always looking for a new way to rid the world of dangerous monsters. 

Although the Garou have a just duty in the world, they are still mercilessly hunted by this society. In the 13[th] century, many Garou kin were taken and tortured, forced to give up the names and whereabouts of their werewolf brethren. While the society is not above stooping to these tactics today, the Garou Nation is wiser than it once was, and it takes more care protecting those kin who are well-informed about werewolves. 

Under the tutelage of the current head of the society, Ingrid Bauer, the Society of Leopold has thrived. Her “means to an end” attitude has resulted in a resurgence of some of the medieval techniques that were once thought to be anathema to the group. Torture is widely regarded as a viable tactic when it comes to dealing with the creatures of the night, and the society has opened multiple training camps throughout the world in order to hone hunters’ skills. There, Inquisitors are taught combat techniques and methods for neutralizing and destroying werewolves, along with other supernatural creatures they may encounter. 

The advent of the Age of Apocalypse has not gone unnoticed by the Society of Leopold. They are aware that werewolves have suffered, and they have taken advantage of this fact. Many Garou, uprooted from their defunct septs, have found themselves cornered and destroyed by hunters. Additionally, the Inquisitors are aware of Lost Cubs, and they have begun to develop methods to hunt down these wayward werewolves in hopes of putting them down before they can pose a threat to humanity. 

## **Inquisition Specializations** 

Inquisition hunters can specialize in one particular type of _numina_ , or magic wielded by mortals: True Faith. NPCs who specialize in True Faith gain 1 dot of that numina per their level of NPC rating. For example, a rating 3 Stock NPC who has True Faith as one of her specializations gains access to the first 3 dots of True Faith. True Faith does not count towards the Gift specialization limit (see **Stock NonPlayer Character Generation, NPC Specializations, Gift Specializations** , page 612). 

## **True Faith** 

Those who are truly faithful to their religion, even beyond the zealotry of belief, are occasionally granted special blessings and abilities through their deep connection to the divine. The numina of _True Faith_ is rated on a scale of 1 to 5. The powers afforded to the faithful depend on this rating, as described below: 

- Any character with True Faith can attempt to ward off werewolves by brandishing a holy symbol or similar sigil. To do so, the NPC must succeed in an opposed Social challenge against her opponent, using the NPC’s rating x2 (+5 if the NPC possesses the Intimidation specialization) versus the werewolf’s Social attribute + Willpower. If the werewolf loses this challenge, she must flee for the remainder of the scene. 

- •• Any character with a True Faith rating of 2 or more can immediately identify any Garou, Fera, kinfolk, or Skin Dancers. Upon encountering one of these character types, she is filled with a feeling of uneasiness that recognizably indicates the presence of a supernatural being. This feeling is amplified when she encounters any creature that possesses Wyrm Taint. 

- ••• A person with a True Faith rating of 3 or more is immune to all Mental and Social Garou gifts. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

- ••••  A person with a True Faith rating of 4 or more may cause a werewolf’s gifts to fail. Any Garou who is touched by the faithful or hears her pray, preach, or recite holy words must make a static Mental attribute + Willpower challenge against a difficulty rating of 20. If she fails, she is unable to use any of her gifts for one turn. Additionally, the werewolf cannot take actions targeting the individual with True Faith, although she may defend herself. If the Garou succeeds in this challenge, she may act normally. 

- •••••  A person who has achieved this level of True Faith is so sacred that her mere presence can temporarily cut off a werewolf’s connection to Gaia. Any Garou who is touched by the faithful must make a successful Mental attribute + Willpower challenge against a difficulty rating of 30. If she fails, she immediately reverts to her breed form. Additionally, her current Rage falls to 1 point, and any gifts that have been activated by her or are currently active on her immediately end. Finally, she increases 1 stage of Harano until her connection to Gaia has been restored. This loss of connection persists for one hour or until the Garou has visited a caern and regained at least 1 point of Gnosis, whichever is longer. 

Remember that these ratings are guidelines; Storytellers may amend them as required to fit the tone of the Stock NPC’s faith or to add drama to a chronicle. 

Note that a character with True Faith cannot receive the Bite or ever go through the First Change. In addition, someone with True Faith can pray for an instance of direct divine intervention. On rare and inspiring occasions, the deity answers such a prayer with a miracle. Such miracles should be dramatic and extremely rare, never more than one per story or a few per chronicle. Prayers that are selfless or seek to aid others have the best chance of being answered. Miracles should be specifically tailored to each unique situation. 

## The Arcanum 

In the 19[th] century, an elite group of occult scholars founded a human society dedicated to supernatural research and academics. _The Arcanum_ , as the group came to be known, first arose from two warring groups in Paris in the late 1800s. Harsh words and mystical in-fighting drove many of their greatest away. These dissidents later united to form the Arcanum. 

Although the Arcanum is relatively young as an organization, this group thrives in the modern age. It is believed that the 

inner circle of the Arcanum, known as the White Monks, first learned of the Garou from members of the Inquisition. Despite this knowledge, the Arcanum has never been officially connected to the Inquisition, and its members do not actively hunt supernatural creatures. The Arcanum has dedicated itself to study, not destruction. 

The Arcanum currently operates five known foundation houses, and its members are recruited from among the top of intellectual society. Despite the fact that the Arcanum prefers to focus on observation rather than action, some Arcanists possess significant power of their own, and the order itself has access to great stores of arcane knowledge. Potential members of the Arcanum are carefully screened, as security is of utmost importance to its existing members. Focused and driven, this group cast their eye towards recovering the lost lore of the past to battle evil in the present. For many years, it believed that werewolves had died out entirely, although the upheaval of the Age of Apocalypse has given them insight into the true nature of the battles Gaia’s children fight. 

The 1910 decimation of the Boston foundation house resulted in increased activity within the Arcanum. Prior to that event, the organization guarded its findings fiercely, refusing to share its knowledge even with other hunters who lurked in the shadows. Today, the Arcanum remains on the outside of active conflict with the supernatural communities; however, they are willing and sometimes eager to share their information with more militant hunters. 

## **Arcanum Specializations** 

Many Arcanum hunters have access to _hedge magic_ , a rudimentary, elementary type of sorcery available to certain types of scholars. To simulate hedge magic, Arcanum hunters can purchase can purchase gift specializations, as though they were partially supernatural creatures (see **Stock Non-Player Character Generation, NPC Specializations, Gift Specializations** , page 612). 

Hedge magicians utilize a resource called _mana_ . Mana is intrinsic to hedge magicians, and it is spent like Gnosis to power their magic. NPCs with hedge magic have 5 points of mana and can spend 1 point of this resource per turn. An NPC can regain 1 point of spent mana by meditating for one hour. 

## Project Twilight 

Perhaps one of the greatest threats to the Garou Nation, outside of the Wyrm itself, is _Project Twilight_ . This elite group of operatives works within the United States government, finding its members in positions at all levels of government. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

From state troopers to NSA operatives, these agents have access to all of the power and monetary backing that the U.S. government has at its disposal. 

Project Twilight devotes the bulk of its time to investigating supernatural incidents, using their considerable resources to cover up those incidents, and hunting down the ones responsible. Their authority has only swelled in recent years, as laws stealthily passed by Congress have given them unprecedented authorization to rifle through e-mails, licenses, and other private information about citizens and businesses. While Project Twilight does not have the significant occult resources of the Inquisition or the historical perspective and lore of the Arcanum, it does possess a number of parapsychologists researching the potential of _psychic numina_ . Some agents are even trained to utilize such abilities. 

Project Twilight has access to a bevy of research facilities scattered across the country. In these facilities, operatives research and study the supernatural creatures that they have captured. Here, they have developed a serum that allows Project Twilight operatives to fight off the effects of Delirium. There are rumors that these scientists are nearing a breakthrough in the cure for lycanthropy. 

## **Project Twilight Specializations** 

Many Project Twilight hunters have access to psychic numina. To simulate psychic numina, Project Twilight hunters can purchase gift specializations, as though they were partially supernatural creatures (see **Stock NonPlayer Character Generation, NPC Specializations, Gift Specializations** , page 612). 

Psychics utilize a resource called _focus_ . Focus is intrinsic to a psychic, and it is spent like Gnosis to power magic. NPCs with psychic numina have 5 points of focus and can spend 1 point of this resource per turn. An NPC can regain 1 point of spent focus by meditating for one hour.

Special Agent Nathan Linden joined the FBI after college. He was attacked and turned into a vampire ghoul shortly after joining the Bureau, and he discovered what truly lurked in the darkness. After his regnant was destroyed, he was sought out by other members of Project Twilight and was brought into their fold. He has joined an elite team of hunters who search for rogue Garou.

Father Jonas joined the Catholic church as a young man, and is incredibly devout in his faith. He joined the Society of Leopold after witnessing the murder of several members of his flock at the hands of a Black Spiral Dancer. Since that time, he has devoted his life to uncovering and destroying the things that lurk in the darkness.

Maritza is a bright young woman who was recruited into the Arcanum while she was still in high school. She spent a great deal of her time working with her foundation house, specializing in uncovering ancient lore related to the Garou Nation.

Sister Mary-Elise is the brawn to Father Jonas’ brain, and she spends her time working alongside him. While he is the researcher, she is the one who takes down the creatures they encounter. 

Mind’s EyE ThEaTrE: WErEWolf ThE apocalypsE 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

## Vampires 

Frozen on the boundary between life and death, _vampires_ are embodiments of the tainted nature of the Wyrm mixed with the static calcification of the Weaver. They do not eat, do not breathe, and their hearts do not beat unless they will them to do so in order to fool the unwary into their vile clutches. Some vampires vainly struggle to retain what little is left of the human within them, while others descend fully into the monstrosity that their natures call them to embrace. For ages, Garou and vampire, life and death given form, have slaughtered each other whenever they could, adversaries to the end. 

For their part, the ancient and mysterious vampires have long found themselves locked in competition with Garou. While the undead prefer their courtly intrigue, their machinations have clashed with lupine interests on more than one occasion. In particular, the vampires’ treatment of humanity often leaves a bad taste in the mouths of shapeshifters. The relationship between the Garou and the vampires is strained at best, although both groups have always avoided outright war. 

Once, the Garou believed all vampires were of the Wyrm. As such, the undead were thought to be just another irredeemable aspect of the nemesis of Gaia. Recent nights have shown that while these creatures do carry an ever- 

present air of Wyrm Taint, they also carry the mark of the Weaver, as they are locked forever in stasis, stuck at the moment of their mortal demise. In the desolate lands of Russia, Garou have reported that a faction of Clan Gangrel has been indispensable to the werewolves’ efforts. At the dawning of the Age of Apocalypse, these vampires came to the aid of Garou as they defended one of the last remaining great caerns. It was only through their assistance that the caern remains undefeated. The alliance offered by these vampires has not been forgotten by the Garou Nation. 

Just as Garou have reason to distrust vampires, the undead also find themselves loathe to place their lives within reach of lupine claws. With their short tempers and terrible rage, most werewolves can easily destroy an unsuspecting vampire. Yet despite their differences and misgivings, some alliances have been forged between vampires and Garou. Even amid the political pressures of the Concordat of Stars, an alliance between Ratkin, Bone Gnawers, and Nosferatu continues to thrive. 

## Creating Vampire NPCs 

To create a vampire, use the same rules for creating standard werewolf player-characters or Stock NPCs, with the following exceptions: 

- Vampires on the Path of Humanity are considered to always be in the first stage of Wyrm Taint. Vampires 

**
## **Vampire Blood** 

The blood of vampires is incredibly potent and addictive, but it carries with it heavy side effects. For the next 24 hours after a Garou drinks a point of vampire Blood, she begins each combat with 1 additional point of Rage, cumulative with any other bonuses she may possess. 

However, her Wyrm Taint also increases by one stage. Unlike most sources of Wyrm Taint, the taint from drinking Blood fades on its own, provided the Garou is not exposed to another source of taint. After two weeks or one game session, whichever is longer, Wyrm Taint gained from drinking the Blood of a vampire fades away. It cannot be removed sooner by other means. 

If a Garou drinks the Blood of a vampire who possesses a Path of Enlightenment merit, the Wyrm Taint gained from this act does not fade, and it must be removed after two weeks or one game session has passed. Usual cleansing methods, such as the Circle of Gaia’s Cleansing work normally to remove the taint after the time period has elapsed. As an exception to this rule, drinking Blood from vampires on the Path of the Feral Heart is treated as though the vampire did not possess a Path of Enlightment merit. 

Garou and Fera are not immune to the blood bond. If a shapeshifter drinks from the same vampire on three different occasions, she becomes emotionally bound to the vampire’s will and is in her mental thrall. For more information, see **Mind’s Eye Theatre: Vampire The Masquerade, Chapter Seven: Dramatic Systems, Blood Bond** , page 295. 

Chapter Fourteen: allies and a ntagonists 

Alona McCreary (Order #15311115) 

with a Path of Enlightenment merit are considered to have Wyrm affinity and to be creatures of the Wyrm, with the exception of vampires on the Path of the Feral Heart. 

- Vampires have a resource called _Blood_ and can spend it as a Garou spends Gnosis. A vampire has a Blood pool of 10 and can spend 1 point of Blood to heal a point of normal damage. 

- Vampires take aggravated damage from flaming weapons and incendiary ammunition. When exposed to sunlight, they take between 1 and 5 points of aggravated damage per turn at the Storyteller’s discretion. 

- Vampires are always considered to have a Rage rating equal to their NPC level. This Rage rating doesn’t increase or decrease, and the NPC suffers none of the ill effects of having high Rage. 

- A vampire may choose to select the Rage specialization. Doing so increases her effective Rage by 3 points. Vampires do not suffer any of the ill effects of having high Rage. 

- A vampire may select the Gifts specialization (see **Stock Non-Player Character Generation, NPC Specializations, Gift Specializations** , page 612). 

For detailed information and crossover mechanics using rules from **Mind’s Eye Theatre: Vampire The Masquerade** , please see **Chapter Ten: Storytelling, Crossover Settings: Antagonist Factions, Vampire Crossover Chornicles** , page 467. 

Sample Vampire Stock NPCs 

## **Sample Vampire Elder NPC** 

## Cosmus 

Tzimisce Sabbat Vampire – Pack Ductus **Stock NPC:** Rating 8 

**Specializations:** Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Elder, Gifts: Ragabash, Gifts: Theurge, Investigation, Occult 

**Health Levels:** OOOOO OOO 

**Blood:** OOOOO OOOOO 

## **Rage:** OOOOO OOO 

**Special:** Cosmus has a test pool of 16 in most challenges. In challenges involving Awareness, Brawl, Dodge, Investigation, or Occult, his test pool is 21. In addition, Cosmus has access to all of the Theurge- and Ragabash-affinity gifts. Cosmus is the Ductus of a large pack that travels the United States, searching for suitable talents to Embrace. The pack’s members are incredibly picky about the people they choose to gift with their blood, and they look for those who have strong personalities and mental proclivities: those who would make fine candidates for the Sabbat. The pack often wipes their recruits’ memories to ensure their loyalties. 

Although Cosmus lacks his long-term memories, he is an incredible asset to the Sabbat, with a strong tactical mind and deep knowledge of the sect. Using this knowledge and his innate strength, Cosmus and his pack look to reshape the leylines of the United States in order to bolster the strength of the Tzimisce and the Sabbat as a whole. 

Cosmus has no love for Gaian Garou, and he has worked alongside Pentex on more than one occasion. His Embrace has lent him a personality similar to that of a feudal lord, and he views humans as his serfs. This mindset has helped further the goals and desires of Pentex.
